2dtransform_path.description:
Transforms Paths using a combination of position offset and transformation matrix.

Note: See the Paths Format Specifications page in Designer's documentation to learn about the method used to encode Paths.

paths.description:
Contains a list of Paths which describe lists of segments, encoded in a color image.

Connect this input to the result of a Mask to Paths or to another Path-processing node.

The transformed Paths. You can use the Preview Paths node to visualize the result of the transformation.

The result can be processed using another Paths-processing node, or sent to a Paths to Spline node to further process it as Splines.

matrix22.description:
The transformation matrix applied to the splines.

Three modes of editing the matrix parameters are available:

  • Transformation gizmo: tweak the handles of the gizmo displayed in the 2D View when the Spline 2D Transform node is selected;
  • Rotation/Stretch: Individually control the rotation and stretching of the splines. Note that values are always applied relatively to the current transformation. E.g., applying 50% width twice results in a 25% width;
  • Matrix values: Click the Edit Matrix Values button to input the raw numerical values of the matrix directly.

offset.description: Applies a position offset to the splines in X (horizontal) and Y (vertical).

3d_linear_gradient.description:
Generates a 3D linear gradient based on a position map.
This node is meant to be used with the GPU Engine only (d3d / ogl).

3d_perlin_noise.description:
Generates a 3D Perlin noise based on the Position Map input.

Note

This noise is meant to be used with the GPU engine only (i.e., Direct3D or OpenGL). Go to Tools > Switch engine... or press the F9 key to select the desired engine.

position.description: The position map which should be used to map the 3D Perlin noise.

output.description: The output image.

absolute.description: Uses absolute values in the 3D Perlin noise. This effectively inverts the value distribution for values below 0.5.

baseline.description: Applies an offset to the baseline luminance value for the 3D Perlin noise value distribution.

contrast.description: Adjusts the contrast of the 3D Perlin noise.

distortion_intensity.description: Controls the intensity of a warping effect applied on the 3D Perlin noise.

distortion_scale_multiplier.description: Controls the scale of the deforming pattern used in the warping effect controlled by the Distortion Intensity.

enable_tiling.description: Adjusts the 3D Perlin noise so its resulting pattern repeats in the X, Y and Z axes.

invert.description: Inverts the output image.

offset.description: Applies an offset to the position of the 3D Perlin noise in the X, Y and Z axes.

scale.description: Controls the scale of the 3D Perlin noise.

size.description: Controls the size of the 3D Perlin noise in the X, Y and Z axes. Non-uniform values result in a stretching or squashing effect.

3d_perlin_noise_fractal.description:
Generates a fractal 3D Perlin noise based on the Position Map input.

Note

This noise is meant to be used with the GPU engine only (i.e., Direct3D or OpenGL). Go to Tools > Switch engine... or press the F9 key to select the desired engine.

position.description: The position map which should be used to map the fractal 3D Perlin noise.

output.description: The output image.

absolute.description: Uses absolute values in the 3D Perlin noise. This effectively inverts the value distribution for values below 0.5.

baseline.description: Applies an offset to the baseline luminance value for the 3D Perlin noise value distribution.

contrast.description: Adjusts the contrast of the 3D Perlin noise.

distortion_intensity.description: Controls the intensity of a warping effect applied on the fractal 3D Perlin noise.

distortion_scale_multiplier.description: Controls the scale of the deforming pattern used in the warping effect controlled by the Distortion Intensity.

enable_tiling.description: Adjusts the 3D Perlin noise so its resulting pattern repeats in the X, Y and Z axes.

global_opacity.description: Controls the range of the fractal 3D Perlin noise values around the Baseline value.

invert.description: Inverts the output image.

lacunarity.description:
Controls how the applied fractal pattern fills space.

A higher value results in less gaps in the pattern and a denser noise.

max_level.description:
The maximum level of of repetition used in the fractal pattern.

A wider minimum/maximum range results in a richer pattern with variation on more frequency ranges.

min_level.description:
The minimum level of of repetition used in the fractal pattern.

A wider minimum/maximum range results in a richer pattern with variation on more frequency ranges.

offset.description: Applies an offset to the position of the fractal 3D Perlin noise in the X, Y and Z axes.

roughness.description:
Controls the balance between low and high levels of repetition in the fractal pattern.

Note

A value of 0 results in an output which is not in line with other low values following it. This is expected.

scale.description: Controls the scale of the fractal 3D Perlin noise.

size.description: Controls the size of the fractal 3D Perlin noise in the X, Y and Z axes. Non-uniform values result in a stretching or squashing effect.

3d_planar_projection.description: Project a texture on a mesh using its position map and its normal map. The projection is orthogonal, and several options allow to control some blending of the result according to the mesh geometry.

texture.description: Texture to be projected on the mesh

wsnormal.description: World Space Normal of the mesh

depth_fade.description: At 1, fade linearly the opacity of the texture from the central point to maxDepth. At 0.5, start fading halfway. Disable at 0.

max_depth.description: Maximum distance (in the normal direction) until which apply the texture on the mesh.

norma_fade.description: At 1, use the cosine rule to fade the effect of the texture depending on the orientationn of the mesh surface. Disabled at 0.

normal_threshold.description: At 0, cut the texture at the terminator of the mesh. At 1, cut everything. At -1, cut nothing.

projection_input.description: Use overriden position and overriden normal to project the input texture.

scale.description: Global scale of the projected texture

size.description: Component-wise scale of the projected texture

target_normal.description: Direction used to project the texture.

target_position.description: Position of the center of the projected texture in 3D space.

target_uv_position.description: UV position of the reference point on the mesh. The reference point gives the direction of the projection (using the normal at this point), and the center of the projection.

3d_planar_projection_color.description: Project a texture on a mesh using its position map and its normal map. The projection is orthogonal, and several options allow to control some blending of the result according to the mesh geometry.

texture.description: Texture to be projected on the mesh

wsnormal.description: World Space Normal of the mesh

depth_fade.description: At 1, fade linearly the opacity of the texture from the central point to maxDepth. At 0.5, start fading halfway. Disable at 0.

max_depth.description: Maximum distance (in the normal direction) until which apply the texture on the mesh.

norma_fade.description: At 1, use the cosine rule to fade the effect of the texture depending on the orientationn of the mesh surface. Disabled at 0.

normal_threshold.description: At 0, cut the texture at the terminator of the mesh. At 1, cut everything. At -1, cut nothing.

projection_input.description: Use overriden position and overriden normal to project the input texture.

scale.description: Global scale of the projected texture

size.description: Component-wise scale of the projected texture

target_normal.description: Direction used to project the texture.

target_position.description: Position of the center of the projected texture in 3D space.

target_uv_position.description: UV position of the reference point on the mesh. The reference point gives the direction of the projection (using the normal at this point), and the center of the projection.

3d_ridged_noise_fractal.description:
Generates a fractal 3D Ridged noise based on the Position Map input.

Note

This noise is meant to be used with the GPU engine only (i.e., Direct3D or OpenGL). Go to Tools > Switch engine... or press the F9 key to select the desired engine.

position.description: The position map which should be used to map the fractal 3D Ridged noise.

output.description: The output image.

baseline.description: Applies an offset to the baseline luminance value for the 3D Ridged noise value distribution.

contrast.description: Adjusts the contrast of the 3D Perlin noise.

distortion_intensity.description: Controls the intensity of a warping effect applied on the fractal 3D Ridged noise.

distortion_scale_multiplier.description: Controls the scale of the deforming pattern used in the warping effect controlled by the Distortion Intensity.

enable_tiling.description: Adjusts the 3D Perlin noise so its resulting pattern repeats in the X, Y and Z axes.

global_opacity.description: Controls the range of the fractal 3D Ridged noise values around the Baseline value.

invert.description: Inverts the output image.

lacunarity.description:
Controls how the applied fractal pattern fills space.

A higher value results in less gaps in the pattern and a denser noise.

max_level.description:
The maximum level of of repetition used in the fractal pattern.

A wider minimum/maximum range results in a richer pattern with variation on more frequency ranges.

min_level.description:
The minimum level of of repetition used in the fractal pattern.

A wider minimum/maximum range results in a richer pattern with variation on more frequency ranges.

offset.description: Applies an offset to the position of the fractal 3D Ridged noise in the X, Y and Z axes.

roughness.description:
Controls the balance between low and high levels of repetition in the fractal pattern.

Note

A value of 0 results in an output which is not in line with other low values following it. This is expected.

scale.description: Controls the scale of the fractal 3D Ridged noise.

size.description: Controls the size of the fractal 3D Ridged noise in the X, Y and Z axes. Non-uniform values result in a stretching or squashing effect.

3d_simplex_noise.description:
Generates a 3D Simplex noise based on a position map.
This noise is meant to be used with the GPU Engine only (d3d / ogl).

3d_texture_offset_color.description: Applies an offset transformation in the X, Y and Z axes on an object described by the 3D texture connected to the Input.

input.description:
The color 3D texture describing a 3D object.

The object is commonly described in a unit cube.

output.description: The output image.

offset.description: The amount of offset in world space applied on the object described by the 3D texture connected to the Input.

3d_texture_offset_grayscale.description: Applies an offset transformation in the X, Y and Z axes on an object described by the 3D texture connected to the Input.

input.description:
The grayscale 3D texture describing a 3D object.

The object is commonly described in a unit cube.

output.description: The output image.

offset.description: The amount of offset in world space applied on the object described by the 3D texture connected to the Input.

3d_texture_position.description:
Generates the position slices of a unit cube.

This can be used to bake 3D noises or functions as a 3D texture atlas.

output.description: The output image.

3d_texture_sdf.description: Generates the signed distance field of a shape from the Input's 3D texture mask representing the slices of the shape's volume.

mask_input.description: The 3D texture mask representing the slices of a shape's volume.

output.description: The output image representing the slices of the shape's signed distance field.

The type of distance field which should be output:

- Distance Field: outputs a distance field describing the distances outside the shape.
- Signed Distance Field: outputs a distance field describing the distances outside (positive) and inside (negative) the shape.

threshold.description: When the shape volume is described by a fading gradient, sets the gradient value at which the surface of the shape is detected.

3d_texture_surface_render.description:
Renders the surface of a shape described by a 3D texture, using its corresponding distance field from the 3D Distance Field image input. The surface is represented within the bounds of a unit cube.
The lighting is computed using the Environment input image mapped to an infinite sphere.

Note

The distance field is expected to be a 4096x4096 texture describing the shape with a 16x16 grid of 256 slices.
You may use the 3D Texture SDF node to compute the distance field for a 3D texture of 256 slices.

3d_distance_field.description:
The 4096x4096 image representing the 256 slices of a shape's distance field, arranged in a 16x16 grid.

You may use the 3D Texture SDF node to compute the distance field for a 3D texture of 256 slices.

environment.description:
The image representing the environment which should be mapped to an infinite sphere in the render, and used for computing the lighting.

The image is also used to render the scene background when the Background Mode parameter is set to Ambient or Environment.

output.description: The output image.

albedo.description: The albedo color of the shape's surface.

background_color.description: The color used to uniformly fill the background of the rendered scene.

background_mode.description:
The method of representing the background of the rendered scene:

- Ground Irradiance: The computed irradiance of the ground plane
- Ambient: The ambient color of the Environment image input mapped to an infinite sphere, which is akin to a strongly blurred version of the image
- Uniform Color: Uniformly fill the background with a specified color
- Environment: The Environment image input mapped to an infinite sphere

camera_distance.description: The distance from the camera to the shape.

camera_fov.description: The field of view of the camera in degrees.

camera_uv_position.description:
The position of the camera around the shape.

When the node is selected, you may use the position gizmo in the 2D View to orbit the camera.

enable_ground_plane.description: When True, renders a ground plane. The unit cube enclosing the shape rests on this plane.

ground_plane_size.description: Adjusts the size of the ground plane.

infinite_plane.description: Sets the ground plane to extend infinitely to the horizon.

output_resolution.description: The resolution of the output image in X and Y, expressed as a power of two.

3d_texture_volume_render_light.description:
Renders the volume of a shape described by a 3D texture, using its corresponding signed distance field from the 3D Signed Distance Field image input. The volume is represented within the bounds of a unit cube.
The lighting is computed using directional light and an hemispherical skylight.

Note

The signed distance field is expected to be a 4096x4096 texture describing the shape with a 16x16 grid of 256 slices.
You may use the 3D Texture SDF node to compute the signed distance field for a 3D texture of 256 slices.

3d_signed_distance_field.description:
The 4096x4096 image representing the 256 slices of a shape's signed distance field, arranged in a 16x16 grid.

You may use the 3D Texture SDF node to compute the signed distance field for a 3D texture of 256 slices.

density.description:
The 4096x4096 image representing the 256 slices of a shape's density, arranged in a 16x16 grid.
Density is mapped using grayscale values from 0 (entirely transparent) to 1 (entirely opaque).

You may use 3D Volume Mask or 3D Noise nodes (3D Perlin Noise, 3D Voronoi, 3D Ridged Noise Fractal, etc.), combined with a 3D Texture Position node as position input, to generate a volume mask as a 3D texture of 256 slices.

output.description: The output image.

absorption.description: Adjusts how much light is absorbed as it passes through the volume.

albedo.description: The albedo color of the volume.

ambient_color.description: The color of the ambient skylight.

ambient_intensity.description: The intensity of the ambient skylight.

background_color.description: The color used to fill the background of the rendered scene.

background_mode.description:
The method of shading the background of the rendered scene, based on the Background Color:

- Shaded: The color is impacted by the directional light's color and intensity
- Constant Color: The color is applied uniformly regardless of the directional light

camera_distance.description: The distance from the camera to the shape.

camera_fov.description: The field of view of the camera in degrees.

camera_uv_position.description:
The position of the camera around the shape.

When the node is selected, you may use the position gizmo in the 2D View to orbit the camera.

dithering.description: Adjusts the intensity of the blue noise dithering used to smooth out the shading.

enable_ground_plane.description: When True, renders a ground plane. The unit cube enclosing the shape rests on this plane.

feather.description:
Multiplies the value supplied by the Density input with the inner distance field value.
This effectively adjusts the width of the fading gradient from the volume's outer limit inwards.

ground_plane_size.description: Adjusts the size of the ground plane.

infinite_plane.description: Sets the ground plane to extend infinitely to the horizon.

light_color.description: The color of the directional light.

light_color_mode.description:
Sets the method of acquiring the color of the directional light:

- Temperature (Kelvin): The color results from the light temperature, where a lower value results in a warmer color.
- RGB Color: Define the color using RGB values.

light_intensity.description: The intensity of the directional light.

light_temperature.description:
The temperature of the directional light, which impacts its color. A lower value results in a warmer color.

Useful values

1800 K - Candle light
2800 K - Incandescent bulb
5500 K - Daylight
6200 K - Natural white
7000 K - Overcast sky

light_uv_pos.description:
The position of the directional light around the shape.

When the node is selected, you may use the position gizmo in the 2D View to orbit the light source.

output_resolution.description: The resolution of the output image in X and Y, expressed as a power of two.

3d_viewer.description:
Computes a 3D render for a specified SDF or intersection scene defined by a function graph, with a custom camera and environment light.

This node is useful for authoring and visualizing SDF functions to be used in the Shape splatter v2 node.

Helpers are available to visualize key attributes of shapes in space.

For advanced users, custom functions can be authored to set up the camera and/or the 3D render per pixel.

environment.description:
The image that should be projected onto an infinite sphere and used for the environment lighting.

The projection is equirectangular, the same used by Designer's default environment maps available in the 3D View > HDRI environments category of the Library.

When left unconnected, a default environment is used.

Tip: Use an HDR image (32-bit) for precise lighting.

input_1.description:
An image that may be sampled in the Custom output function graph when the Output parameter is set to 'Custom'.

Use a Sample color node set to 'Image input 0' to sample from this image.

input_2.description:
An image that may be sampled in the Custom output function graph when the Output parameter is set to 'Custom'.

Use a Sample color node set to 'Image input 1' to sample from this image.

output.description:
The rendered scene, using the AOV selected in the Output parameter.

Note: For accurate readings in some AOVs, make sure the 2D View uses a linear color space, and the node uses an HDR 32-bit output format.

background_color.description: The flat color used as the scene's background.

background_mode.description:
Specifies the source of the scene's background, drawn where no shape surfaces are visible.

- Color: The flat 'Background color'.
- Environment: The image provided to the 'Environment' input, applied to an infinite sphere using equirectangular projection. (When the input is not connected, a default environment is used.)

bounding_frame_size.description:
Sets the XYZ size of the bounding frame.

Adjust the frame to your scene, then apply those same values to the SDF bound frame size parameter of the Shape splatter v2 node to ensure all shapes in the scene are correctly included and drawn by that node.

camera_distance_from_target.description:
The distance that the camera should rest from the Target position.

Adjusting this moves the camera along the camera-to-target direction.

camera_exposure.description:
The amount of light reaching the camera sensor, I.e. the intensity of the lighting in the render.

A lower value results in a darker rendered scene.

The 'Exposure Value' (EV) specifically refers to how much light the camera sensor is exposed to.

camera_fnumber.description:
The camera's aperture.

A lower value results in a shorter depth of field, I.e. a shorter distance range for objects being sharp and a stronger blurring effect as the distance from that range increases.

camera_focal_length.description: The camera's focal length, which impacts the zoom factor and depth of field effect.

camera_focus_distance.description:
Sets the distance of the focal point as a distance from the camera along along its forward vector.

Surfaces within range of that distance will appear sharp, that range — the depth of field — is defined by the F-number.

camera_fov.description: The orthographic camera's field of view (FOV), which impacts the zoom factor.

camera_function.description:
The function graph defining the camera's transform. This may be used for setting up a custom camera.

The function should set these variables:
- view.origin (Float3) The per-pixel world space position of the camera view.
- view.direction (Float3) The per-pixel forward vector of the camera view, according to the projection mode. (E.g. perspective or orthographic)

The following variables are available to get:
- camera.origin (Float3) The world space position of the camera. (camera.direction * camera_distance + camera.target)
- camera.direction (Float3) The world space direction of the camera, I.e. the camera's Y-forward vector.
- camera.right (Float3) The camera's X-right vector.
- camera.up (Float3) The camera's Z-up vector.
- camera.target (Float3) The world space position of the camera target.

camera_mode.description:
The method of projecting the scene onto the render image:

- Perspective: This projection conveys depth and enables lens effects such as depth of field.
- Orthographic: This projection flattens the scene, nullifying depth.
- Custom function: Author a function graph to set up a custom camera.

camera_samples.description:
The amount of camera samples performed per pixel.

This parameter affects the quality of the anti-aliasing and the depth of field effect.

A higher value results in a clearer, less noisy image at the cost of performance.

camera_target_position.description: The position of the camera target, towards which the camera is always oriented.

camera_tonemapper.description:
The tonemapping algorithm that should be applied to the scene render.

- None (Raw)
- sRGB
- AgX
- ACES

camera_uv_pos.description:
The position in 2D image space used to infer the camera position and direction orbiting the Target position.

Tip: This parameter can be adjusted intuitively by using the position gizmo available in the 2D View when the node is selected.

colorize_out_of_frame.description:
Applies a red color to the surfaces outside of the bounding frame.

This helps check that the scene is fully included in its bounding frame.

custom_output.description:
The function graph defining the per-pixel RGBA colors of the rendered scene, as a Float4 value.

Available variables:
- scene.position (Float3) The world space position of the scene's surfaces.
- scene.normal (Float3) The world space normals of the scene's surfaces.
- scene.hit (Boolean) Returns 'True' when a surface is hit by a camera ray.
- view.origin (Float3) The per-pixel world space position of the camera view.
- view.direction (Float3) The per-pixel forward vector of the camera view, according to the projection mode. (E.g. perspective or orthographic)
- material.color (Float3) The base color of the scene's surfaces.
- material.metalness (Float) The metalness of the scene's surfaces.
- material.roughness (Float) The roughness of the scene's surfaces.
- material.id (Integer) The material IDs of the scene's surfaces.

The node's image inputs can be sampled by selecting the following Sample color node slots:
- Image input 0 samples Input 1.
- Image input 1 samples Input 2.

default_basecolor.description: The default base color for surfaces where that color is not defined by its SDF or intersection function.

default_metalness.description: The default metalness value for surfaces where that value is not defined by its SDF or intersection function.

default_roughness.description: The default roughness value for surfaces where that value is not defined by its SDF or intersection function.

enable_axis.description: A visualization of the scene's XYZ axes as colored lines starting at the scene's origin.

enable_bounding_frame.description:
A visualization of a six-sided cage defining the bounds of the entire scene. Should ideally be the smallest possible size which fully includes the scene.

Use the Bounding frame size parameter to adjust the size of the cage.

The Colorize out of frame parameter lets you easily visualize the surfaces outside of that cage, which impacts the result of using that scene in the Shape splatter v2 node. (See 'Bounding frame size' tooltip)

enable_dof.description:
Simulates the 'depth of field' camera lens effect for the perspective camera.

Use the F number and Focus distance parameters to adjust the aperture and focal point of the effect respectively.

The result is also impacted bt the Focal length.

enable_grid.description: A visualization of a grid laid on the XY axes, where the size of one cell in X and Y is one scene unit.

enable_sdf_isolines.description:
A colored visualization of the signed distance field (SDF) function isolines.

The isolines are regularly repeating lines representing the distance field of the shape on the XY plane at a given height.

These are useful for checking the uniformity of the space defined by the SDF function.

Use the SDF isolines frequency and SDF isolines position parameters to adjust the density and height of the isolines.

enable_transform_helpers.description:
A visualization of the last applied rotation.

The visualisation includes
- An arrow representing the rotation axis direction vector, and colored after the weights of each world space axis.
- An arc representing the rotation angle, orthogonal to the arrow matching its color.

environment_rotation_horizontal.description: The rotation of the Environment, in number of turns.

helpers_opacity.description: The opacity of the 3D helpers, where a lower value results in fainter helpers.

ibl_samples.description:
The amount of light samples performed per camera sample.

A higher value results in smoother, more accurate lighting at the cost of performance.

intersect_scene.description:
The intersection function describing the surfaces and shapes in the scene.

Intersection functions for simple primitives and operators are available in the 3d_intersection folders of the 3d_functions.sbs Library package.

Tip: You can access the package by dropping any SDF node from the Library into the Explorer.

min_hit_distance.description:
Defines the minimum distance that translates into a hit for the SDF ray marching process.

A low value will increase the number of ray marching steps.

output.description:
The type of 3D render that should be output by the node, commonly referred to as AOVs (Arbitrary output variables).

Available AOVs are:
- Beauty: The final result of the 3D render, with art-directed colors and effects.
- Normal WS: The world space normals of the shapes in the scene.
- Normal TS: The tangent space normals of the shapes in the scene.
- Position: The world space position of the surfaces of the shapes in the scene.
- Distance: The raw distance from the camera to the shapes in the scene
- Depth: The signed distance of the shapes from the camera target plane, where the plane always faces the camera.
- Color: The shapes' base color (Use the 'Set color' node to assign colors to shapes in the scene function)
- Material ID: The material IDs applied to the shape surfaces (Use the 'Set material ID' node to assign material IDs to shapes in the scene function)
- Sphere tracing steps: A visualization of the amount of steps required for defining the surface of a shape. Brighter values mean more steps were required.
- Custom: Author a custom function to compute the color of the render per pixel.

Note: For accurate readings in some AOVs, make sure the 2D View uses a linear color space, and the node uses an HDR 32-bit output format.

ray_marching_steps.description:
The amount of steps performed in the sphere tracing process, the ray marching technique used to detect and draw the surfaces of the shapes.

A higher value results in accurate, consistent surfaces (especially for complex shapes), at the cost of performance.

Tip: Set the Output parameter to the 'Sphere tracing steps' AOV to visualize the areas of the shapes that require more steps. These areas will be impacted first by reducing the amount of steps.

scene_type.description:
The type of function used to describe the surfaces and shapes to be rendered:
- SDF: Use a signed distance field (SDF) function, which can describe complex shapes.
- Intersection: Use intersection functions, which are faster when only simple primitive shapes are needed.

sdf_isolines_frequency.description:
The amount of isoline repetitions within a given distance.

A higher value results in denser, thinner lines.

sdf_isolines_position.description:
The world space height of the XY plane used to draw the isolines.

Use this to check the distance field of the shape at various elevations.

sdf_scene.description:
The signed distance field (SDF) function describing the surfaces and shapes in the scene.

Use the nodes in the SDF functions category of the Library to author the function.

secondary_sphere_tracing_steps.description:
The amount of steps performed in the sphere tracing process to compute the diffusion and specular occlusion in order to draw cast shadows.

A higher value results in more accurate shadows at the cost of performance.

3d_volume_mask.description: Generates a representation of a primitive shape based on the Position input map.

position.description:
The map describing the 3D space coordinates the primitive is represented into.
The X/Y/Z coordinates are mapped to the R/G/B channels respectively.

output.description: The output image.

feather_width.description: Adjusts the width of the fading gradient from the primitive's surface inwards.

position_input.description:
The method of representing space through the Position input:

- UV Position: Use a UV map. The X/Y (U/V) coordinates are mapped to the R/G channels respectively. The Z axis is assumed to be the orthogonal forward vector.
- World Space Position: Use a position map to map the primitive in 3D space. The X/Y/Z coordinates are mapped to the R/G/B channels respectively.

position_uv.description: The position of the primitive in UV space.

position_worldspace.description: The position of the primitive in world space.

rotation.description: Defines the rotation of the shape in world space.

scale.description: Defines the global scale of the primitive, applied uniformly on all axes.

shape.description:
The primitive shape which should be represented:

- Cube
- Cylinder
- Sphere

size.description: Defines the size of the shape on each axis.

3d_voronoi.description:
Generates a 3D Voronoi noise based on the Position Map input.

Note

This noise is meant to be used with the GPU engine only (i.e., Direct3D or OpenGL). Go to Tools > Switch engine... or press the F9 key to select the desired engine.

position.description: The position map which should be used to map the 3D Voronoi noise.

output.description: The output image.

disorder.description: The intensity of the random offset applied to each point of the noise in the X, Y and Z axes.

distance_mode.description: Sets the method to compute the distance gradient around each point of the noise.

distance_scale.description: Adjusts the distance of the gradient around each point of the noise.

distortion_intensity.description: Controls the intensity of a warping effect applied on the 3D Voronoi noise.

distortion_scale_multiplier.description: Controls the scale of the deforming pattern used in the warping effect controlled by the Distortion Intensity.

edge_thickness.description:
Adjusts the thickness of the edges detected between cells of the 3D Voronoi noise.

Edges are detected in the X, Y and Z axes, thus some thicknesses may increase quicker than other depending on the cells' depth.

enable_tiling.description: Adjusts the 3D Voronoi noise so its resulting pattern repeats in the X, Y and Z axes.

invert.description: Inverts the output image.

minkowski_number.description:
The order p of the Minkowski distance.

If we divide the distance gradient into quadrants, this number impacts these quadrants as follows:

- p is exactly 1: Straight
- p is lower than 1: Concave
- p is greater than 1: Convex

Interesting values:

1.0 - Manhattan distance
2.0 - Euclidean distance
Infinity - Chebyshev distance

offset.description: Applies an offset to the position of the 3D Voronoi noise in the X, Y and Z axes.

random_color_seed.description: The discrete random seed which should be used for the color selection per cell.

random_color_seed_mode.description:
Sets the method of acquiring the random seed for the color selection per cell:

- Global Random Seed: Use the seed inherited by the node
- Manual Seed: Use a discrete seed

rounded_curve.description: Rounds the slope around each point of the noise to make it convex.

scale.description:
Controls the scale of the 3D Voronoi noise.

Note

When Tiling is enabled on any axis, the scale adjustement is stepped. This is expected.

size.description:
Controls the size of the 3D Voronoi noise in the X, Y and Z axes. Non-uniform values result in a stretching or squashing effect.

Note

When Tiling is enabled on any axis, the size adjustment is stepped. This is expected.

style.description: Sets the method rendering the data of the 3D Voronoi noise, considering the noise is based on a set of points in 3D space: - F1: the distance to the closest point in 3D space - F2: the distance to the second closest point in 3D space - F2-F1 - F1*F2 - F1/F2 - Edge: the edge between each cell of the noise in 3D space - Random color: assign a random flat color to each cell of the noise in 3D space

3d_voronoi_fractal.description:
Generates a fractal 3D Voronoi noise based on the Position Map input.

Note

This noise is meant to be used with the GPU engine only (i.e., Direct3D or OpenGL). Go to Tools > Switch engine... or press the F9 key to select the desired engine.

position.description: The position map which should be used to map the fractal 3D Voronoi noise.

output.description: The output image.

blend_mode.description:
Sets the method of blending together the values of overlapping cells in 3D space:

- Add: Add the values
- Max: Retain the highest value
- Min: Retain the lowest value

disorder.description: The intensity of the random offset applied to each point of the noise in the X, Y and Z axes.

distance_mode.description: Sets the method to compute the distance gradient around each point of the noise.

distance_scale.description: Adjusts the distance of the gradient around each point of the noise.

distortion_intensity.description: Controls the intensity of a warping effect applied on the 3D Voronoi noise.

distortion_scale_multiplier.description: Controls the scale of the deforming pattern used in the warping effect controlled by the Distortion Intensity.

edge_thickness.description:
Adjusts the thickness of the edges detected between cells of the 3D Voronoi noise.

Edges are detected in the X, Y and Z axes, thus some thicknesses may increase quicker than other depending on the cells' depth.

enable_tiling.description: Adjusts the 3D Voronoi noise so its resulting pattern repeats in the X, Y and Z axes.

global_opacity.description: Controls the range of the fractal 3D Perlin noise values from 0.

invert.description: Inverts the output image.

lacunarity.description:
Controls how the applied fractal pattern fills space.

A higher value results in less gaps in the pattern and a denser noise.

max_level.description:
The maximum level of of repetition used in the fractal pattern.

A wider minimum/maximum range results in a richer pattern with variation on more frequency ranges.

min_level.description:
The minimum level of of repetition used in the fractal pattern.

A wider minimum/maximum range results in a richer pattern with variation on more frequency ranges.

minkowski_number.description:
The order p of the Minkowski distance.

If we divide the distance gradient into quadrants, this number impacts these quadrants as follows:

- p is exactly 1: Straight
- p is lower than 1: Concave
- p is greater than 1: Convex

Interesting values:

1.0 - Manhattan distance
2.0 - Euclidean distance
Infinity - Chebyshev distance

offset.description: Applies an offset to the position of the fractal 3D Voronoi noise in the X, Y and Z axes.

random_color_seed.description: The discrete random seed which should be used for the color selection per cell.

random_color_seed_mode.description:
Sets the method of acquiring the random seed for the color selection per cell:

- Global Random Seed: Use the seed inherited by the node
- Manual Seed: Use a discrete seed

roughness.description:
Controls the balance between low and high levels of repetition in the fractal pattern.

Note

A value of 0 results in an output which is not in line with other low values following it. This is expected.

rounded_curve.description: Rounds the slope around each point of the noise to make it convex.

scale.description:
Controls the scale of the fractal 3D Voronoi noise.

Note

When Tiling is enabled on any axis, the scale adjustement is stepped. This is expected.

size.description:
Controls the size of the fractal 3D Voronoi noise in the X, Y and Z axes. Non-uniform values result in a stretching or squashing effect.

Note

When Tiling is enabled on any axis, the size adjustment is stepped. This is expected.

style.description: Sets the method rendering the data of the 3D Voronoi noise, considering the noise is based on a set of points in 3D space: - F1: the distance to the closest point in 3D space - F2: the distance to the second closest point in 3D space - F2-F1 - F1*F2 - F1/F2 - Edge: the edge between each cell of the noise in 3D space - Random color: assign a random flat color to each cell of the noise in 3D space

3d_worley_noise.description:
Generates a 3D Worley noise based on a position map.
This noise is meant to be used with the GPU Engine only (d3d / ogl).

style.description:
F1 represent the closest distance from the 3D position to the cell random center
F2 is

anisotropic_kuwahara_color.description:
Applies an anisotropic directional blur which conforms to the details of the image. The result is an image which appears to flow in the direction of the shapes within.

This adjustable blur computes or receives a direction map to determine that flow, which can be sharpened into flatter, more clearly defined areas.

The flow may also be broken down by rotating the direction in which blurring is applied. Similarly, a custom direction map may be used to override the one computed out of the image.

This filter can produce a painterly effect, and is useful for stylization.

anisotropy_angle_map.description:
The grayscale image describing the additional rotation applied to the computed direction, where the grayscale value is a number of turns.

The map still has an effect when the Anisotropy parameter is set to 0, as it impacts the rotation of the kernel used by the Kuwahara filter.

direction_map.description:
The map describing the direction used by the anisotropic filter kernel.

The map still has an effect when the Anisotropy parameter is set to 0, as it impacts the rotation of the kernel used by the Kuwahara filter.

Note: This input is only used when the Use Input Direction Map parameter is set to True.

input.description: The color image which should be processed.

radius_map.description: When connected, the blurring Radius is multiplied against the input image.

slope_map.description: The map representing slopes that the direction map is conformed to, according to the Slope Map Input Multiplier parameter value.

direction_map.description:
The direction map computed from the input image and used to drive the anisotropic blurring.

If the Use Input Direction Map parameter is set to True, the image provided to the Direction Map input is used and output as is.

output.description: The result of the anisotropic blurring applied by the node on the input image.

anisotropy.description:
Adjusts the contribution of the direction map in the blurring.

The direction map and all its modifiers (both parameters and input maps) still have an effect when this parameter value is 0, as the direction map is used in the Kuwahara filter kernel.

anisotropy_angle.description:
Adds a rotation to the direction map, in number of turns.

This additional rotation is cumulative with the one specified by the Anisotropy Angle Map input.

anisotropy_angle_map_multiplier.description:
Adjusts the intensity of the values in the Anisotropy Angle Map input, which are then added on top of the rotation applied to the direction map, in number of turns.

This additional rotation is cumulative with the one specified by the Anisotropy Angle parameter.

ignore_alpha.description:
When True, the alpha channel of the image is not impacted by the filter.

When False, the filter is also applied to the alpha channel.

radius.description:
The blurring radius, where a higher value results in a stronger blurring effect.

The maximum value is 32.

sharpness.description: Increases the contrast in the blurred areas, resulting in them looking flatter and more clearly defined.

slope_map_input_multiplier.description: Adjusts the intensity by which the direction map is conformed to the slopes provided by the Slope Map input.

smoothness.description:
Adjusts the amount of blending of colors in the computed direction.

When this value is 0, colors are mostly displaced in that direction and very little blending occurs.

tensor_smoothness.description:
Adjusts the intensity of blurring applied to the directions computed from the image and stored in the direction map.

Increasing this value ensures a smoother result when the image has a lot of high frequency detail.

use_input_direction_map.description: When True, no direction map is computed from the input image, and the image connected to the Direction Map input is used to drive the anisotropic blurring instead.

anisotropic_kuwahara_grayscale.description:
Applies an anisotropic directional blur which conforms to the details of the image. The result is an image which appears to flow in the direction of the shapes within.

This adjustable blur computes or receives a direction map to determine that flow, which can be sharpened into flatter, more clearly defined areas.

The flow may also be broken down by rotating the direction in which blurring is applied. Similarly, a custom direction map may be used to override the one computed out of the image.

This filter can produce a painterly effect, and is useful for stylization.

The map still has an effect when the Anisotropy parameter is set to 0, as it impacts the rotation of the kernel used by the Kuwahara filter.

direction_map.description:

The map still has an effect when the Anisotropy parameter is set to 0, as it impacts the rotation of the kernel used by the Kuwahara filter.

Note: This input is only used when the Use Input Direction Map parameter is set to True.

radius_map.description: When connected, the blurring Radius is multiplied against the input image.

slope_map.description: The map representing slopes that the direction map is conformed to, according to the Slope Map Input Multiplier parameter value.

direction_map.description:

If the Use Input Direction Map parameter is set to True, the image provided to the Direction Map input is used and output as is.

The direction map and all its modifiers (both parameters and input maps) still have an effect when this parameter value is 0, as the direction map is used in the Kuwahara filter kernel.

anisotropic_noise_v2.description:
A horizontal or vertical stack of randomly colored strips fading into one another.

The amount of strips is adjustable, as is the smoothness of their transitions.

output.description: The generated noise as a grayscale bitmap.

X_Amount.description: The amount of strips on the X axis.

Y_Amount.description: The amount of strips on the Y axis.

disorder.description:
Displaces the ingredients of the noise.

This can be used to animate the noise.

disorder_speed.description:
Adjusts the distance of displacement applied by the Disorder parameter.

This can be used to control the speed of displacement when animating the noise.

non_square_expansion.description: In non-square images, keeps the generated tile square and expands the noise generation to the image's bounds.

smoothness.description: The amount of fading between the strips, where 0 is no fading and 1 is fading over their entire length.

smoothness_interpolation.description: The weighting of the two methods of interpolation applied to fade the strips, where 0 is linear and 1 is Gaussian.

y_amount_by_resolution.description: If True, the number of strips on the Y axis will be equal to the image size on that axis.

ao_cancel.description: Cancels the residual ambient occlusion on a color map.

ao_cancellation.description: Cancels ambient shadows (AO) of the original image. The default value of 0.25 should work fine with most cases.

sat.description: Re-saturates areas that were undersaturated due to the AO.

apply_color_palette.description:
Applies the colors in an ordered palette to an image, using an ID map.

Colors are distributed by matching the indexes in the ID map to the indexes of colors in the palette.

For instance, color #2 in the palette will be applied to all pixels in the ID map with an ID value of 2.

This node may be used in combination with the following nodes: Quantize Color, Create Color Palette, Modify Color Palette, View Color Palette.

id.description:
The input ID map used to distribute the colors in the input palette.

An ID map is an image where pixels which are part of a whole (E.g., a shape) all hold the same unique identification value. In this case, the value is an integer.

An ID map can be produced using a Quantize Color node.

palette.description:
The amount of colors stored in the palette.

If that number does not match the actual amount of colors in the Palette image input, some colors of the palette will not be used or palette slots before the new appended colors may be blank – I.e., their color will be (0, 0, 0, 0).

output.description: The result of mapping the colors ¡n the palette to the indexes of the ID map.

atlas_scatter.description: Extract elements from an Atlas and scatter them on a background.

height_conform_to_background.description: At 0, the shape height remains intact, at 1 the shape height will be deformed by the underlying height background.

height_cutout_black_pixels.description: Ignores the black value from the pattern inputs.

height_offset_map_multiplier.description: Multiplies the shape height offset in function of the input image grayscale value.

height_scale_auto_adjust.description: Automatically adjust the height in function of the pattern scale to keep the shape height proportionnal to the background height.

height_scale_map_multiplier.description: Multiplies the shape height scale in function of the input image grayscale value.

height_skew.description: Deforms the shape height in function of the local background height slope: a linear gradient corresponding to the background slope is added to the shape height.

normal_skew.description: Skew the shape normal according to the background normal.

out_of_bounds_option.description:
Due to the technical nature of the splatter, shapes can't be drawn more than 2 cells size away from their original position.
If a shape becomes too large or is moved too far you have two options:
- Scale Shape will reduce the shape size when it hit a bound
- Constrain Position will move the shape back to its original position

pattern_distribution_map_multiplier.description: Select shape pattern in function of the input image grayscale value.

pattern_range.description:
Defines the range of patterns to be scattered.
If set to 0, all patterns will be used.

position_random_distribution.description:
Switches from a Guaussian distribution to a Uniform Distribution for the random position.

The Gaussian distribution will produce a more organic result compared to the Uniform distribution.

shape_rotation_from_background_slope.description:
Modifies the shape rotation in function of the background height slope.
Usually used in combination with the "Size Ratio from Bg Slope" parameter

shape_rotation_map_multiplier.description: Multiplies the shaperotation in function of the input image grayscale value.

shape_rotation_vector_map_multiplier.description: Set the shape rotation in function of the vector image input.

shape_scale_map_multiplier.description: Multiplies the shape scale in function of the input image grayscale value.

shape_scale_no_overlap.description: Reduces the shape scale so that they don't overlap.

shape_size_ratio_from_background_slope.description: Modifies the shape size ratio in function on the background height slope.

atlas_spliter.description:
Takes an atlas image input and splits out all separate elements as individual materials.

It can also be used to reorganize and move all elements into a grid.

The node works as an advanced application of the Flood Fill node.

auto_crop.description: Automaticaly crops the output size according to the largest shape to minimize empty space.

auto_rotation.description: Automatically rotates the shape according to its bounding box size ratio.

auto_scale.description: Automatically scale the shapes to fit in the grid cell.

custom_background_color.description: Allows you to choose a custom background color, instead of a dilation of the contents of that layer.

dilation_width.description: Applies a dilation effect based on the Opacity mask on all channels except the Normal and Height.

downscale_opacity_mask.description:
Downscales the Opacity Mask to remove potential noise or isolated pixels.

It prevents the detection of unwanted shapes and also increases performance.

enale_additional_inputs.description: Makes User 1 and User 2 inputs and settings available, for any additional maps not covered.

grid_opacity.description:
Sets the opacity of the grid lines when Grid View is True.

This parameter is meant for debugging.

grid_selection_opacity.description:
Sets the opacity of the grid selection highlight if Grid View is True.

This parameter is meant for debugging.

grid_view.description: Displays all the detected shapes in a grid.

ignore_shape_smaller_than.description: Ignores shapes whose diagonal size is lower than the specified value.

input_normal_format.description: Sets the format of the input normals. Setting the wrong format will lead to an incorrect result.

rotation.description: Global shape rotation angle, as a number of turns.

shape_detection_precision.description: Applies a dilation effect based on the Opacity Mask on all channels except Normal and Height.

shape_selection.description:
When Grid View is True, this sets which cell is highlighted.

In the other case, this sets which cell is returned.

auto_crop_color.description:
The Auto Crop Color node adjusts the Input so that its content is either placed at the center of the image without being resized, or resized to the span of the image.

The content of the image is defined by a box fitted to the first and last pixels on X and Y which RGB or Alpha values are higher than 0 (i.e. non-black), depending on the Use alpha parameter.

input.description: The input color image which should be cropped.

output.description: The output image.

filtering_mode.description:
Defines how to treat the sampled results when interpolating between pixels:

Nearest: will sample exactly the same value (faster)

Bilinear: will apply a bilinear filter on the result for a smoother look

Auto: Uses the most appropriate of the two modes above depending on the selected Mode for cropping.

mode.description:
Set the cropping method which should be applied:

Crop square: the image is cropped so that the shape is at the center of the smallest square image which can fully include it.

Crop auto: The image is cropped so that the shape is at the center of the smallest square or non-square image which can fully include it.

Fit (Keep ratio): The image is resized to the full span of the image while keeping its proportions (i.e. width to length ratio).

Fill (Stretch): The image is resized to the full span of the image.

use_alpha.description:
Use the alpha channel of the Input to determine the image content's bounds for cropping.

When set to False, black pixels are used instead.

auto_crop_grayscale.description:
The Auto Crop Grayscale node adjusts the Input so that its content is either placed at the center of the image without being resized, or resized to the span of the image.

The content of the image is defined by a box fitted to the first and last pixels on X and Y which values are higher than 0 (i.e. non-black).

input.description: The input grayscale image which should be cropped.

output.description: The output image.

filtering_mode.description:
Defines how to treat the sampled results when interpolating between pixels:

Nearest: will sample exactly the same value (faster)

Bilinear: will apply a bilinear filter on the result for a smoother look

Auto: Uses the most appropriate of the two modes above depending on the selected Mode for cropping.

mode.description:
Set the cropping method which should be applied:

Crop square: the image is cropped so that the shape is at the center of the smallest square image which can fully include it.

Crop auto: The image is cropped so that the shape is at the center of the smallest square or non-square image which can fully include it.

Fit (Keep ratio): The image is resized to the full span of the image while keeping its proportions (i.e. width to length ratio).

Fill (Stretch): The image is resized to the full span of the image.

auto_levels.description:
Applies automatic 'Level In Low' and 'Level In High' values.

In other words, remaps the grayscale values in an input image to use the full span of grayscale values from 0 to 1, where the darkest pixel is 0 (pure black) and the brightest pixel is 1 (pure white).

base_color_2_inputs.description: Defines base colors for a texture from a black and white UV to SVG mask.

Color_Replacement_Intensity.description: Set how much original textures colors will be replaced.

base_color_4_inputs.description: Defines base colors for a texture from a Hue shift UV to SVG baked map.

Color_Replacement_Intensity.description: Set how much original textures colors will be replaced.

base_color_8_inputs.description: Define base colors for a texture from a Hue shift UV 2 SVG baked map.

Color_Replacement_Intensity.description: Set how much original textures colors will be replaced.

basecolor_metallic_roughness_converter.description:
Converts PBR BaseColor/Metallic/Roughness to various targets.

- PBR Diffuse/Spec/Gloss
- Vray
- Corona
- Redshift
- Arnold 4
- RenderMan (PxrSurface)

For renderers, make sure the Glossiness, Roughness and IOR maps are loaded with gamma 1.0 (linear).

f0.description:
Gamma 1.0
Specular F0 map.
[0, 0.08] For dielectrics
1 for metals

ior.description:
Gamma 1.0
For Vray: plug directly into the ior channel
For Corona: create a CoronaMix texture in the ior channel
- mode: Divide
- input 1: white
- input 2: this texture

bevel.description: Adds a bevel effect on a black and white mask to generate hard surface details normal and height maps.

custom_curve.description: Input a vertical gradient representing the bevel curve

bevel_smooth.description:
Draws a gradient or a flat color from the borders of a mask outward, inward or both.

Overlapping gradients are sorted by inverted normalized distance so that the distance to the closest border is drawn.

The distance of the gradient can be dynamically adjusted along the border using a distance map.

distance_map.description:
An optional input used when the value of the Distance Map Multiplier parameter is higher than 0.

It is used to adjust the beveling/dilation distance along the borders of the mask, where a darker value results in a shorter distance.

mask_input.description:
The image from which the mask should be extracted.

All values above the Mask Threshold value are white in that mask.

source_input.description:
An optional input only used when the Output Mode parameter is set to Dilation.

In that case, this image is overlaid on the white areas of the mask, and the grayscale values at the borders are dilated.

output.description: The result image, according to the selected Output Mode.

uv.description:
A UV map where the UVs are dilated along the mask borders.

This can be connected to a UV Mapper node to map any other image using these dilated UVs.

direction.description:
The side of the mask border which should be dilated:

  • In: draw towards the interior of the mask
  • Out: draw towards the exterior of the mask
  • In/Out: draw towards both the interior and exterior of the mask

distance_map_multiplier.description: Adjusts the impact of the Distance Map over the Maximum Distance.

mask_offset.description: Moves the mask borders inward or outward.

mask_smoothness.description:
The intensity of the smoothing applied to the mask.

The value is the radius of the blur, and 1 unit is 1/256th of the image.

mask_threshold.description:
The value used to detect the borders of the mask in the Mask Input image.

Values above this threshold are the inside of the mask shapes, while values below are the outside.

maximum_distance.description: The distance of dilation, in normalized image space where 1 is the length of the shorter side of the input image.

output_mode.description:
The method of dilating the mask borders:

  • Bevel: draw a gradient from 1 to 0 where 0 is reached at the Maximum Distance
  • Dilation: draw a solid color as far as the Maximum Distance. This color is white or the color Source Input image at the mask border, if connected
  • Distance: the raw distance from the closest mask border, in normalized image space where 1 is the length of the shortest side of the image

scale.description:
Adjusts the horizontal (X) and vertical (Y) distance of the dilation.

These values are multipliers for the Maximum Distance parameter value.

bitmap2material_2_light.description: Converts a single bitmap or diffuse texture into a full material.

AO_Light_Angle.description: Angle from which the directional light generate shadows

AO_Light_Distance.description: Distance of the light which generate shadows

AO_Multiply.description: Intensity of generated shadows in the Diffuse map

Depth_Balance.description: Balances the size of background/foreground elements

Normal_Sharpen.description: Sharpens the Normal and Height maps

Normal_Soften.description: Softens the Normal and Height maps

Normal_Strength.description: Changes the Normal map intensity. Set to 0 if you want to use input normal only.

Pinch_Intensity.description: Sharpens edges on height and normal maps.

Relief_Graphic_Equalizer.description: Allow to smooth or sharpen the Normal and Height maps depending on details size.

Specular_Diffuse_Influence.description: Influence of the Diffuse map color on the Specular map

Specular_Saturation.description: Changes the saturation of the Specular map

metallic_glossiness_influence.description: Substract the metallic map to the glossiness map to get metal area reflection more intense.

metallic_roughness_influence.description: Substract the metallic map to the roughness map to get metal area more smooth.

metallic_specular_influence.description: Add the metallic map to the specular map to get metal area more reflective.

blackbody.description: Converts color temperature in Kelvin to RGB.

blue_noise_fast.description: Fast approximation of blue noise. This noise has minimal low frequency components and no concentrated spikes in energy.

blur_hq_grayscale.description: Uniform high quality blur grayscale version

bnw_spots_1.description: A variation of the rough Black and white (BnW) spots noises.

output.description: The generated noise as a grayscale bitmap.

disorder.description:
Displaces the ingredients of the noise.

This can be used to animate the noise.

disorder_anisotropy.description:
Controls the span of directions of the displacement applied by the Disorder parameter, where a higher value results in a narrower, more defined direction.

The direction is controlled by the Disorder anisotropy angle parameter.

disorder_anisotropy_angle.description: Controls the direction of the displacement applied by the Disorder parameter, when the 'Disorder anisotropy' parameter is not zero.

disorder_speed.description:
Adjusts the distance of displacement applied by the Disorder parameter.

This can be used to control the speed of displacement when animating the noise.

non_square_expansion.description: In non-square images, keeps the generated tile square and expands the noise generation to the image's bounds.

roughness.description: The balance of the noise octaves, where a higher value will make the higher frequency octaves more visible.

scale.description:
The subdivision of the grid used to generate the noise tiles.

A higher value results in more tiles being drawn and a denser noise.

tile_offset.description: Controls the position of the portion of infinite plane used to render the noise.

bnw_spots_2.description: A variation of the rough Black and white (BnW) spots noises.

output.description: The generated noise as a grayscale bitmap.

disorder.description:
Displaces the ingredients of the noise.

This can be used to animate the noise.

disorder_anisotropy.description:
Controls the span of directions of the displacement applied by the Disorder parameter, where a higher value results in a narrower, more defined direction.

The direction is controlled by the Disorder anisotropy angle parameter.

disorder_anisotropy_angle.description: Controls the direction of the displacement applied by the Disorder parameter, when the 'Disorder anisotropy' parameter is not zero.

disorder_speed.description:
Adjusts the distance of displacement applied by the Disorder parameter.

color_to_mask.description: Extracts a greyscale mask from selected colors in a color image.

color.description: The reference uniform color around which the color selection should be performed.

distance_color_space.description:
The Equalize process involves comparing colors to determine the distance between them. Certain color spaces and distance algorithms are better suited for specific use cases.

This drop-down list lets you select the color space used to compare the colors:

- RGB (Data): Color is split into Red, Green, Blue channels and distributed straight along those axis, disregarding human perception. This is appropriate for images holding raw data.
- Linear sRGB (Color): Color is split into Red, Green, Blue channels and distributed in a linear relationship to the pixel light intensity. This is appropriate for image which may be visualised on displays.
- Luminance (Color): Color is split into Hue, Chroma, Luminance values where only the Luminance value is used in the comparaison. This is appropriate for image which may be visualised on displays.
- Lab (Color): A standardized perceptual color space, which distributes colors in such a way that colors that 'feel' close are actually close in the cube. This is appropriate for image which may be visualised on displays.
- Angle (Normal): Color is split into the X, Y, Z axes of a vector and compared through a dot product. This is appropriate for images holding Tangent Space Normals.

distance_weights.description:
The Lab color distance algorithm (DeltaE2000) introduces certain weight factors for each lightness, chroma, and hue value.

Lower values will decrease the influence of the factors in the color difference algorithm.

Since the eye will generally accept larger differences in lightness (L) than in chroma (C) or hue (H), a default ratio for (L:C:H) is (0.5:1:1). A 0.5:1:1 ratio will allow twice as much difference in lightness as in chroma or hue.

selection_fade.description: Fade the color selection based on the distance to the reference color.

threshold.description: The distance to the reference color below which colors are selected.

use_color_input.description:
Use an input image instead of a uniform color, in order to define a reference color per pixel.

The input image is provided by the Color input input.

create_color_palette_16colors.description:
Creates an ordered list of colors and outputs it as a palette, with a maximum of 16 colors.

The node can append new colors to an existing palette, using the set of Palette inputs.

This node may be used in combination with the following nodes: Quantize Color, Apply Color Palette, Modify Color Palette, View Color Palette.

palette.description:
An ordered list of RGB colors encoded as a row of pixels. The palette can hold a maximum of 256 colors.

This input is optional. If used, colors set up by the node are appended to this palette.

The palette may be visualized with the View Color Palette node.

palette_color_amount.description:
The amount of colors stored in the palette.

If that number does not match the actual amount of colors in the Palette image input, the visualization may be incomplete or have more blank slots than absolutely necessary.

palette_color_amount.description: The updated amount of colors stored in the palette, with the specified amount of colors added to it.

curvature.description: Creates a curvature map from a normal map.

curvature_smooth_v2.description:
Computes the curvature of a surface described by a normal map.

A curvature map represents the concave and convex areas of a surface.
Flat areas are 50% gray. Convex areas are brighter, while concave areas are darker.

The concave and convex areas are also split into their own outputs, for easier selection or masking of areas based on those characteristics.

input.description: The normal map describing the surface which curvature should be computed.

concavity.description:
The concavity map computed out of the input normal map.

The more concave an area is, the brighter it is in the map.
Flat or convex areas are black.

convexity.description:
The convexity map computed out of the input normal map.

The more convex an area is, the brighter it is in the map.
Flat or concave areas are black.

curvature.description:
The curvature map computed out of the input normal map.

Flat areas are 50% gray. Convex areas are brighter, while concave areas are darker.

normal_format.description:
The format of the input normal map. Effectively inverts the green channel.

  • DirectX: The Y axis points up
  • OpenGL: The Y axis points down

curvature_sobel.description: Creates a curvature map from a normal map using a Sobel filter.

Curvature_Intensity.description: Set the intensity of the curvature details.

Normal_Intensity.description: Set the intensity of the normal details.

diffusion_color.description: Apply a diffusion process to the colors in Source image according to the provided Mask (creating smooth gradations between colors). Only colors from pixels matching the mask are diffused; other pixels do not participate in the result.

input.description: The image to diffuse.

intensity.description: Defines locally how strong the diffusion process is applied. This map should be contrasted for a noticeable effect.

mask.description:
Diffusion mask (assumed to be black and white): White pixels are sampled in Source and diffused in black pixels.The image should be black and white. If the mask includes gradients, the cutoff value is 0.5.
The image should have the same resolution as Source.

distance.description: Adjusts the maximum distance of the diffusion.

enable_dithering.description:
Controls the sampling method of each pass.
Dithering allows convergence in less passes, but introduces noise.
Without it, each pass is faster but more passes are required to achieve a smooth result without banding artifacts.

is_normal.description: Adds a normalization on values at every step.

iterations.description:
The number of diffusion iterations to perform (higher is better but slower). Useful values are in the [8, 48] range.
Please note that if you are not looking for mathematical correctness, low values are fine or even better.

use_alpha_as_mask.description: Use the alpha channel of the Source input as the diffusion mask, instead of the Mask input.

diffusion_grayscale.description: Apply a diffusion process to the values in Source image according to the provided Mask (creating smooth gradations between values). Only values from pixels matching the mask are diffused; other pixels don't participate in the result.

input.description: The image to diffuse.

intensity.description: Defines locally how strong the diffusion process is applied. This map should be contrasted for a noticeable effect.

mask.description:
Diffusion mask (assumed to be black and white): White pixels are sampled in Source and diffused in black pixels. The image should be black and white. If the mask includes gradients, the cutoff value is 0.5.
The image should have the same resolution as Source.

distance.description: Adjusts the maximum distance of the diffusion.

enable_dithering.description:
Controls the sampling method of each pass.
Dithering allows convergence in less passes, but introduces noise.
Without it, each pass is faster but more passes are required to achieve a smooth result without banding artifacts.

iterations.description:
The number of diffusion iterations to perform (higher is better but slower). Useful values are in the [8, 48] range.
Please note that if you are not looking for mathematical correctness, low values are fine or even better.

diffusion_uv.description:
Apply a diffusion process to the UV coordinates in Source image according to the provided Mask , interpolating coordinates between values from Source. Only UVs from pixels matching the mask are diffused; other pixels do not participate in the result.

Please note tiling is handled in a special way: When tiling is enabled (which is the case by default), neighboring coordinates can be averaged accross the 0/1 limit - i.e. if the U coordinate value is 0.1 on one pixel and 0.8 on another, the averaged value will be 0.95 rather than 0.45 because tiling of the coordinates is assumed. This is independent from the actual pixel position: coordinate values are handled the same way all other the image).
This can lead to undesirable results when using this filter for texture deformation. If that happens, make sure your mask defines "control curves/points" no more than half a texture length apart.

input.description:
The UVs to diffuse.
Please note tiling is handled in a special way in this filter (see filter description).

mask.description:
The diffusion mask: White pixels are sampled in Source and diffused in black pixels. The image should be black and white. If the mask includes gradients, the cutoff value is 0.5.
The image should have the same resolution as Source.

iterations.description:
The number of diffusion iterations to perform (higher is better but slower). Useful values are in the [8, 48] range.
Please note that if you are not looking for mathematical correctness, low values are fine or even better.

directional_distance.description:
Draws a distance gradient from the borders of a mask in a specified direction.

Overlapping gradients are sorted by inverted normalized distance so that the distance to the closest border is drawn.

The distance of the gradient can be dynamically adjusted along the border using a distance map.

angle_map.description:
An optional input used when the value of the Angle Map Multiplier parameter is higher than 0.

It is used to adjust the direction of the distance gradient by adding its value to the direction angle, in number of turns.

The Angle Map Offset parameter lets you remap the values by specifying which value is 0.

distance_map.description:
An optional input used when the value of the Distance Map Multiplier parameter is higher than 0.

It is used to adjust the beveling/dilation distance along the borders of the mask, where a darker value results in a shorter distance.

input.description:
The image from which the mask should be extracted.

All values above 0.5 are white in that mask.

output.description: The result image according to the selected Output Mode.

uv.description:
A UV map where the UVs are dilated from the mask borders along the specified direction.

This can be connected to a UV Mapper node to map any other image using these dilated UVs.

angle.description: The direction of the distance gradient in number of turns, where 0 is horizontal and to the right – I.e., a (1,0) vector.

angle_map_multiplier.description: Adjusts the impact of the Angle Map over the Angle.

angle_map_offset.description:
Remaps the values in the Angle Map by specifying which value in that map should be 0.

E.g., an offset of 0.5 means that a value of 0.75 is 0.25 turns, and a value of 0.3 is -0.2 turns.

distance_map_multiplier.description:
Adjusts the impact of the Distance Map over the Maximum Distance.

Note: This parameter has no effect when the Distance Map input is not connected.

max_distance.description: The distance travelled by the distance gradient, in normalized image space where 1 is the length of the shorter side of the input image.

output.description:
The method of drawing the distance gradient from the mask borders:

  • Inverted Normalized Distance: A gradient from 1 to 0 where 0 is reached at the Maximum Distance, multiplied by the Distance Map if connected
  • Distance: A gradient of raw distance values from the mask border, where 1 is the length of the shorter side of the input image

angle_random.description: The maximum amout of random variation applied to the Angle value, in number of turns.

disorder.description:
Displaces the ingredients of the noise.

This can be used to animate the noise.

disorder_anisotropy.description:
Controls the span of directions of the displacement applied by the Disorder parameter, where a higher value results in a narrower, more defined direction.

The direction is controlled by the Disorder anisotropy angle parameter.

disorder_anisotropy_angle.description: Controls the direction of the displacement applied by the Disorder parameter, when the Disorder anisotropy parameter is not zero.

disorder_speed.description:
Adjusts the distance of displacement applied by the Disorder parameter.

This can be used to control the speed of displacement when animating the noise.

non_square_expansion.description: In non-square images, keeps the generated tile square and expands the noise generation to the image's bounds.

scale.description:
The subdivision of the grid used to generate the noise tiles.

A higher value results in more tiles being drawn and a denser noise.

tile_offset.description: Controls the position of the portion of infinite plane used to render the noise.

directional_noise_4.description: A variation of the Directional noise noises.

extend_shape_color.description:
The Extend Shape node extends a section of the Input over a set direction and distance.

The Show helper parameter lets you visualize the extended section and extension direction.

input.description: The color input image which should be extended.

output.description: The output image.

end_position.description: The end position of the extension vector.

extension_angle.description: The angle of the section which should be extended, considering the starting point is a vertical section.

extension_distance.description:
The distance over which section specified by the Extension Position and Extension Angle should be extended.

The distance is expressed as a proportion of the image span.

extension_position.description:
The position in the image of the section which should be extended.

The value is expressed as an offset from center.

filtering_mode.description:
Defines how to treat the sampled results when interpolating between pixels:

Nearest: will sample exactly the same value (faster)

Bilinear: will apply a bilinear filter on the result for a smoother look

mode.description:
Defines the parameters used to apply the extension.

Bidirectional: The section of the Input specified by the Extension Position and Extension Angle is extended over the Extension Distance in opposite directions.

Unidirectional: The section of the Input specified by the Extension Position and Extension Angle is extended over the Extension Distance in a single direction.

Start / End Positions: An extension vector is defined by Start Position and End Position. The perpendicular section of the Input at the Start Position is extended over this vector up to the End Position.

show_helper.description: Visualize the extended section as an overlay with arrows showing the direction of the extension.

start_position.description: The start position of the extension vector.

extend_shape_grayscale.description:
The Extend Shape node extends a section of the Input over a set direction and distance.

The Show helper parameter lets you visualize the extended section and extension direction.

input.description: The grayscale input image which should be extended.

output.description: The output image.

end_luminance_offset.description:
Applies a luminance offset to the area of the image following the extended section.

This luminance offset is interpolated along the section to the luminance of the area of the image preceding the section.

end_position.description: The end position of the extension vector.

extension_angle.description: The angle of the section which should be extended, considering the starting point is a vertical section.

extension_distance.description:

extension_position.description:

luminance_offset_ignores_black_pixels.description: When set to True, the luminance offsets specified in both Start Luminance Offset and End Luminance Offset are only applied to non-black pixels – i.e., pixels which value is higher than 0.

mode.description:
