show_helper.description: Visualize the extended section as an overlay with arrows showing the direction of the extension.

start_luminance_offset.description:

This luminance offset is interpolated along the section to the luminance of the area of the image following the section.

start_position.description: The start position of the extension vector.

fabric_weathering.description: This filter add a weathering effect on a fabric material.

AO.description: Here, input a mesh baked ambient occlusion map.

Input_12.description: Here, input a mask, the effect will be applied on the white area of the mask. The mask also help to avoid seams discontinuity.

Input_2.description: Here, input an ambient occlusion map.

Normal_Wold_Space.description: Here, Input a mesh baked world space normal map.

basecolor_1.description: Here, input a base color map.

ambient_occlusion_intensity.description: Sets the ambient occlusion map blend intensity.

basecolor.description: Sets On/Off the base color channel.

basecolor_intensity.description: Sets the base color map blend intensity.

diffuse_intensity.description: Sets the diffuse map blend intensity.

glossiness_intensity.description: Sets the glossiness map blend intensity.

height_intensity.description: Sets the height map blend intensity.

normal_format.description: Sets the type of normal map input(s) and output(s).

normal_intensity.description: Sets the normal map blend intensity.

roughness_intensity.description: Sets the roughness map blend intensity.

specular_intensity.description: Sets the specular map blend intensity.

facing_normal.description: This filter take a normal map as an entry image and produce a grayscale image in which the value correspond to how much the normals are facing the viewer in texture space

flood_fill.description:
Convert a black&white mask to data usable by additional filters:
  • Flood Fill to Gradient
  • Flood Fill to RandomColor
  • Flood Fill to BBox Size
  • Flood Fill to Position
This filter can only work with the GPU engine.

mask.description:
A black and white mask used to delimit shapes.
Shapes that tiles across the entire image may not be properly recognized.

output.description:
Output the BBox map for shapes.
RG channels holds the UVs for each shape.
BA channels holds the shape size (whole image = 1.0).

referenceMap.description:
Provide extra infos on shapes and pixels.
RG channels hold the position of one pixel that belongs to the shape. Every pixel of the shape "references" the same pixel.
The Blue channel holds information about shape borders: the unique external border holds value 1, while internal "holes" borders (if any) holds value 0.5. Other pixels holds value 0.
The Alpha channel holds the original provided mask.

advanced.description: Make advanced parameters and output visible. You should'nt normally need to use these.

profile.description:
This parameter roughly controls the number of iterations of this filter. As your mask will become more complex, the filter will need more iteraton to find the shapes limits.
We recommend that you use the `Simple`preset while tweaking your mask if you want better responsiveness, and that you stay to `Complex`profile otherwise.
You will rarely need to use `No failure mode`. Please note that this profile doesn't actually garantee no failure if you have a lot of holes in a shape, or a shape that cross the entire texture.

profileOverride.description:
Provide fine control of the number of iterations. Provided mainly for learning purpose.
  • -1 disable the profile override.
  • 40 matches`Simple`profile.
  • 70 matches`Complex`profile.
  • 100 matches`No failure`profile

flood_fill_to_bbox_size.description: Takes the flood fill node as input and returns the bounding box size for each shape.

flood_fill_to_color.description: Takes the flood fill node as input and colorizes shapes using the color image input.

flood_fill_to_gradient_2.description:
Takes the flood fill node as input and generates a gradient per shape.

v2

flood_fill_to_grayscale.description: Takes the flood fill node as input and colorizes shapes using the grayscale image input.

flood_fill_to_position.description: Takes the flood fill node as input and returns the center position of each shape.

flood_fill_to_random_color.description: Takes the flood fill node as input and generates a random color(RGBA) per shape.

flood_fill_to_random_grayscale.description: Takes the flood fill node as input and generates a random grayscale color per shape.

rotation_autoscale.description: Automatically scale up the pattern to fill the shape when a rotation is applied. This prevents having an empty area around the pattern.

luminance_offset_map_multiplier.description:
Use the sampled input image to offset the shape luminance offset.<li>
The pixel value is centered around 0.5:
 -values under 0.5 will apply a negative offset
- values above 0.5 will apply a positive offset

rotation_autoscale.description: Automatically scale up the pattern to fill the shape when a rotation is applied. This prevents having an empty area around the pattern.

floodfill_to_index.description:
Assigns a unique index to shapes from a Flood Fill node.

Make sure you plug the two outputs coming from the Flood Fill node by enabling the "Display advanced Parameters and Output" option.

The Fractal sum family of noises are all based on this node.

GlobalOpacity.description:
The opacity of the noise octaves added together in the final result.

A high value may result in areas being burned to white.

MaxLevel.description:
The maximum octave used in the noise.

A higher value results in a higher noise frequency.

MinLevel.description:
The minimum octave used in the noise.

A higher value results in a higher noise frequency.

Roughness.description:
The balance of the noise octaves.

A higher value will make the higher frequency octaves more visible.

grid_atlas_color.description:
Pack up to 16 color images on a grid of adjustable XY size.
The output atlas image can be sampled from by a Shape splatter v2 or a Shape splatter mapper node.

output.description: The output color grid atlas.

grid_size_x.description:
The size of the grid on the X axis.
I.e. the number of images being packed on the X axis.

grid_size_y.description:
The size of the grid on the Y axis.
I.e. the number of images being packed on the Y axis.

output_size_mode.description:
The method of defining the size of the output image according to the node's 'Output size' base parameter:

- Manual: Use the size as is.
- Automatic ratio: Adjust the image ratio according to the grid size in order to minimise the image size. Deformation will occur for non-square grids using 3 rows or columns, e.g. (3, 2), (4, 3)

grid_atlas_grayscale.description:
Pack up to 16 grayscale images on a grid of adjustable XY size.
The output atlas image can be sampled from by a Shape splatter v2 or a Shape splatter mapper node.

output.description: The output grayscale grid atlas.

grid_size_x.description:
The size of the grid on the X axis.
I.e. the number of images being packed on the X axis.

grid_size_y.description:
The size of the grid on the Y axis.
I.e. the number of images being packed on the Y axis.

output_size_mode.description:
The method of defining the size of the output image according to the node's 'Output size' base parameter:

- Manual: Use the size as is.
- Automatic ratio: Adjust the image ratio according to the grid size in order to minimise the image size. Deformation will occur for non-square grids using 3 rows or columns, e.g. (3, 2), (4, 3)

hald_clut.description:
Applies a LUT on the input image. The LUT has to be in the Hald format in 4096*4096 resolution.

http://www.quelsolaar.com/technology/clut.html

lut_intensity_by_input_alpha.description: Defines if the LUT effect is weighted by the alpha channel.

hbao.description: Horizon Based AO filter. Takes a height map as input.

gpu_optim.description:
Optimizes the computation for the Substance GPU (D3D/OpenGL) engines and gives a higher quality result.
Don't use this option if you plan to use the CPU engine in Unity/Unreal Engine.

height_depth.description:
Defines the height map scale compared to the image size.
A value of 1 means the height map depth is the same as its the largest border.

non_square.description: If true, the filter will behave correctly for non square images, otherwise the filter will act as if the image was square and will stretch the result.

radius.description:
Adjusts the Ambient Occlusion radius. Corresponds to the "Max Occluder Distance" parameter in the bakers.
A value of 1 equals to the largest border of the image.

hdr_merge_2.description:
Merge multiple photographic exposures to create a High Dynamic Range image.
The first input is the most under-exposed image.

hdr_range_viewer.description: Displays the out of range values using color overlay.

hdr_range_viewer_grayscale.description: Displays the out of range values using color overlay.

height_blend.description: Creates a mask allowing to blend realistically2 materials based on their respective height map.

height_extrude.description:
Generates an orthographic 3D rendering of the given Height.
The camera view can be controlled with the position gizmo. To use a shape rather than a height, try to set the Ground parameter to Off.

height.description: A terrain height to render or a black and white mask to extrude

depth.description: The depth buffer of the rendered terrain of extruded object

height_scale.description: Applies a global factor on the input height values.

inversedy.description: The Normal Format parameter inverts y coordinate of the normal map.

normal_intensity.description: Same as Intensity parameter of Normal node. Set it to 256 to get a shar-free normal while rotating.

scale.description: Global scale applied to the output.

terrain_mode.description: If Ground is off, a black background is shown where the input is 0 rather than a ground-like plane.

view_angle.description:
Euler angles of the camera, in half-turns.
Please note that horizontal rotation and scale are applied directly to the input.

voffset.description: Moves the final output up or down.

height_map_frequencies_mapper.description: Separates frequencies of a height map in two different maps.

height_normal_blend.description: Allows to add normal informations from a height map to another normal map.

Normal_Intensity.description: Set the intensity of the normal details.

height_to_normal_world_units_2.description: Converts a height map to a proportional normal map by using the ratio between a set Surface size and a defined Height depth which are both in world unit (cm).

surface_size.description: Width or Height (whichever is the smallest) of the represented surface.

histogram_compute.description:
Computes the histogram for a grayscale image.

The histogram is encoded as a row of pixels in an image, where each pixel value is the population of the color value matching the pixel position on the X-axis.
E.g., a pixel value of 75 at (0.25, 0) means there are 75 pixels which have the 0.25 color value in the image.

The node also outputs the cumulative distribution function (CDF) computed for the image.

Note: All values out of the [0,1] range are clamped, therefore the histogram may not be accurate for HDR images.

input.description: The image for which the histogram should be computed.

cdf.description:
The result of the cumulative distribution function (CDF) computed for the image, encoded in a row of pixels where each pixel is the sum of all pixel values to its left.

That sum is then normalized against the total number of pixels in the image.

histogram.description:
The histogram computed for the input image, encoded as a row of pixels where each pixel value is the population of the color value matching the pixel position on the X-axis.

E.g., a pixel value of 75 at (0.25, 0) means there are 75 pixels which have the 0.25 color value in the image.

histogram_resolution.description:
The width of the histogram. A higher value allows for a finer value distribution.

Available resolutions are, in pixels:
256, 512, 1024, 2048, 4096

histogram_equalize.description: Equalizes the histogram for a grayscale image, effectively adjusting the grayscale values aiming for an equal distribution.

input.description: The image for which the histogram should be equalized.

output.description: The result image with histogram equalization applied.

histogram_resolution.description:
The width of the histogram. A higher value allows for a finer value distribution.

Available resolutions are, in pixels:
256, 512, 1024, 2048, 4096

histogram_smoothing.description:
The histogram may be smoothed by redistributing the grayscale values in the image to equalize the difference between each value.

This parameter adjusts the intensity of that smoothing.

histogram_range.description: Adjust the range and position of the grayscale input histogram.

histogram_render.description: Draws the histogram for a grayscale image.

input.description: The image for which the histogram should be drawn.

output.description: The histogram visualization computed out of the input image.

automatic_scale.description:
When True, remaps the histogram to use the full height of the image.

When False, each column uses as many pixels in height as there are occurrences of a value in the input image.

histogram_resolution.description:
The width of the histogram. A higher value allows for a finer value distribution.

Available resolutions are, in pixels:
256, 512, 1024, 2048, 4096

sampling.description:
The method of filtering the histogram image, which impacts the result when the histogram resolution and render resolution mismatch:

  • Bilinear: applies bilinear filtering to the histogram, resulting in interpolated points
  • Nearest: samples the nearest pixel with no filtering, resulting in flat steps

scale.description: Scales the histogram vertically, where a value of 1 is the full height of the histogram.

histogram_scan_non_uniform.description: This filter lets you drive the contrast and position of the histogram on a per-pixel basis for complex effects and masks.

id_to_mask_grayscale.description:
Creates a mask out of an ID map where the pixels with the selected pixel values are white.

An ID map is an image where pixels which are part of a whole (E.g., a shape) all hold the same unique identification value. In this case, the value is an integer.

id.description: The input ID map from which a mask should be extracted.

output.description: The binary mask extracted from the input ID map.

id_integer.description: The pixel value in the ID map which should be white in the output mask.

id_range.description: The range of pixel values in the ID map, from start to end, which should be white in the output mask.

selection_mode.description:
The method of selecting the pixel values in the ID map which should be white in the mask:

  • Solo: Select a single pixel value
  • Range: Select a range of pixel values

invert.description: Inverts colors. Every channels but alpha are inverted

leather_weathering.description: This filter add a weathering effect on a leather material.

AO.description: Here, input a mesh baked ambient occlusion map.

Input_12.description: Here, input a mask, the effect will be applied on the white area of the mask. The mask also help to avoid seams discontinuity.

Input_2.description: Here, input an ambient occlusion map.

Normal_Wold_Space.description: Here, Input a mesh baked world space normal map.

basecolor_1.description: Here, input a base color map.

ambient_occlusion_intensity.description: Sets the ambient occlusion map blend intensity.

basecolor.description: Sets On/Off the base color channel.

basecolor_intensity.description: Sets the base color map blend intensity.

diffuse_intensity.description: Sets the diffuse map blend intensity.

glossiness_intensity.description: Sets the glossiness map blend intensity.

height_intensity.description: Sets the height map blend intensity.

normal_format.description: Sets the type of normal map input(s) and output(s).

normal_intensity.description: Sets the normal map blend intensity.

roughness_intensity.description: Sets the roughness map blend intensity.

specular_intensity.description: Sets the specular map blend intensity.

lighting_cancel_hf.description: Cancels the high frequencies highlights of a texture.

lighting_cancel_lf.description: Cancels the low frequencies highlights of a texture.

make_it_tile_patch.description: Creates a bigger texture from a small patch

make_it_tile_patch_grayscale.description: Creates a bigger texture from a small patch

mask_to_paths_2.description:
Converts a grayscale input pattern Mask into a list of path segments encoded in the output Paths.

Controls over the start position of generated paths as well as their order in the list are available.

The generated Paths can be further processed using dedicated nodes – E.g., Path 2D Transform, Paths Warp – or converted into splines using the Path to Spline node to map or scatter shapes along them.

Note: See the Paths Format Specifications page in Designer's documentation to learn about the method used to encode Paths.

mask.description: The input pattern which should be converted into a list of Paths.

paths.description:
A list of paths encoded in a color image. each path describes a list of encoded segments.

The result can be processed using another Paths-processing node, or sent to a Paths to Spline node to further process it as Splines.

preview.description: A preview composited on top of the mask to help visualizing the effects of the parameters.

corner_threshold.description:
Each vertex encoded in paths can hold a flag indicating whether it is hard (I.e., a corner) or smooth.

This parameter lets you mark more or less corners according to the angle between their adjacent segments.

Note: This 'corner' flag is currently not supported by any existing node. Nevertheless. you may still visualize its effect with the Preview Paths node, by using its Show Corners parameter.

force_close_paths.description:
Create a segment between the start and the end vertices of open paths.

Disabling this may fix undesirable lines traversing your pattern in an unexpected way, however paths may not be closed anymore.

mask_filtering.description:
Apply smoothing on the input mask.

Useful when the input pattern has very sharp edges, which usually causes artifacts.

order_direction.description:
The angle describing the direction used to order the Paths from first to last along that direction.

The value is a number of turns used to rotate an X-left direction vector. This means 0 sets a direction vector of (-1, 0), and 0.25 (90 degrees) sets a direction vector of (0, 1).

order_func.description:
The function used to order the Paths. It returns a Float value.

Paths are ordered in ascending order according to this function's value. In other words, the result of the function for each Path is the sorting key used to order the Paths.

Available variables:

  • bbox.center (Float2): The position of the Path Bbox's center
  • bbox.topleft (Float2): The position of the Path Bbox's top-left corner
  • bbox.size (Float2): The size of the Path Bbox (X: width, Y: height)

order_mode.description:
The method of ordering the generated Paths.

The position or size Paths' bounding box (Bbox) may be used as a criterion for ordering the Paths.

This has a significant impacts when converting the generated Paths to Splines using the dedicated node, as multiple Spline nodes use the Splines' order.

  • Legacy (fast): The method used in the previous version of this node, which offers significantly better performance
  • By Bbox center position along direction: Paths are ordered according to the position of the center of their Bbox, from first to last along the specified direction
  • By Bbox Bbox top-left position along direction: Paths are ordered according to the position of the top-left corner of their Bbox, from first to last along the specified direction
  • By Bbox size - Largest to smallest: Paths are ordered according to the size of their Bbox, from largest to smallest
  • By Bbox size - Smallest to largest: Paths are ordered according to the size of their Bbox, from smallest to largest
  • Custom ordering function: Use a custom function to order Paths

startup_direction.description:
The angle describing the direction used to select the startup vertex. For each Path, the last vertex in this direction is selected.

The value is a number of turns used to rotate an X-leftdirection vector. This means 0 sets a direction vector of (-1, 0), and 0.25 (90 degrees) sets a direction vector of (0, 1).

startup_func.description:
The function used to select the startup vertex. It returns a Float value.

For each vertex, the function is executed and the vertex for which the function returns the highest result is selected.

Available variables:

  • vertex.cornerness (Float): The score of the vertex as a candidate to be a corner
  • vertex.pos (Float2): The vertex position in image space

startup_mode.description:
The method of selecting which vertex should be the start of each generated Path around the shapes in the Mask.

This has a significant impact when converting the generated Paths to Splines using the dedicated node, as multiple Spline nodes use the Splines' start and end.

  • Most acute vertex: The vertex forming the lowest angle with its previous and next vertices
  • Vertex at extreme of a specified direction: The last vertex in a given direction
  • Vertex closest to a specified position
  • Vertex farthest from a specified position
  • Custom startup function: Use a custom function to select the vertex which should be used as the start each Path

startup_target_pos.description:
The position in the image used to select the startup vertex.

For each Path, the vertex closest to or farthest from this position is selected, according to the selected Path Startup Mode.

threshold.description: The grayscale value of Mask that will be used to separate the outside (values < Mask Threshold Value) and the inside (values > Mask Threshold Value) of the shape.

tolerance.description:
Implicitly controls the amount of segments that will be generated.

A high amount of decimation will make round shapes somewhat polygonal, while no decimation will generate almost one segment by pixel.

A reasonable amount will better match the shape of both straight lines and curves without creating a lot of intermediary points for straight lines.

material_adjustment_blend.description: Blends full material with the same adjusted material (contrast/luminosity).

material_blend.description: Blends 2 full materials together using a Color or Greyscale mask.

material_color_blend.description: Blends full material with custom uniform colors.

material_height_blend.description: Blends 2 full materials together using their Height map.

material_mesh_data_blender.description: Allows to mix data from mesh baked maps and materials maps to get more details in the final material maps set.

material_selector.description: Generates binary masks based on a Color ID image.

material_transform.description: This node allows the 2d transform of all the channels of a material at once

normal_scale_and_skew_transformation.display_name: Normal affected by scale and skew transformations

median_filter_color.description:
This filter smoothes out noise in an image while preserving edges.

For every pixel, the node computes a color value according to the median value of the pixel and its neighbours.

input.description: The color image that the filter should be applied to.

output.description: The color image computed by applying the filter to the input color image.

affect_alpha.description:
Controls whether the filter should be applied to the image's alpha channel.

When True, the alpha channel is unchanged.

filter_type.description:
The computation applied to the values sampled in the kernel.

  • Median: Use the median value directly.
  • MLMAD: Stands for 'Median Of Least Median Absolute Deviation'. The deviation accounts for how different a value is from the median.
    Instead of using the median value directly which may be skewed by an outlier pixel with high deviation, the MLMAD method uses the median of all deviations.
    This method results in a stronger smoothing effect that may flatten areas according to the kernel size.

kernel_size.description:
A kernel is a specific group of values used in a filter’s computations. In this context, it is the values of the target pixel and its neighbours.

For every pixel the filter samples its value all its neighbors in a square kernel, then computes the median value.

This parameter controls the size of that square kernel, in pixels. A larger kernel results in a stronger, farther-reaching smoothing effect at the cost of some detail.

  • 3x3: a kernel 3 pixels wide and 3 pixels tall, totalling 9 sampled pixels.
  • 5x5: a kernel 5 pixels wide and 5 pixels tall, totalling 25 sampled pixels.

median_filter_grayscale.description:
This filter smoothes out noise in an image while preserving edges.

For every pixel, the node computes a grayscale value according to the median value of the pixel and its neighbours.

input.description: The grayscale image that the filter should be applied to.

output.description: The grayscale image computed by applying the filter to the input grayscale image.

filter_type.description:
The computation applied to the values sampled in the kernel.

  • Median: Use the median value directly.
  • MLMAD: Stands for 'Median Of Least Median Absolute Deviation'. The deviation accounts for how different a value is from the median.
    Instead of using the median value directly which may be skewed by an outlier pixel with high deviation, the MLMAD method uses the median of all deviations.
    This method results in a stronger smoothing effect that may flatten areas according to the kernel size.

kernel_size.description:
A kernel is a specific group of values used in a filter’s computations. In this context, it is the values of the target pixel and its neighbours.

For every pixel the filter samples its value all its neighbors in a square kernel, then computes the median value.

This parameter controls the size of that square kernel, in pixels. A larger kernel results in a stronger, farther-reaching smoothing effect at the cost of some detail.

  • 3x3: a kernel 3 pixels wide and 3 pixels tall, totalling 9 sampled pixels.
  • 5x5: a kernel 5 pixels wide and 5 pixels tall, totalling 25 sampled pixels.

mesh_data_combiner.description: Allows to get only one cable from all baked mesh data, and so to plug only one cable in the library "Mesh Adaptive" filters "Mesh Data" inputs

messy_fibers_1.description: A variation of the Messy fibers structured noises.

output.description: The generated noise as a grayscale bitmap.

angle.description: The angle used to set the direction of the threads, in number of turns and starting from horizontal right.

angle_random.description: The maximum amout of random variation applied to the Angle value, in number of turns.

disorder.description:
Displaces the ingredients of the noise.

This can be used to animate the noise.

disorder_anisotropy.description:
Controls the span of directions of the displacement applied by the Disorder parameter, where a higher value results in a narrower, more defined direction.

The direction is controlled by the Disorder anisotropy angle parameter.

disorder_anisotropy_angle.description: Controls the direction of the displacement applied by the Disorder parameter, when the 'Disorder anisotropy' parameter is not zero.

disorder_speed.description:
Adjusts the distance of displacement applied by the Disorder parameter.

This can be used to control the speed of displacement when animating the noise.

lines_number.description: The amount of tiling applied to the base threads, where a higher value results in denser, thinner threads.

non_square_expansion.description: In non-square images, keeps the generated tile square and expands the noise generation to the image's bounds.

scale.description:
The subdivision of the grid used to generate the noise tiles.

A higher value results in more tiles being drawn and a denser noise.

tile_offset.description: Controls the position of the portion of infinite plane used to render the noise.

messy_fibers_2.description: A variation of the Messy fibers structured noises.

output.description: The generated noise as a grayscale bitmap.

angle.description: The angle used to set the direction of the threads, in number of turns and starting from horizontal right.

angle_random.description: The maximum amout of random variation applied to the Angle value, in number of turns.

disorder.description:
Displaces the ingredients of the noise.

This can be used to animate the noise.

disorder_anisotropy.description:
Controls the span of directions of the displacement applied by the Disorder parameter, where a higher value results in a narrower, more defined direction.

The direction is controlled by the Disorder anisotropy angle parameter.

disorder_anisotropy_angle.description: Controls the direction of the displacement applied by the Disorder parameter, when the 'Disorder anisotropy' parameter is not zero.

disorder_speed.description:
Adjusts the distance of displacement applied by the Disorder parameter.

This can be used to control the speed of displacement when animating the noise.

lines_number.description: The amount of tiling applied to the base threads, where a higher value results in denser, thinner threads.

non_square_expansion.description: In non-square images, keeps the generated tile square and expands the noise generation to the image's bounds.

scale.description:
The subdivision of the grid used to generate the noise tiles.

A higher value results in more tiles being drawn and a denser noise.

tile_offset.description: Controls the position of the portion of infinite plane used to render the noise.

messy_fibers_3.description: A variation of the Messy fibers structured noises.

output.description: The generated noise as a grayscale bitmap.

angle.description: The angle used to set the direction of the threads, in number of turns and starting from horizontal right.

angle_random.description: The maximum amout of random variation applied to the Angle value, in number of turns.

disorder.description:
Displaces the ingredients of the noise.

This can be used to animate the noise.

disorder_anisotropy.description:
Controls the span of directions of the displacement applied by the Disorder parameter, where a higher value results in a narrower, more defined direction.

The direction is controlled by the Disorder anisotropy angle parameter.

disorder_anisotropy_angle.description: Controls the direction of the displacement applied by the Disorder parameter, when the 'Disorder anisotropy' parameter is not zero.

disorder_speed.description:
Adjusts the distance of displacement applied by the Disorder parameter.

This can be used to control the speed of displacement when animating the noise.

luminance_random.description: The range of luminance randomly subtracted from the threads, where 1 is the full range.

non_square_expansion.description: In non-square images, keeps the generated tile square and expands the noise generation to the image's bounds.

scale.description:
The subdivision of the grid used to generate the noise tiles.

A higher value results in more tiles being drawn and a denser noise.

tile_offset.description: Controls the position of the portion of infinite plane used to render the noise.

ambientOcclusion.description: Here, Input an Ambient Occlusion Map.

mg_ground_dirt.description: Generate a dirt mask based on a single axis position map.

occlude.description: Remove the effect on part of the object based on the world space normal map.

min_max.description: Returns the minimum and maximum pixel value from the input image.

mirror.description: Mirror a source image by the X/Y axis or a specific corner.

mirror_grayscale.description: Mirror a source image by the X/Y axis or a specific corner.

mlv_color.description:
MLV stands for 'Mean of Least Variance'. This filter enhances edges and smoothes out noise in an image.

The filter finds structuring areas in an image and uses them to both sharpen and flatten it. In some cases, this may result in steps along gradients wider than the structuring areas.

input.description: The color image which should be processed.

output.description: The filtered color image.

affect_alpha.description:
When True, filtering is also applied on the alpha channel of the image.

When False, the alpha channel is ignored entirely and left as is in the output.

criterion.description:
The criterion used to select the values that will define the structuring areas in the image.

In other words, how pixels should be grouped into areas that should be smoothed out.

  • Variance: Select values with the lowest dispersion around the mean, which results in clusters of pixels similar to each other
  • Coefficient of variation: Select values while taking the mean into account, which results in less variation in brighter areas inversely

gaussian.description:
Use a Gaussian distribution for grouping pixels into structuring areas.

When True, this results in smoother areas and a reduced flattening effect.

intensity.description:
The strength of the filtering applied to the image.

Higher values results in more smoothing of details and noise into flatter areas.

iterations.description:
The number of times the filter is run, where each iteration is applies on the result of the previous one.

More iterations result in flatter and sharper structuring areas.

smoothness.description: The intensity of the smoothing applied to the structuring areas, which results in rounder areas and lessens the stepping effect which may occur at higher filtering intensities.

mlv_grayscale.description:
MLV stands for 'Mean of Least Variance'. This filter enhances edges and smoothes out noise in an image.

The filter finds structuring areas in an image and uses them to both sharpen and flatten it. In some cases, this may result in steps along gradients wider than the structuring areas.

input.description: The grayscale image which should be processed.

output.description: The filtered grayscale image.

criterion.description:
The criterion used to select the values that will define the structuring areas in the image.

In other words, how pixels should be grouped into areas that should be smoothed out.

  • Variance: Select values with the lowest dispersion around the mean, which results in clusters of pixels similar to each other
  • Coefficient of variation: Select values while taking the mean into account, which results in less variation in brighter areas inversely

gaussian.description:
Use a Gaussian distribution for grouping pixels into structuring areas.

When True, this results in smoother areas and a reduced flattening effect.

intensity.description:
The strength of the filtering applied to the image.

Higher values results in more smoothing of details and noise into flatter areas.

iterations.description:
The number of times the filter is run, where each iteration is applies on the result of the previous one.

More iterations result in flatter and sharper structuring areas.

smoothness.description: The intensity of the smoothing applied to the structuring areas, which results in rounder areas and lessens the stepping effect which may occur at higher filtering intensities.

modify_color_palette.description:
Modifies the colors in an ordered palette and applies it to an image using an ID map.

Colors can be selected by matching the indexes in the ID map to the indexes of colors in the palette.

For instance, color #2 in the palette will be applied to all pixels in the ID map with an ID value of 2.

This node may be used in combination with the following nodes: Quantize Color, Create Color Palette, Apply Color Palette, View Color Palette.

id.description:
The input ID map used to select colors, in order to modify and distribute them in the output.

An ID map is an image where pixels which are part of a whole (E.g., a shape) all hold the same unique identification value. In this case, the value is an integer.

An ID map can be produced using a Quantize Color node.

palette.description:
An ordered list of RGB colors encoded as a row of pixels. The palette can hold a maximum of 256 colors.

This is the palette that the node modifies.

output.description: The result of mapping the colors ¡n the modified palette to the indexes of the ID map.

palette.description:
The updated palette with the specified color modifications applied.

The palette may be visualized with the View Color Palette node.

color_index.description:
The index of the target color.

Colors in the palette are ordered from left to right, and the first color's index is 0.

color_position.description:
The position in the ID map where the index should be sampled.

Use the gizmo in the 2D View for easily selecting a location in the image.

Tip: You may display the quantized image that the ID map is extracted from, then select the Modify Color Palette node to display the gizmo. This makes selecting a color to modify more intuitive.

color_selection_contrast.description:
Controls the falloff gradient of the selection over neighbouring colors.

Colors are arranged in a cube which width, height and depth are a gradient where a component of a color increases from 0 to 1 (E.g. red, green and blue in RGB).

This parameter adjusts the selection falloff over other colors in the cube around the selected color, where 0 is a smooth gradient from the selected color to the farthest and 1 is a cutoff from fully included to not included.

color_selection_mode.description:
The method of selecting the target color in the palette which should be modified:

  • Color index: The index of the target color
  • Image space: The position in the ID map where the index should be sampled. When this mode is selected, a position gizmo is available in the 2D View for easy selection

color_selection_spread.description:
Controls how far the selection reaches into neighbouring colors.

Colors are arranged in a cube which width, height and depth are a gradient where each component of a color increases from 0 to 1 (E.g. red, green and blue in RGB).

This parameter adjusts how far around the selected color in the cube other colors can also be modified, where 1 is the full cube's width.

distance_color_space.description:
Colors are arranged in a cube which width, height and depth are a gradient where a component of a color increases from 0 to 1 (E.g. red, green and blue in RGB).

This parameter lets you select the color space used to distribute colors in the cube, which changes neighbouring colors.

You may select the color space which fits your use case:

  • Lab (Color): A standardized perceptual color space, which distributes colors in such a way that colors that 'feel' close are actually close in the cube. This is appropriate for images which may be visualized on displays.
  • RGB (Data): Color is split into Red, Green and Blue and distributed straight along those axis, disregarding human perception. This is appropriate for images holding raw data, such as normal maps.

hsl.description: Controls the hue, saturation and lightness offsets applied to the original color.

mode.description:
The method of modifying the target color:

  • Override color: replace the color with another one
  • HSL: adjust the color using hue, saturation and lightness offsets

opacity.description: Controls the interpolation between the original and modified colors, where 1 means the modified color fully replaces the original color.

override_color.description: Specifies the color which should replace the original color.

moisture_noise_1.description:
A variation of the rich and spongy Moisture noises.

Discs of varying hardness and size and scattered and add or subtract from the color below, starting from a base gray.

output.description: The generated noise as a grayscale bitmap.

disorder.description:
Displaces the ingredients of the noise.

This can be used to animate the noise.

disorder_anisotropy.description:
Controls the span of directions of the displacement applied by the Disorder parameter, where a higher value results in a narrower, more defined direction.

The direction is controlled by the Disorder anisotropy angle parameter.

disorder_anisotropy_angle.description: Controls the direction of the displacement applied by the Disorder parameter, when the 'Disorder anisotropy' parameter is not zero.

disorder_speed.description:
Adjusts the distance of displacement applied by the Disorder parameter.

This can be used to control the speed of displacement when animating the noise.

global_opacity.description: The opacity of all ingredients of the noise, where 0.0 results in a base flat gray and 1.0 is the result of the full addition or subtraction applied by the ingredients

non_square_expansion.description: In non-square images, keeps the generated tile square and expands the noise generation to the image's bounds.

pattern_angle.description: The angle used to set the direction of the scattered pattern, in number of turns and starting from horizontal right.

pattern_angle_random.description: The maximum amout of random variation applied to the Pattern angle value, in number of turns.

pattern_size.description: A multiplier for the size of a scattered pattern., where 1.0 is its original scaterring size.

scale.description:
The subdivision of the grid used to generate the noise tiles.

A higher value results in more tiles being drawn and a denser noise.

tile_offset.description: Controls the position of the portion of infinite plane used to render the noise.

moisture_noise_2.description:
A variation of the rich and spongy Moisture noises.

Discs of varying hardness and size and scattered and add or subtract from the color below, starting from a base gray.

roughness_intensity.description: Sets the roughness map blend intensity.

specular_intensity.description: Sets the specular map blend intensity.

multi_directional_warp_grayscale.description: Warps the input image multiple times into various directions.

multi_material_blend.description: Blends up to 16 full materials together using a Color ID image.

noise_upscale_1.description: Allows to increase the resolution of a noise without a too much visible tiling effect.

noise_upscale_2.description: Allows to increase the resolution of a noise without a too much visible tiling effect.

noise_upscale_3.description: Allows to increase the resolution of a noise without a too much visible tiling effect.

Transform node for non square texture.

- update pixel size automatically

non_square_transform_greyscale.description:

- keep content ratio intact
- update pixel size automatically

non_uniform_blur_grayscale.description: Non-uniform blur driven by a grayscale map grayscale version

non_uniform_directional_warp_color.description: Applies a Directional Warp where the intensity and angle are sampled from image inputs.

non_uniform_directional_warp_grayscale.description: Applies a Directional Warp where the intensity and angle are sampled from image inputs.

non_uniform_rotation_color.description:
Rotates the Input using the Rotation Map input. The values of the image represent a number of turns.

The rotation is performed around the position specified by the Pivot Position value or the Pivot Position map input.

Positive values in the Rotation Map input result in a clockwise rotation.

input.description: The input color image which should be rotated.

rotation_map.description:
The map used to control the amount of rotation, in number of turns.

The sampled values are multiplied against the Rotation Angle Multiplier.

Negative values result in a counter-clockwise rotation.

rotation_pivot_map.description:
The image used to specify the position of the rotation pivot.

The X/Y position is mapped to the R/G channels of the image.

output.description: The output image.

background_color.description: Background color to display outside of the image's bounds in case the tiling is not set to H and V Tiling.

filtering_mode.description:
Defines how to treat the sampled results when interpolating between pixels:

Nearest: will sample exactly the same value (faster)

Bilinear: will apply a bilinear filter on the result for a smoother look

pivot_position.description: The position of the pivot around which the image is rotated.

rotation_angle_multiplier.description: Adjusts the intensity of the Rotation Map input.

rotation_angle_offset.description: Applies the specified additional amount of rotation.

use_pivot_position_map.description: Use a bitmap input to specify the position of the rotation pivot. The X/Y position is mapped to the R/G channels of the Position Map input.

non_uniform_rotation_grayscale.description:
Rotates the Input using the Rotation Map input. The values of the image represent a number of turns.

The rotation is performed around the position specified by the Pivot Position value or the Pivot Position map input.

Positive values in the Rotation Map input result in a clockwise rotation.

input.description: The input grayscale image which should be rotated.

rotation_map.description:
The map used to control the amount of rotation, in number of turns.

The sampled values are multiplied against the Rotation Angle Multiplier.

Negative values result in a counter-clockwise rotation.

rotation_pivot_map.description:
The image used to specify the position of the rotation pivot.

The X/Y position is mapped to the R/G channels of the image.

output.description: The output image.

background_color.description: Background color to display outside of the image's bounds in case the tiling is not set to H and V Tiling.

Defines how to treat the sampled results when interpolating between pixels:

Nearest: will sample exactly the same value (faster)

Bilinear: will apply a bilinear filter on the result for a smoother look

pivot_position.description: The position of the pivot around which the image is rotated.

rotation_angle_multiplier.description: Adjusts the intensity of the Rotation Map input.

use_pivot_position_map.description: Use a bitmap input to specify the position of the rotation pivot. The X/Y position is mapped to the R/G channels of the Position Map input.

normal_blend.description: Allows to blend two normal maps keeping normalisation.

normal_intensity.description: Increases or decreases the intensity of the normal map input.

normal_invert.description: Invert channels of the input image (invert green channel by default).

normal_sobel_2.description: The Normal filter computes a normal map from a grayscale input interpreted as a height map based on a Sobel filter.

normal_to_height.description: Transforms tangent normal map into a height map

normal_to_height_hq.description: Generates Height map from Normal map

normal_transform.description:
Transform a normal map using a transformation widget.
The output normal is accuratly corrected in function of the rotation.

handle_scale_and_skew_transformation.description: If enabled, the normal intensity will be affected by the scale and skew transformations. The resulting normal map will be the same as if the original height map was transformed then converted to normal.

normal_uncombine.description: Removes from a normal map the surface details described by a height map.

height.description: The height map representing the surface details which should be removed from the combined normal map.

normal.description: The normal map from which details should be removed.

guessed_intensity.description: An estimate of the intensity which should be set to a Normal node connected to the input height map, to match the intensity of the input normal map.

normal.description: The normal map where the surface details described by the input height map were removed.

normal_format.description:
The format of the input normal map. Effectively inverts the green channel.

  • DirectX: The Y axis points up
  • OpenGL: The Y axis points down

normal_vector_rotation.description:
Applies a rotation on the normal map vectors.
It can also be used to rotate any vec2 (like positions).

normal_intensity.description: Set the normal map strength intensity. 0 = Null 1 = Maximum Intensity

painted_metal_weathering.description: This filter add a weathering effect on a metal material.

dirtiness.description: Set how dirty is the object.

dust.description: Sets the amount of dust on top of the object

edges_wearing.description: Sets how used are the sgarp edges of the object.

metallic_intensity.description: Sets the metallic map blend intensity.

paint_cracks_warp_intensity.description: Sets the shape variation intensity for the damaged paint edges.

paint_peeling.description: Sets the amount of paint removed the object.

raw_metal_color.description: Sets the color of the metal visible where paint will be damaged.

raw_metal_glossiness_value.description: Sets the glossiness value of the metal visible where paint will be damaged.

raw_metal_roughness_value.description: Sets the roughness value of the metal visible where paint will be damaged.

sharp_edges_scratches_scale.description: Sets the shape variation scale for the edges wearing.

panorama_line_light.description: Generates a spherically projected line shape based on the coordinates of two points in space.

panorama_physical_sun_sky.description: Physical Sun and Sky implementation based on Hosek-Wikie skylight model.

sun_size.description: Scale of the Sun, any value different than 1 is non physically correct.

panorama_plane_light.description: Generates a spherically projected plane shape. The plane can be placed and oriented in 3d using the input parameters.

panorama_shape_2.description: Generates a spherically projected rectangular shape. The shape transformation is driven by a transformation guizmo.

panorama_sphere_light.description: Generates a spherically projected sphere shape.

path_to_spline.description:
Converts a paths into splines which can be visualized using a Spline Render node and processed using Spline nodes.

This node can be used after the Mask to Paths node to form a chain that converts a mask into splines.

Note: Splines are curves thus cannot retain the sharpness of paths. Expect some smoothing of shapes when converting paths into splines.

paths.description: A list of encoded segments paths. Connect this input to the result of a Mask to Paths or to another Path-processing node.

preview.description: The preview of the output splines as a grayscale image.

spline_coords.description:
The coordinates of the output splines’ points encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - X position
G - Y position
B - Height
A - Packed data:

  • Sign: Spline is closed (negative) or open (positive).
  • Absolute value: Thickness + 1

spline_data.description:
Additional data of the input splines encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - Tangents X
G - Tangents Y
B - Unused
A - Unused

preview_segment_amount.description:
Adjusts the number of segments used to draw the spline visualization in the Preview output.

A higher value results in a smoother line.

preview_show_direction_helper.description: Displays a dot at the start of the spline and an arrowhead at its end in the Preview output.

show_thickness_envelope.description: Displays additional lines at the edges of the spline’s thickness.

spline_precision.description: The base-2 logarithm (log2) of the number of vertices sampled in each path of the Paths input to build the corresponding spline.

thickness_px.description: Adjusts the thickness in pixels of the spline visualization in the Preview output.

paths_polygon.description:
Generates a polygon primitive in the Paths format.

Use the Path 2D Transform node to position the primitive precisely. The result can be visualized with a Preview Paths node.

Note: See the Paths Format Specifications page in Designer's documentation to learn about the method used to encode Paths.

paths.description:
Contains a list of one path which describes a list of segments, encoded in a color image.

Connect this input to the result of a Mask to Paths or to another Path-processing node.

nbSides.description:
The amount of equilateral faces of the generated polygon primitive.

Set a value between 100 and 1000 to generate a circle.

paths_trace.description:
Traces segments and vertices of the path over the input background. A random color is assigned to each path.

The result is similar to the 'Preview' output of the Mask to Paths node, with more options.

background.description:
The image over which the paths visualization should be drawn.

This also controls the node's Output Size.

paths.description:
Contains a list of one path which describes a list of segments, encoded in a color image.

Connect this input to the result of a Mask to Paths or to another Path-processing node.

tracing.description:
Display the each path line with the given Segment Thickness in a random color.
If Show Corners is enabled, displays a square on each vertex that is marked as corner (additive blending).
If Show Vertices is enabled, displays a circular shape on each vertex (additive blending). (Corners are still displayed as squares.)

thickness.description: Adjusts the thickness of rendered segments in pixels.

trace_corners.description:
Displays a square on each vertex that is marked as a corner, with additive blending.

Note: This 'corner' flag is currently not supported by any existing node.

trace_vertices.description:
Displays a circular shape on each vertex, with additive blending.

Corners are still displayed as squares.

paths_warp.description:
Deforms the input Paths according to the Gradient Input image.

The effect is identical to that of the Warp node.

Note: See the Paths Format Specifications page in Designer's documentation to learn about the method used to encode Paths.

gradient.description:
The grayscale input image controlling both the amount and the direction of warping. The image may be compared to a height map.

The effect is identical to that of the Warp node.

paths.description:
Contains a list of one path which describes a list of segments, encoded in a color image.

Connect this input to the result of a Mask to Paths or to another Path-processing node.

The transformed Paths. You can use the Preview Paths node to visualize the result of the transformation.

The result can be processed using another Paths-processing node, or sent to a Paths to Spline node to further process it as Splines.

intensity.description: Adjusts the intensity of the warping effect, where 0 results in no warping.

quality.description:
A higher value warps the input paths by multiple small increments.

This can prevent the path from crossing itself, especially when using high Intensity values.

pbr_albedo_safe_color.description: Adjust albedo colors (basecolor or diffuse) for PBR workflows.

tolerance.description:
Adjust the tolerance for dark values:
- 0:  max value = 50/255
- 1: max vallue = 30/255

pbr_base_material.description: Used to create a base metal PBR material with options to work in metal-rough or specular-glossiness workflows.

glossiness.description: Set a base value for glossiness

glossiness_grunge_amount.description: Controls the contrast of the noise pattern

material_preset.description: Select the type of metal, which utilizes the appropriate measured reflectance value.

pbr_workflow.description: Choose between metal/rough or specular/glossiness PBR workflow

roughness_grunge_amount.description: Controls the contrast of the noise pattern

user_ao.description: Add user defined ambient occlusion map

pbr_basecolor_metallic_validate_2.description:
Utility for validating PBR data. Ranges checked are albedo dark values and metal reflectance. 

Albedo Mode: checks dark ranges in the diffuse reflected color
Albedo Dark Range Threshold:
No dark values below 30 sRGB 
No dark values below 50 sRGB

Metal Mode: Checks the specular range for the metal reflectance value
Metal Reflectance Range:
155-255 sRGB (60-100% reflective) 
180-255 sRGB (70-100% reflective) 

Combined Mode:
Shows both the albedo and metal data. 

Overlay Map:
Will the validation over the map data.

albedo_range.description:
Albedo dark value threshold:
No dark values below 30 sRGB 
No dark values below 50 sRGB

metal_reflectance_range.description:
Ranges for metal reflectance:
155-255 sRGB (60-100% reflective) 
180-255 sRGB (70-100% reflective)

overlay_maps.description: Sow the validation over the maps

pbr_dielectric_f0.description:
F0 preset values for common dielectric materials for use with the specular/glossiness workflow. 
These presets fall within 2-5% reflective (0.02 - 0.05 - linear). 
Use Custom IOR to enter an IOR. The F0 will be output.

ior.description:
Enter an IOR value to produce F0.
Default value set to plastic.

specular_values.description: Choose preset F0 value for common dielectrics

pbr_metal_reflectance.description:
Presets of metal reflectance colors to be used with PBR shaders.
Usage:
- Specular with the diffuse/specular shader
- BaseColor with baseColor/metallic shader

pbr_render.description: Renders a PBR material onto a sphere, plane or cylinder using Image Based Lighting (IBL).

diffuse_emissive_multiplier.description: Controls how much the emissive parts are contributing to the irradiance.

recompute_normals.description: Normals will be recomputed from the height map according to the displacement intensity.

perlin_noise.description: Generates a Perlin noise, a widely used smooth distribution of grayscale values.

output.description: The generated noise as a grayscale bitmap.

disorder.description:
Displaces the ingredients of the noise.

This can be used to animate the noise.

disorder_speed.description:
Adjusts the distance of displacement applied by the Disorder parameter.

This can be used to control the speed of displacement when animating the noise.

non_square_expansion.description: In non-square images, keeps the generated tile square and expands the noise generation to the image's bounds.

scale.description:
The subdivision of the grid used to generate the noise tiles.

A higher value results in more tiles being drawn and a denser noise.

tile_offset.description: Controls the position of the portion of infinite plane used to render the noise.

point_list.description:
Generates a list of points to be traversed by a spline.

If an existing point list is supplied to the Point inputs, the generated list is appended to the input list.

point_list_input.description:
An input list of points encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image.

R - X position
G - Y position
B - Height
A - Packed data:

  • Integer part: Smoothness;
  • Fractional part: Thickness.

preview.description: The preview of points as a grayscale image.

point_list.description:
The output list of points encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image.

R - X position
G - Y position
B - Z position
A - Packed data:

  • Integer part: Smoothness;
  • Fractional part: Thickness.

preview.description: The preview of the points as a grayscale image.

global_smoothness_adjustment.description:
Applies a uniform offset to the smoothness value of all points.

The resulting smoothness value is clamped to the [0;1] range.

labels_size.description: The size of the label for each point in texture space, where 0.1 is a tenth of the texture's width.

p1.description: Sets the position of the p# point in texture space.

p10.description: Sets the position of the p# point in texture space.

p10_properties.description:
Sets the properties of the p# point:

  • Height: Adjusts the height of p# where a lower value means a lower or deeper location;
  • Smoothness: Offsets the start of the smoothing of the spline at p#, where a value of 0 results in a hard trajectory and 1 in an entirely smooth one;
  • Thickness: Adjusts the thickness of the spline at p#. Thickness is used by specific Spline nodes.

  • Thickness: Adjusts the thickness of the spline at p#. Thickness is used by specific Spline nodes.
