points_size.description: The radius of the points in texture space, where 0.1 is a tenth of the texture's width.

show_labels.description: For each point, displays the point's name next to it in the Preview output.

polar_to_cartesian.description: Transform the image by converting polar to cartesian coordinates.

polar_to_cartesian_grayscale.description: Transform the image by converting polar to cartesian coordinates.

premult_to_straight.description: Pre-Multiplied Alpha to Srtaight Alpha conversion.

culling.description: Specify the culling mode to use when the tranformed image is wrapped. The "Back" is the part of the image that is mirrored relative to the input image, and the "Front" is the part that is not mirrored.

enable_tiling.description: Choose whether the part of the result image that are out of the deformation cage should be transparent (False) or contains a replication of the input image (True). Please note this parameter controls the tiling of the input, not the one of the output.

culling.description: Specify the culling mode to use when the tranformed image is wrapped. The "Back" is the part of the image that is mirrored relative to the input image, and the "Front" is the part that is not mirrored.

enable_tiling.description: Choose whether the part of the result image that are out of the deformation cage should be transparent (False) or contains a replication of the input image (True). Please note this parameter controls the tiling of the input, not the one of the output.

quad_transform_path.description:
Deforms Paths as a quadrilateral using a handle on each corner.

Note: See the Paths Format Specifications page in Designer's documentation to learn about the method used to encode Paths.

paths.description:
Contains a list of Paths which describe lists of segments, encoded in a color image.

Connect this input to the result of a Mask to Paths or to another Path-processing node.

The transformed Paths. You can use the Preview Paths node to visualize the result of the transformation.

The result can be processed using another Paths-processing node, or sent to a Paths to Spline node to further process it as Splines.

quantize_color.description:
Reduces the amount of colors in a color image, effectively flattening gradients.

In addition to the processed image, the node also extracts the following:

  • A palette of the remaining colors, which may be used to colorize other images
  • An ID map of the quantized areas, which may be used to recolorize the processed image using a different palette
  • The amount of remaining colors as a raw integer value

If the Ignore alpha parameter is set to False, the alpha channel of the original image is used to select the areas of the image from which the colors should be extracted for the quantization process, while colors in transparent areas are ignored.
This effectively provides some control over the extracted colors.

This node may be used in combination with the following nodes: Create Color Palette, Apply Color Palette, Modify Color Palette, View Color Palette.

input.description: The color image which should be quantized.

id.description:
A map where each quantized color is assigned a unique integer identifier.

This may be used to:

  • Extract a mask out of some quantized areas with the ID to Mask node
  • Recolorize the quantized image with the Apply Color Palette or Modify Color Palette nodes

output.description: The quantized color image.

palette.description:
The palette extracted from the image, holding the remaining colors after quantization.

The image is an ordered list of RGB colors encoded as a row of pixels, and can hold a maximum of 256 colors.

The palette may be visualized with the View Color Palette node.

palette_color_amount.description: The amount of colors stored in the palette.

color_amount.description:
The maximum amount of colors which should be used in the quantized image.

This amount is the same used in the Palette extracted from the image.

'Maximum' means this amount may not be met, because of the quantization technique being used. Check the Palette Color Amount output for the actual amount of extracted colors.

contour_smoothing.description:
Controls the radius of a smoothing effect applied to the input image, used to simplify the quantized image into more solid, cohesive shapes.

Note: This smoothing requires intensive computations thus raising this value noticeably increases the node's computation time.

distance_color_space.description:
Colors are arranged in a cube which width, height and depth are a gradient where each component of a color increases from 0 to 1 (E.g. red, green and blue in RGB).

The quantization process involves selecting the defining colors in an image, then finding the colors closest to them in the cube and replacing them with that defining color.

This parameter lets you select the color space used to distribute colors in the cube, which changes the result of the quantization by changing the criteria for detecting a defining color and rearranging neighbouring colors.

You may select the color space which fits your use case:

  • Lab (Color): A standardized perceptual color space, which distributes colors in such a way that colors that 'feel' close are actually close in the cube. This is appropriate for image which may be visualised on displays
  • RGB (Data): Color is split into Red, Green and Blue and distributed straight along those axis, disregarding human perception. This is appropriate for images holding raw data, such as normal maps

dithering.description:
Applies a dithering pattern in order to recreate the gradients and color blends in the original image, while still only using the colors remaining after the quantization.

Make sure to use a Contour smoothing value of 0 to produce the expected dithering effect.

dithering_pattern.description:
The dithering pattern used to recreate the gradients and color blends in the original image:

  • Blue noise
  • Bayer

downscale_filtering.description:
The color quantization process involves computing a histogram of an image at a reduced size (I.e. downscaled), in order to sort its colors by importance.

This parameter controls the method of filtering the downscaled image before computing its histogram:

  • Bilinear: applies bilinear filtering to the image, resulting in a histogram with interpolated colors which may not be part of the original image, diluting some of the original colors. This helps with images using lots of colors.
  • Nearest: samples the color of the nearest pixel with no filtering, resulting in a histogram using colors from the original image exclusively. This is appropriate for images using few colors.

id_sort_mode.description:
Colors are arranged in a cube where width, height and depth are a gradient where each component of a color increases from 0 to 1 (E.g. red, green and blue in RGB).

This parameter selects the method used to order the list of colors in the extracted palette, and the indexes in the areas of the extracted ID map:

  • Z-curve: colors are sorted by next found in the color cube using a Z-curve, from white to black
  • Hue: colors are sorted by closest hue
  • Representativity: colors are sorted from most to least used in the quantized image

ignore_alpha.description:
By default, the alpha channel of the original image is used to select the areas of the image from which the colors should be extracted for the quantization process, while colors in transparent areas are ignored.
This effectively provides some control over the extracted colors.

Indeed, you may wish to only use the colors in the visible parts of the image for the quantization process.

This toggle lets you disable this masking and use the full image regardless of transparency.

quantize_grayscale.description: Approximates the input range to a predefined number of values. Effectively introduces banding.

curve_input.description:
When the Slope Curve parameter is set to Curve input, this input map is used to describe the curve applied to the slope gradient in the transitions between approximated values.

Also, the Slope parameter value should be greater than 0 for this input to have any effect.

You may use a Curve node to describe this curve with a great amount of control.

input.description: The input grayscale image which should be approximated to a limited set of grayscale values.

output.description: The output image.

offset.description: Applies an offset to the input range, which shifts the results along the range.

slope.description: Applies a slope gradient to the transitions between approximated values, up to the full span of a step.

slope_curve.description:
Sets the method of acquiring the curve for the slope set by the Slope parameter:

- Linear: Applies a linear curve, resulting in a straight slope
- Smoothstep: Applies a smoothstep curve, resulting in a smooth slope
- Curve input: Applies the curve described by the Curve Input input map. You may use a Curve node to describe this curve with a great amount of control.

steps.description: The number of separate values the input range should be approximated to.

reaction_diffusion.description: Reaction/Diffusion approximation based on input image.

replace_color.description:
Allow to get a visual control on HSL
The HSL will be set in function of the difference between source and target colors.

replace_color_range.description: Modify the selected color in function of a target color.

rgb_a_merge.description: Merge an RGB image and a grayscale image into a  RGBA image.

rgb_a_split.description: Separate an RGBA image entry into an RGB image and an Alpha image.

rgba_merge.description: Merge 4 grayscale images entry in a RGBA image

rgba_split.description: Separate a RGBA image entry in 4 grayscale images

rock_weathering.description: This filter add a weathering effect on a rock material.

AO.description: Here, input a mesh baked ambient occlusion map.

Curvature.description: Here, input a mesh baked curvature map.

Input_12.description: Here, input a mask, the effect will be applied on the white area of the mask. The mask also help to avoid seams discontinuity.

Input_2.description: Here, input an ambient occlusion map.

Normal_Wold_Space.description: Here, Input a mesh baked world space normal map.

basecolor_1.description: Here, input a base color map.

age.description: Sets the age of the rock.

age_threshlod.description: Sets the position threshold of the age effect.

ambient_occlusion.description: Sets On/Off the ambient occlusion channel.

ambient_occlusion_intensity.description: Sets the ambient occlusion map blend intensity.

basecolor.description: Sets On/Off the base color channel.

cracks_intensity.description: Sets the intensity and deepness of the cracks.

cracks_scale.description: Sets the size of the cracks.

diffuse_intensity.description: Sets the diffuse map blend intensity.

dirtiness.description: Sets how dirty the rock is.

dust.description: Sets the amount of dust on the rock.

edges_wearing.description: Sets the amount of edges wearing.

glossiness_intensity.description: Sets the glossiness map blend intensity.

height_intensity.description: Sets the height map blend intensity.

normal_format.description: Sets the type of normal map input(s) and output(s).

normal_intensity.description: Sets the normal map blend intensity.

roughness_intensity.description: Sets the roughness map blend intensity.

sharp_edges_scratches_scale.description: Sets the shape variation scale for the edges wearing.

sharp_edges_scratches_warp_intensity.description: Sets the shape variation intensity for the edges wearing.

specular_intensity.description: Sets the specular map blend intensity.

used_rock.description: Sets the wearing of the rock.

used_rock_brightness.description: Sets the rock sub-layer brightness level.

used_rock_desaturation.description: Sets the rock sub-layer saturation level.

contrast.description: Set the diffuse map contrast

normal_intensity.description: Set the normal map strength intensity. 0 = Null 1 = Maximum Intensity

rt_ao.description:
Generates an Ambient Occlusion map based on a height map input.

This filter gives more accurate results compared to the HBAO but should not be used in combination with the CPU (SSE) engine due to computation time.

distribution.description: Distribution of the rays.Cosine produces a physically accurate result.

height_scale.description: Scale of the input height map.

max_distance.description: Sets the maximum distance rays can travel to be occluded.

samples.description: Number of rays used to compute the Ambient Occlusion.

spread_angle.description: Sets the spreading angle for the rays to be shot at. A value of 1 is a full hemisphere.

use_physical_size.description: Toggles the Physical size settings on or off.

rt_bent_normal.description:
Generates a Bent Normal Map based on a height map input.
This node should not be used in combination with the CPU (SSE) engine due to computation time.

samples.description: Number of rays used to compute the Ambient Occlusion.

rt_caustics_color.description: Generates projected caustics based on a height map and a light direction.

rt_caustics_grayscale.description: Generates projected caustics based on a height map and a light direction.

rt_irradiance.description:
Generates ray traced irradiance on a height map input generated from an environment map and an emissive map.
This node should not be used in combination with the CPU (SSE) engine due to computation time.

ibl_shadows_in_raw_irradiance_alpha.display_name: IBL shadows in raw irradiance alpha

rt_shadows.description:
Generates ray traced shadows from a height map input.
This node should not be used in combination with the CPU (SSE) engine due to computation time.

ambientOcclusion.description: Here, Input an Ambient Occlusion Map.

ambientOcclusion_1.description: Here, input an ambient occlusion map.

basecolor_1.description: Here input a base color map.

diffuse_1.description: Here input a diffuse or a albedo color map.

mask_1.description: Here, input a mask, the effect will be applied on the white area of the mask. The mask also help to avoid seams discontinuity.

normal_1.description: Here, input a tangent space normal map.

position.description: Here, Input a mesh position map.

ambient_occlusion_intensity.description: Sets the AO map blend intensity.

basecolor.description: Sets On/Off the base color channel.

diffuse_intensity.description: Sets the diffuse map blend intensity.

glossiness_intensity.description: Sets the glossiness map blend intensity.

height_intensity.description: Sets the height map blend intensity.

metallic_intensity.description: Sets the metallic map blend intensity.

normal_format.description: Sets the type of normal map input(s) and output(s).

normal_intensity.description: Sets the normal map blend intensity.

roughness_intensity.description: Sets the roughness map blend intensity.

specular_intensity.description: Sets the specular map blend intensity.

safe_transform.description:
Tiling safe transform node.
- pixel snap offset
- tile safe rotation

safe_transform_grayscale.description:
Tiling safe transform node.
- pixel snap offset
- tile safe rotation

scatter_on_spline_color.description:
Draws the specified color pattern(s) along the input splines over the input background.

The node offers deep customization options for controlling how patterns are scattered.

Some aspects of the scattering may be controlled using images from other nodes in the graph to extend the dynamic aspect of the result.

background.description: The color image over which splines should be drawn.

color_map.description:
The color map controlling the color of the scattered patterns.

The effect of this map is controlled by the Color Input Multiplier parameter and is combined with other parameters in the Color group.

mask_map.description:
The grayscale map controlling the masking of the scattered patterns.

The effect of this map is controlled by the Mask Map Threshold parameter and is combined with other parameters in the Color group.

pattern_input_1.description: A color pattern that should be scattered along the splines.

pattern_input_2.description: A color pattern that should be scattered along the splines.

pattern_input_3.description: A color pattern that should be scattered along the splines.

pattern_input_4.description: A color pattern that should be scattered along the splines.

pattern_input_5.description: A color pattern that should be scattered along the splines.

pattern_input_6.description: A color pattern that should be scattered along the splines.

pattern_input_7.description: A color pattern that should be scattered along the splines.

pattern_input_8.description: A color pattern that should be scattered along the splines.

scale_map.description:
The grayscale map controlling the scale of the scattered patterns.

The effect of this map is controlled by the Scale Map Input Multiplier parameter and is combined with the other parameters in the Size group.

spline_coords.description:
The coordinates of the input splines’ points encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - X position
G - Y position
B - Height
A - Packed data:

  • Sign: Spline is closed (negative) or open (positive).
  • Absolute value: Thickness + 1

spline_data.description:
Additional data of the input splines encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - Tangents X
G - Tangents Y
B - Unused
A - Unused

output.description: The color image representing the Pattern Input(s) scattered along the input Splines over the input Background.

background_color.description: The color of the background in the output image.

blend_mode.description:
The method of blending together the colors of patterns with both the background and other overlapping patterns:

  • Add: Adds the colors together;
  • Alpha Blend: Applies a simple transparency blending using the pattern's alpha channel. Patterns drawn last are in front.

color_map_input_multiplier.description:
Controls the intensity of the Color Map input. This map acts as a multiplier for the current color of the patterns.

The effect of this map is combined with the other parameters in the Color group.

Note: The output color is the weighted result of all color multipliers.

color_map_input_sampling_mode.description:
The method of mapping the values in the Color Map to the splines:

  • Texture space: The values are applied to the splines where they would be if placed in a texture using the texture’s UV coordinates. This effectively applies the value to the splines ‘in place’;
  • Horizontal along spline: The values are applied to the encoded splines’ coordinates directly (see Spline Coords input), where each row is applied to a different spline from top to bottom;
  • Hor. along spline (rand. offset X): The values are applied to the encoded splines’ coordinates directly (see Spline Coords input), with a random horizontal offset in the Color Map for each spline (I.e., each row in Spline Coords);
  • Hor. along spline (rand. offset Y): The values are applied to the encoded splines’ coordinates directly (see Spline Coords input), with a random vertical offset in the Color Map for each spline (I.e., each row in Spline Coords).

color_mode.description:
The method of blending selecting the color of each pattern:

  • Base Color: The Base Color is applied to all patterns;
  • Position: The position of the pattern in texture space is used to drive its color so that the X and Y coordinates are mapped to the red and green channels respectively.

duplicates_distribution_mode.description:
The method used to place the duplicate patterns:

  • Linear: duplicates are evenly spaced along the spline’s normal from the pattern’s original location;
  • Circular: duplicated are arranged along a virtual circle centered on the spline at the pattern’s original location.

duplicates_local_offset.description:
Applies an offset to the duplicates’ positions along the spline’s tangent (parallel) and normal (perpendicular).

Duplicates on opposite sides of the spline are moved in opposite directions.

duplicates_offset_center.description: Applies an offset to the duplicates along the spline on X (parallel) and Y (perpendicular).

duplicates_offset_distance.description: The radius of the virtual circle along which duplicates are distributed.

duplicates_offset_start_end_attenuation.description:
Factors in the distance from the midpoint of the spline to its Start and End, when applying offsets to duplicates.

This means offsets are decreased for duplicates closer to a spline’s extremities.

duplicates_rotation.description: Rotates the virtual circle along which duplicates are distributed.

duplicates_spread_angle.description: The arc of the virtual circle along which duplicates are distributed, as the angle of that arc where 1 is the full circle.

end.description:
Offsets the point from the start of a spline where the scattering ends.

The value is the normalized length of each spline.

first_pattern_input_index.description: The index of the pattern that should be placed at the start of each spline.

invert_mask_map.description: Inverts the values of the Mask Map using a One minus operation (1 - x).

last_pattern_input_index.description: The index of the pattern that should be placed at the end of each spline.

local_offset.description: Applies an offset to the patterns’ positions along the spline’s tangent (parallel) and normal (perpendicular).

local_offset_attenuation_by_thickness.description:
Factors in the spline’s thickness when applying offsets to patterns.

This means offsets are decreased for duplicates on a portion of a spline with a lower thickness.

local_offset_random.description: Applies an additional random offset to the patterns’ positions along the spline’s tangent (parallel) and normal (perpendicular).

local_offset_random_center.description: Offsets the center of the random offset applied by the Local Offset Random parameter along the spline’s tangent (parallel) and normal (perpendicular).

local_offset_start_end_attenuation.description:
Factors in the distance from the midpoint of the spline to its Start and End when applying position offsets to the patterns.

This means offsets are decreased for patterns closer to a spline’s extremities.

mask_map_input_sampling_mode.description:
The method of mapping the values in the Mask Map to the splines:

  • Texture space: The values are applied to the splines where they would be if placed in a texture using the texture’s UV coordinates. This effectively applies the value to the splines ‘in place’;
  • Horizontal along spline: The values are applied to the encoded splines’ coordinates directly (see Spline Coords input), where each row is applied to a different spline from top to bottom;
  • Hor. along spline (rand. offset X): The values are applied to the encoded splines’ coordinates directly (see Spline Coords input), with a random horizontal offset in the Mask Map for each spline (I.e., each row in Spline Coords);
  • Hor. along spline (rand. offset Y): The values are applied to the encoded splines’ coordinates directly (see Spline Coords input), with a random vertical offset in the Mask Map for each spline (I.e., each row in Spline Coords).

mask_map_threshold.description:
Values in the Mask Map below this threshold value are processed as black, while values above the threshold are processed as white.

This means all patterns in areas of the Mask Map below this value will be masked.

mask_random.description: Adjusts the range of the random masking of patterns, where 0 means no patterns are masked and 1 means all patterns are.

non_square_correction.description:
Adjust the points’ positions and thickness to retain the spline shape in non-square resolutions.

This also impacts uniform distribution.

offset_attenuation_by_thickness.description:
Factors in the spline’s thickness when applying offsets to duplicates.

This means offsets are decreased for duplicates on a portion of a spline with a lower thickness.

offset_on_spline.description: Applies a position offset to the patterns along the splines.

override_first_pattern.description: Manually select the index of the pattern that should be placed at the start of each spline.

override_last_pattern.description: Manually select the index of the pattern that should be placed at the end of each spline.

pattern.description:
The pattern which should be scattered along the splines:

  • Pattern Input: Use the patterns supplied to the Pattern Input # inputs;
  • Square;
  • Disc;
  • Paraboloid;
  • Bell;
  • Gaussian;
  • Thorn;
  • Pyramid;
  • Brick;
  • Gradation;
  • Waves;
  • Half Bell;
  • Ridged Bell;
  • Crescent;
  • Capsule;
  • Cone;
  • Gradation w. offset;
  • Hemisphere.

pattern_distribution_jiterring.description: Randomly increases or decreases the selected index of patterns on the spline.

pattern_input_distribution.description:
The method used to select which of the input patterns should be scattered on a given spline:

  • Random: A pattern is randomly selected;
  • Along Spline: The pattern index increases gradually along the spline;
  • Pattern Index: Loops over the index of input patterns along each spline;
  • Spline Index: Loops over the index of input patterns from one spline to the next in the list of input splines.

pattern_input_number.description: Selects the index of the input pattern which should be scattered.

pattern_scale.description:
A global control for the size of all patterns, where 1 is the full span of the image.

Scaling is applied relatively to a pattern’s pivot. The pivot position can be offset using the Shape Pivot parameter.

pattern_scale_start_end_attenuation.description:
Factors in the distance from the midpoint of the spline to its Start and End when scaling the patterns.

This means size is decreased for patterns closer to a spline’s extremities.

pattern_size.description: The original size of patterns in X and Y before other adjustments are made by other parameters.

pattern_variation.description: The intensity of the variation specific to the current pattern.

random_alpha_luminance.description:
Applies a random multiplier up to the specified value for decreasing the alpha of the patterns.

Note: The output color is the weighted result of all color multipliers.

random_color.description:
Applies a random offset up to the specified values to the patterns’ colors in HSV space, as well as their alpha.

Note: The output color is the weighted result of all color multipliers.

random_color_center.description:
Applies an offset to the range of the random offset applied in Random Color

A value of -1 means all random values are higher and a value of 1 means all random values are lower.

random_color_luminance.description:
Applies a random multiplier up to the specified value for decreasing the luminance of the patterns.

Note: The output color is the weighted result of all color multipliers.

random_offset_on_spline.description: Applies an additional position offset to the patterns along the splines.

rotation_align_with_tangent.description: Rotates the patterns to match the direction of the spline at their location.

rotation_around_center_random.description: Applies an additional random rotation to the patterns around their center.

rotation_around_center_random_center.description: Rotates the center of the random rotations applied by the Rotation Random parameter around the pattern’s center.

rotation_around_pivot.description:
Rotates the patterns around their pivots.

The pivot position can be offset using the Shape Pivot parameter.

rotation_around_pivot_random.description:
Applies an additional random rotation to the patterns around their pivots.

The pivot position can be offset using the Shape Pivot parameter.

rotation_around_pivot_random_center.description: Rotates around the pattern’s pivots the center of the random rotations applied by the Rotation Random parameter.

scale_input_sampling_mode.description:
The method of mapping the values in the Scale Map to the splines:

  • Texture space: The values are applied to the splines where they would be if placed in a texture using the texture’s UV coordinates. This effectively applies the value to the splines ‘in place’;
  • Horizontal along spline: The values are applied to the encoded splines’ coordinates directly (see Spline Coords input), where each row is applied to a different spline from top to bottom;
  • Hor. along spline (rand. offset X): The values are applied to the encoded splines’ coordinates directly (see Spline Coords input), with a random horizontal offset in the Scale Map for each spline (I.e., each row in Spline Coords);
  • Hor. along spline (rand. offset Y): The values are applied to the encoded splines’ coordinates directly (see Spline Coords input), with a random vertical offset in the Scale Map for each spline (I.e., each row in Spline Coords).

scale_map_input_multiplier.description:
Controls the intensity of the Scale Map input. This map acts as a multiplier for the current size of the patterns.

The effect of this map is combined with the other parameters in the Size group.

scale_random.description: Applies a random multiplier up to the specified value for decreasing the size of the patterns.

scatter_mode.description:
The method of scattering the patterns along the splines, which impacts the amount of patterns on each spline:

  • Shape Amount: The specified amount of evenly spaced patterns is scattered;
  • Shape Spacing: The number of patterns is automatically adjusted to fit the specified even spacing.

In both cases, the first and last patterns fall exactly on the start and end of each spline respectively.

shape_amount.description: The amount of evenly spaced patterns scattered along each spline.

shape_distribution_along_spline.description:
The method of distributing the patterns along a spline:

  • From Source: The spacing of the patterns is influenced by the spline point’s tangents, where shapes are further apart near points with long tangents;
  • Uniform: The patterns are evenly spaced along the spline regardless of its tangents and trajectory.

shape_index_alpha_multiplier.description:
The intensity by which the alpha of each pattern is multiplied against its normalized index.

Note: The output color is the weighted result of all color multipliers.

shape_index_color_multiplier.description:
The intensity by which the color of each pattern is multiplied against its normalized index.

Note: The output color is the weighted result of all color multipliers.

shape_pivot.description:
Offsets the X and Y coordinates of patterns' pivots in spline tangent space.

Considering the pivot is what is placed on the spline, this effectively offsets the patterns along or perpendicularly to the spline.

Note: The positions of the pivots impacts the effect of the Scale and Rotation (Pivot) parameters.

shape_scale_alpha_multiplier.description:
The intensity by which the alpha of each pattern is multiplied against its scale.

Note: The output color is the weighted result of all color multipliers.

shape_scale_color_multiplier.description:
The intensity by which the color of each pattern is multiplied against its scale.

Note: The output color is the weighted result of all color multipliers.

shape_spacing.description: The minimum distance along a spline by which patterns should be spaced, while still landing the first and last pattern on the start and end of each spline respectively.

size_mode.description:
The method of setting the size of the scattered patterns:

  • Normal: Size is controlled uniformly using a global Scale parameter;
  • Use Thickness from Spline: Size is driven by the spline’s thickness.

size_random.description: Applies a random multiplier up to the specified value for reducing the size of the patterns in X and Y.

size_thickness_affects.description:
Specifies which axis of a pattern’s scale should be driven by the spline’s thickness:

  • X & Y: Thickness is multiplied against the size in both the X and Y axes;
  • X: Thickness is multiplied against the size on the X axis only;
  • Y: Thickness is multiplied against the size on the Y axis only.

When not multiplied, the pattern’s original scale is the full span of the image.

This means in the X mode, the size in the Y axis is the full span of the image and needs to be tweaked using the Size parameter. The same applies for size in the X axis when using the Y mode.

sline_range.description: The range of indexes of the splines which should be used for scattering patterns.

spline_height_alpha_multiplier.description:
The intensity by which the alpha of each pattern is multiplied against the height of the spline at its location.

Note: The output color is the weighted result of all color multipliers.

spline_height_color_multiplier.description:
The intensity by which the color of each pattern is multiplied against the height of the spline at its location.

Note: The output color is the weighted result of all color multipliers.

spline_index.description: The index of the spline which should be used for scattering patterns.

spline_input.description:
The method of selecting which splines should be used for scattering patterns:

  • Spline List: Use all splines in the input list;
  • Single Spline: Use only the specified spline from the input list;
  • Spline Range: Use only the splines in the specified range from the input list.

spline_thickness_alpha_multiplier.description:
The intensity by which the alpha of each pattern is multiplied against the thickness of the spline at its location.

Note: The output color is the weighted result of all color multipliers.

spline_thickness_color_multiplier.description:
The intensity by which the color of each pattern is multiplied against the thickness of the spline at its location.

Note: The output color is the weighted result of all color multipliers.

start.description:
Offsets the point from the start of a spline where the scattering starts.

The value is the normalized length of each spline.

thickness_scale.description: An additional multiplier for the scale of the patterns when driven by the spline’s thickness.

scatter_on_spline_grayscale.description:
Draws the specified grayscale pattern(s) along the input splines over the input background.

The node offers deep customization options for controlling how patterns are scattered.

Some aspects of the scattering may be controlled using images from other nodes in the graph to extend the dynamic aspect of the result.

background.description: The grayscale image over which splines should be drawn.

height_map.description:
The grayscale map controlling the height of the scattered patterns.

The effect of this map is controlled by the Height Input Multiplier parameter and is combined with other parameters in the Color group.

mask_map.description:
The grayscale map controlling the masking of the scattered patterns.

The effect of this map is controlled by the Mask Map Threshold parameter and is combined with other parameters in the Color group.

pattern_input_1.description: A grayscale pattern that should be scattered along the splines.

pattern_input_2.description: A grayscale pattern that should be scattered along the splines.

pattern_input_3.description: A grayscale pattern that should be scattered along the splines.

pattern_input_4.description: A grayscale pattern that should be scattered along the splines.

pattern_input_5.description: A grayscale pattern that should be scattered along the splines.

pattern_input_6.description: A grayscale pattern that should be scattered along the splines.

pattern_input_7.description: A grayscale pattern that should be scattered along the splines.

pattern_input_8.description: A grayscale pattern that should be scattered along the splines.

scale_map.description:
The grayscale map controlling the scale of the scattered patterns.

The effect of this map is controlled by the Scale Map Input Multiplier parameter and is combined with the other parameters in the Size group.

spline_coords.description:
The coordinates of the input splines’ points encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - X position
G - Y position
B - Height
A - Packed data:

  • Sign: Spline is closed (negative) or open (positive).
  • Absolute value: Thickness + 1

spline_data.description:
Additional data of the input splines encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - Tangents X
G - Tangents Y
B - Unused
A - Unused

output.description: The grayscale image representing the Pattern Input(s) scattered along the input Splines over the input Background.

blend_mode.description:
The method of blending together the colors of patterns with both the background and other overlapping patterns:

  • Add: Add the colors together;
  • Max: Use the brightest color.

duplicates_distribution_mode.description:
The method used to place the duplicate patterns:

  • Linear: duplicates are evenly spaced along the spline’s normal from the pattern’s original location;
  • Circular: duplicated are arranged along a virtual circle centered on the spline at the pattern’s original location.

duplicates_local_offset.description:
Applies an offset to the duplicates’ positions along the spline’s tangent (parallel) and normal (perpendicular).

Duplicates on opposite sides of the spline are moved in opposite directions.

duplicates_offset_center.description: Applies an offset to the duplicates along the spline on X (parallel) and Y (perpendicular).

duplicates_offset_distance.description: The radius of the virtual circle along which duplicates are distributed.

duplicates_offset_start_end_attenuation.description:
Factors in the distance from the midpoint of the spline to its Start and End, when applying offsets to duplicates.

This means offsets are decreased for duplicates closer to a spline’s extremities.

duplicates_rotation.description: Rotates the virtual circle along which duplicates are distributed.

duplicates_spread_angle.description: The arc of the virtual circle along which duplicates are distributed, as the angle of that arc where 1 is the full circle.

end.description:
Offsets the point from the start of a spline where the scattering ends.

The value is the normalized length of each spline.

first_pattern_input_index.description: The index of the pattern that should be placed at the start of each spline.

height_map_input_multiplier.description:
Controls the intensity of the Height Map input. This map acts as a multiplier for the current luminance of the patterns.

The effect of this map is combined with the other parameters in the Color group.

Note: The output color is the weighted result of all color multipliers.

height_map_input_sampling_mode.description:
The method of mapping the values in the Height Map to the splines:

  • Texture space: The values are applied to the splines where they would be if placed in a texture using the texture’s UV coordinates. This effectively applies the value to the splines ‘in place’;
  • Horizontal along spline: The values are applied to the encoded splines’ coordinates directly (see Spline Coords input), where each row is applied to a different spline from top to bottom;
  • Hor. along spline (rand. offset X): The values are applied to the encoded splines’ coordinates directly (see Spline Coords input), with a random horizontal offset in the Height Map for each spline (I.e., each row in Spline Coords);
  • Hor. along spline (rand. offset Y): The values are applied to the encoded splines’ coordinates directly (see Spline Coords input), with a random vertical offset in the Height Map for each spline (I.e., each row in Spline Coords).

hemisphere_height_mode.description:
The effect of the spline’s height on a Hemisphere pattern scattered onto it:

  • Offset: the spline height is added to the height of the hemisphere;
  • Scale: the spline height is multiplied against the height of the hemisphere.

invert_mask_map.description: Inverts the values of the Mask Map using a One minus operation (1 - x).

last_pattern_input_index.description: The index of the pattern that should be placed at the end of each spline.

local_offset.description: Applies an offset to the patterns’ positions along the spline’s tangent (parallel) and normal (perpendicular).

local_offset_attenuation_by_thickness.description:
Factors in the spline’s thickness when applying offsets to patterns.

This means offsets are decreased for duplicates on a portion of a spline with a lower thickness.

local_offset_random.description: Applies an additional random offset to the patterns’ positions along the spline’s tangent (parallel) and normal (perpendicular).

local_offset_random_center.description: Offsets the center of the random offset applied by the Local Offset Random parameter along the spline’s tangent (parallel) and normal (perpendicular).

local_offset_start_end_attenuation.description:
Factors in the distance from the midpoint of the spline to its Start and End when applying position offsets to the patterns.

This means offsets are decreased for patterns closer to a spline’s extremities.

mask_map_input_sampling_mode.description:
The method of mapping the values in the Mask Map to the splines:

  • Texture space: The values are applied to the splines where they would be if placed in a texture using the texture’s UV coordinates. This effectively applies the value to the splines ‘in place’;
  • Horizontal along spline: The values are applied to the encoded splines’ coordinates directly (see Spline Coords input), where each row is applied to a different spline from top to bottom;
  • Hor. along spline (rand. offset X): The values are applied to the encoded splines’ coordinates directly (see Spline Coords input), with a random horizontal offset in the Mask Map for each spline (I.e., each row in Spline Coords);
  • Hor. along spline (rand. offset Y): The values are applied to the encoded splines’ coordinates directly (see Spline Coords input), with a random vertical offset in the Mask Map for each spline (I.e., each row in Spline Coords).

mask_map_threshold.description:
Values in the Mask Map below this threshold value are processed as black, while values above the threshold are processed as white.

This means all patterns in areas of the Mask Map below this value will be masked.

mask_random.description: Adjusts the range of the random masking of patterns, where 0 means no patterns are masked and 1 means all patterns are.

non_square_correction.description:
Adjust the points’ positions and thickness to retain the spline shape in non-square resolutions.

This also impacts uniform distribution.

offset_attenuation_by_thickness.description:
Factors in the spline’s thickness when applying offsets to duplicates.

This means offsets are decreased for duplicates on a portion of a spline with a lower thickness.

offset_on_spline.description: Applies a position offset to the patterns along the splines.

override_first_pattern.description: Manually select the index of the pattern that should be placed at the start of each spline.

override_last_pattern.description: Manually select the index of the pattern that should be placed at the end of each spline.

pattern.description:
The pattern which should be scattered along the splines:

  • Pattern Input: Use the patterns supplied to the Pattern Input # inputs;
  • Square;
  • Disc;
  • Paraboloid;
  • Bell;
  • Gaussian;
  • Thorn;
  • Pyramid;
  • Brick;
  • Gradation;
  • Waves;
  • Half Bell;
  • Ridged Bell;
  • Crescent;
  • Capsule;
  • Cone;
  • Gradation w. offset;
  • Hemisphere.

pattern_distribution_jiterring.description: Randomly increases or decreases the selected index of patterns on the spline.

pattern_input_distribution.description:
The method used to select which of the input patterns should be scattered on a given spline:

  • Random: A pattern is randomly selected;
  • Along Spline: The pattern index increases gradually along the spline;
  • Pattern Index: Loops over the index of input patterns along each spline;
  • Spline Index: Loops over the index of input patterns from one spline to the next in the list of input splines.

pattern_input_number.description: Selects the index of the input pattern which should be scattered.

pattern_scale.description:
A global control for the size of all patterns, where 1 is the full span of the image.

Scaling is applied relatively to a pattern’s pivot. The pivot position can be offset using the Shape Pivot parameter.

pattern_scale_start_end_attenuation.description:
Factors in the distance from the midpoint of the spline to its Start and End when scaling the patterns.

This means size is decreased for patterns closer to a spline’s extremities.

pattern_size.description: The original size of patterns in X and Y before other adjustments are made by other parameters.

pattern_variation.description: The intensity of the variation specific to the current pattern.

random_luminance.description:
Applies a random multiplier up to the specified value for decreasing the luminance of the patterns.

Note: The output color is the weighted result of all color multipliers.

random_offset_on_spline.description: Applies an additional position offset to the patterns along the splines.

rotation_align_with_tangent.description: Rotates the patterns to match the direction of the spline at their location.

rotation_around_center_random.description: Applies an additional random rotation to the patterns around their center.

rotation_around_center_random_center.description: Rotates the center of the random rotations applied by the Rotation Random parameter around the pattern’s center.

rotation_around_pivot.description:
Rotates the patterns around their pivots.

The pivot position can be offset using the Shape Pivot parameter.

rotation_around_pivot_random.description:
Applies an additional random rotation to the patterns around their pivots.

The pivot position can be offset using the Shape Pivot parameter.

rotation_around_pivot_random_center.description: Rotates around the pattern’s pivots the center of the random rotations applied by the Rotation Random parameter.

scale_input_sampling_mode.description:
The method of mapping the values in the Scale Map to the splines:

  • Texture space: The values are applied to the splines where they would be if placed in a texture using the texture’s UV coordinates. This effectively applies the value to the splines ‘in place’;
  • Horizontal along spline: The values are applied to the encoded splines’ coordinates directly (see Spline Coords input), where each row is applied to a different spline from top to bottom;
  • Hor. along spline (rand. offset X): The values are applied to the encoded splines’ coordinates directly (see Spline Coords input), with a random horizontal offset in the Scale Map for each spline (I.e., each row in Spline Coords);
  • Hor. along spline (rand. offset Y): The values are applied to the encoded splines’ coordinates directly (see Spline Coords input), with a random vertical offset in the Scale Map for each spline (I.e., each row in Spline Coords).

scale_map_input_multiplier.description:
Controls the intensity of the Scale Map input. This map acts as a multiplier for the current size of the patterns.

The effect of this map is combined with the other parameters in the Size group.

scale_random.description: Applies a random multiplier up to the specified value for decreasing the size of the patterns.

scatter_mode.description:
The method of scattering the patterns along the splines, which impacts the amount of patterns on each spline:

  • Shape Amount: The specified amount of evenly spaced patterns is scattered;
  • Shape Spacing: The number of patterns is automatically adjusted to fit the specified even spacing.

In both cases, the first and last patterns fall exactly on the start and end of each spline respectively.

shape_amount.description: The amount of evenly spaced patterns scattered along each spline.

shape_base_color.description:
The base color of the patterns.

Note: The output color is the weighted result of all color multipliers.

shape_base_color_multiplier.description:
The intensity of the patterns’ Shape Base Color.

Note: The output color is the weighted result of all color multipliers.

shape_distribution_along_spline.description:
The method of distributing the patterns along a spline:

  • From Source: The spacing of the patterns is influenced by the spline point’s tangents, where shapes are further apart near points with long tangents;
  • Uniform: The patterns are evenly spaced along the spline regardless of its tangents and trajectory.

shape_index_color_multiplier.description:
The intensity by which the color of each pattern is multiplied against its normalized index.

Note: The output color is the weighted result of all color multipliers.

shape_pivot.description:
Offsets the X and Y coordinates of patterns' pivots in spline tangent space.

Considering the pivot is what is placed on the spline, this effectively offsets the patterns along or perpendicularly to the spline.

Note: The positions of the pivots impacts the effect of the Scale and Rotation (Pivot) parameters.

shape_scale_color_multiplier.description:
The intensity by which the color of each pattern is multiplied against its scale.

Note: The output color is the weighted result of all color multipliers.

shape_spacing.description: The minimum distance along a spline by which patterns should be spaced, while still landing the first and last pattern on the start and end of each spline respectively.

size_mode.description:
The method of setting the size of the scattered patterns:

  • Normal: Size is controlled uniformly using a global Scale parameter;
  • Use Thickness from Spline: Size is driven by the spline’s thickness.

size_random.description: Applies a random multiplier up to the specified value for reducing the size of the patterns in X and Y.
