Specifies which axis of a pattern’s scale should be driven by the spline’s thickness:

  • X & Y: Thickness is multiplied against the size in both the X and Y axes;
  • X: Thickness is multiplied against the size on the X axis only;
  • Y: Thickness is multiplied against the size on the Y axis only.

When not multiplied, the pattern’s original scale is the full span of the image.

This means in the X mode, the size in the Y axis is the full span of the image and needs to be tweaked using the Size parameter. The same applies for size in the X axis when using the Y mode.

sline_range.description: The range of indexes of the splines which should be used for scattering patterns.

spline_height_color_multiplier.description:
The intensity by which the color of each pattern is multiplied against the height of the spline at its location.

Note: The output color is the weighted result of all color multipliers.

spline_index.description: The index of the spline which should be used for scattering patterns.

spline_input.description:
The method of selecting which splines should be used for scattering patterns:

  • Spline List: Use all splines in the input list;
  • Single Spline: Use only the specified spline from the input list;
  • Spline Range: Use only the splines in the specified range from the input list.

spline_thickness_color_multiplier.description:
The intensity by which the color of each pattern is multiplied against the thickness of the spline at its location.

Note: The output color is the weighted result of all color multipliers.

start.description:
Offsets the point from the start of a spline where the scattering starts.

The value is the normalized length of each spline.

thickness_scale.description: An additional multiplier for the scale of the patterns when driven by the spline’s thickness.

scatter_splines_on_splines.description:
Places splines along the input parent splines.

The node offers deep customization options for controlling how splines are scattered, and lets you scatter simple straight splines, or your own custom splines.

The node lets you create intricate structures for mapping colors and images using the Spline Mapper nodes, or used as a skeleton for placing shapes using the Scatter on Spline nodes.

custom_spline_coords.description:
The coordinates of the custom splines’ points encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - X position
G - Y position
B - Height
A - Packed data:

  • Sign: Spline is closed (negative) or open (positive).
  • Absolute value: Thickness + 1

custom_spline_data.description:
Additional data of the custom splines encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - Tangents X
G - Tangents Y
B - Tangents Z
A - Unused

preview.description: The visualisation of the parent splines as a grayscale image.

rotation_map.description:
The grayscale map controlling the rotation of the scattered splines.

The effect of this map is controlled by the Rotation Map Input Multiplier parameter and is combined with the other parameters in the Rotation group.

scale_map.description:
The grayscale map controlling the scale of the scattered splines.

The effect of this map is controlled by the Scale Map Input Multiplier parameter and is combined with the other parameters in the Size group.

spline_coords.description:
The coordinates of the parent splines’ points encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - X position
G - Y position
B - Height
A - Packed data:

  • Sign: Spline is closed (negative) or open (positive).
  • Absolute value: Thickness + 1

spline_data.description:
Additional data of the parent splines encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - Tangents X
G - Tangents Y
B - Tangents Z
A - Unused

preview.description: The visualisation of the scattered splines as a grayscale image.

spline_coords.description:
The coordinates of the scattered splines’ points encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - X position
G - Y position
B - Height
A - Packed data:

  • Sign: Spline is closed (negative) or open (positive).
  • Absolute value: Thickness + 1

spline_data.description:
Additional data of the scattered splines encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - Tangents X
G - Tangents Y
B - Tangents Z
A - Unused

amount_mode.description:
The method of scattering the splines along the parent splines, which impacts the amount of scattered splines on each parent spline:

  • Fixed amount per spline: The specified amount of evenly spaced splines is scattered;
  • Spacing: The amount of splines is automatically adjusted to fit the specified even spacing.

In both cases, the first and last scattered splines fall exactly on the start and end of each parent spline respectively.

background_intensity.description: The intensity of the Preview input in the Preview output visualization.

custom_spline_alignment.description:
Controls the initial orientation of the custom spline(s) on the parent splines.

  • First point tangent: The splines are oriented according to the tangent of their first point. In other words, they go off the parent splines in the direction set by their first point
  • Image space: The splines are placed as they originally appear, with no additional adjustment to their position or orientation, as if the image representing them was resting on the parent spline

custom_spline_selection.description:
When using multiple custom splines appended together into a list, this parameter lets you select how these splines should be distributed in the scattering.

  • Whole list: All splines are scattered together as a group
  • Sequential: Each individual spline is scattered in order, looping around the list
  • Random: A random spline is picked from the list for every spline scattered

end.description:
Offsets the point from the start of the parent splines where the scattering ends.

The value is the normalized length of each parent spline.

end_height.description: Sets an absolute value for the scattered spline's ending height.

end_height_mode.description:
The method of computing the ending height of the scattered splines.

  • Manual: Set the same absolute value for all scattered splines
  • From parent spline (+ custom spline): Use the height of the parent spline, then add the custom spline's height using the Custom Spline End Height Mult. parameter
  • From custom spline: Use the height of the custom spline as is

Note: Set Spline Type to Custom Spline and connect the Custom Spline inputs to use the height of custom splines.

end_height_multiplier.description:
Controls the contribution of the custom spline's own ending height to the ending height of the scattered splines, where 1 means the full height of the custom spline is used.

The custom spline's height is used differently according to the selected End Height Mode:

  • From parent spline (+ custom spline): The height is added to the parent spline's
  • From custom spline: The height is used directly

end_height_offset.description: Applies an absolute offset to the scattered spline's ending height.

end_thickness.description: Sets an absolute value for the scattered spline's ending thickness.

end_thickness_mode.description:
The method of computing the ending thickness of the scattered splines.

  • Manual: Set the same absolute value for all scattered splines
  • From parent spline: Use the thickness of the parent spline
  • From custom spline: Use the thickness of the custom spline

Note: Set Spline Type to Custom Spline and connect the Custom Spline inputs to use the thickness of custom splines.

end_thickness_multiplier.description: Scales the starting thickness of the scattered splines, where 1 is the full thickness.

end_thickness_offset.description: Applies an absolute offset to the scattered spline's ending thickness.

flip_direction.description: Inverts the direction of the scattered splines.

left_right_angle.description: Controls the angle of symmetrical rotation applied to the splines on each side of the parent splines, in number of turns.

left_right_angle_random.description: Adds a random amount of symmetrical rotation to the splines on each side of the parent splines, in number of turns.

left_right_random_link.description:
Controls whether the splines on each side of the parent spline should use the same values when using random rotation, random scaling, etc.
In other words:

  • False: each spline uses separate random values
  • True: both splines share the same random values

left_right_symmetry_mode.description:
The method of symmetry applied to the splines scattered on each side of the parent splines.

  • Disabled: No symmetry is applied, the splines are place on each side using a simple rotation
  • Left symmetry: The spline on the left is symmetrical to the one on the right relatively to the parent spline
  • Right symmetry: The spline on the right is symmetrical to the one on the left relatively to the parent spline

local_offset.description: Applies an offset to the splines’ positions along the parent spline’s tangent (parallel) and normal (perpendicular).

non_square_correction.description:
Adjust the splines positions and thickness to retain the their shape in non-square resolutions.

Note: When using custom splines, the custom spline should use the same image ratio as the Scatter Splines on Splines nodes.

offset_by_thickness.description:
Applies an offset to the scattered splines along the normal of the parent splines, up to the parent splines' thickness.

Effectively, a value of 1 lets you place the scattered splines on the surface of the parent splines' envelope.

offset_on_spline.description: Applies a position offset to the splines along the parent splines.

offset_on_spline_range.description:
Sets the range of offset applied to the scattered splines along the parent splines.

  • Interval: The range spans the interval between each scattered spline
  • Parent spline: The range spans the full length of the parent spline

pivot_absolute_position.description: The position in UV space of the pivot.

pivot_position_along_spline.description:
The normalized position of the pivot along the scattered spline, where 0 is its start and 1 is its end.

Note that the pivot follows the direction of the scattered spline and the spline's orientation may change to preserve the pivot's position and rotation relatively to the parent spline.

preview_segment_amount.description:
Adjusts the number of segments used to draw the spline visualization in the Preview output.

A higher value results in a smoother line.

preview_show_direction_helper.description: Displays a dot at the start of the spline and an arrowhead at its end in the Preview output.

random_offset_on_spline.description: Applies an additional position offset to the splines along the parent splines.

random_offset_range.description:
Sets the range of random offset applied to the scattered splines along the parent splines.

  • Interval: The range spans the interval between each scattered spline
  • Parent spline: The range spans the full length of the parent spline

rotation.description:
Rotates the splines around their pivots, in number of turns.

The pivot position can be offset using the Spline Pivot parameter.

rotation_map_input_affects.description:
Selects the rotation parameter that is impacted by the Rotation Map:

  • Spline rotation: The map impacts the splines' global rotation clockwise
  • Left / Right angle: The map impacts the Left/Right splines' symmetrical rotation

rotation_map_input_multiplier.description:
Controls the intensity of the Rotation Map input. This map acts as a multiplier for the current rotation of the patterns.

The effect of this map is combined with the other parameters in the Rotation group.

rotation_map_input_sampling_mode.description:
The method of mapping the values in the Rotation Map to the splines:

  • Texture space: The values are applied to the splines where they would be if placed in a texture using the texture’s UV coordinates. This effectively applies the value to the splines ‘in place’
  • Horizontal along spline: The values are applied to the encoded splines’ coordinates directly (see Spline Coords input), where each row is applied to a different spline from top to bottom
  • Hor. along spline (rand. offset X): The values are applied to the encoded splines’ coordinates directly (see Spline Coords input), with a random horizontal offset in the Rotation Map for each spline (I.e., each row in Spline Coords)
  • Hor. along spline (rand. offset Y): The values are applied to the encoded splines’ coordinates directly (see Spline Coords input), with a random vertical offset in the Rotation Map for each spline (I.e., each row in Spline Coords)

rotation_mode.description:
Sets the initial orientation of the scattered splines.

  • From spline: The splines are oriented to match the normal of the parent splines at their location
  • Absolute: The splines are all oriented the same way, regardless of the parent splines' direction

rotation_random.description:
Applies an additional random rotation to the splines around their pivots, in number of turns.

The pivot position can be offset using the Spline Pivot parameter.

scale.description:
A global control for the size of all splines, where 1 is their full original size.

Scaling is applied relatively to a spline’s pivot. The pivot position can be offset using the Spline Pivot parameter.

scale_map_input_multiplier.description:
Controls the intensity of the Scale Map input. This map acts as a multiplier for the current size of the patterns.

The effect of this map is combined with the other parameters in the Size group.

scale_map_input_sampling_mode.description:
The method of mapping the values in the Scale Map to the splines:

  • Texture space: The values are applied to the splines where they would be if placed in a texture using the texture’s UV coordinates. This effectively applies the value to the splines ‘in place’;
  • Horizontal along spline: The values are applied to the encoded splines’ coordinates directly (see Spline Coords input), where each row is applied to a different spline from top to bottom;
  • Hor. along spline (rand. offset X): The values are applied to the encoded splines’ coordinates directly (see Spline Coords input), with a random horizontal offset in the Scale Map for each spline (I.e., each row in Spline Coords);
  • Hor. along spline (rand. offset Y): The values are applied to the encoded splines’ coordinates directly (see Spline Coords input), with a random vertical offset in the Scale Map for each spline (I.e., each row in Spline Coords).

scale_random.description: Applies a random multiplier up to the specified value for decreasing the size of the splines.

scale_start_end_attenuation.description:
Factors in the distance from the midpoint of the spline to its Start and End when scaling the splines.

This means size is decreased for splines closer to a spline’s extremities.

show_thickness_envelope.description: Displays additional lines at the edges of the spline’s thickness.

side.description:
Controls on which side(s) of the parent splines the splines should be scattered, considering that 'forward' is the direction of the parent splines.

  • Left: Place the splines on the left side
  • Right: Place the splines on the right side
  • Left + Right: Place the splines on both sides
  • Left / Right - Alternate: Place splines left then right alternatively (E.g., every other side)
  • Left / Right - Random: Pick the side randomly for each spline

spline_amount_per_spline.description: The amount of evenly spaced splines scattered along each parent spline.

spline_pivot_mode.description:
Sets the method of placing the pivot of scattered splines, which impacts rotation and scaling.

Note that the pivot is always placed on the parent spline and its controls impact the scattered spline.
In other words: the pivot does not move, it is the scattered spline that moves and scales relatively to it.

  • Position along spline: Move the pivot along the scattered spline
  • Absolute position: Set an arbitrary position for the pivot

spline_spacing.description: The minimum distance along parent splines by which splines should be spaced, while still landing the first and last spline on the start and end of each parent spline respectively.

spline_type.description:
Selects which type of spline should be scattered on the parent splines.

  • Straight: A simple, straight spline
  • Custom spline: The spline(s) provided to the Custom Spline inputs. Mutliple splines are supported when appended together into a list

start.description:
Offsets the point from the start of the parent splines where the scattering starts.

The value is the normalized length of each parent spline.

start_height.description: Sets an absolute value for the scattered spline's starting height.

start_height_mode.description:
The method of computing the starting height of the scattered splines.

  • Manual: Set the same absolute value for all scattered splines
  • From parent spline (+ custom spline): Use the height of the parent spline, then add the custom spline's height using the Custom spline start height mult. parameter
  • From custom spline: Use the height of the custom spline as is

Note: Set Spline Type to Custom Spline and connect the Custom Spline inputs to use the height of custom splines.

start_height_multiplier.description:
Controls the contribution of the custom spline's own starting height to the starting height of the scattered splines, where 1 means the full height of the custom spline is used.

The custom spline's height is used differently according to the selected Start Height Mode:

  • From parent spline (+ custom spline): The height is added to the parent spline's
  • From custom spline: The height is used directly

start_height_offset.description: Applies an absolute offset to the scattered spline's starting height.

start_thickness.description: Sets an absolute value for the scattered spline's starting thickness.

start_thickness_mode.description:
The method of computing the starting thickness of the scattered splines.

  • Manual: Set the same absolute value for all scattered splines
  • From parent spline: Use the thickness of the parent spline
  • From custom spline: Use the thickness of the custom spline

Note: Set Spline Type to Custom Spline and connect the Custom Spline inputs to use the thickness of custom splines.

start_thickness_multiplier.description: Scales the starting thickness of the scattered splines, where 1 is the full thickness.

start_thickness_offset.description: Applies an absolute offset to the scattered spline's starting thickness.

thickness_px.description: Adjusts the thickness of the spline visualization in the Preview output, in number of pixels.

select_path.description:
Isolate one path among multiples contained in Paths.

Note: See the Paths Format Specifications page in Designer's documentation to learn about the method used to encode Paths.

paths.description:
Contains a list of Paths which describe lists of segments, encoded in a color image.

Connect this input to the result of a Mask to Paths or to another Path-processing node.

The selected Paths. You can use the Preview Paths node to visualize the result of the transformation.

The result can be processed using another Paths-processing node, or sent to a Paths to Spline node to further process it as Splines.

greater.description: Controls whether the selection should include or greater or lower length than the Target Length.

length.description: The length threshold used to select splines.

mode.description:
The method used to select the Paths:

  • By ID: Selects the path from the list whose index matches the one specified in Path ID;
  • By Length: Selects the paths whose length is above or below the threshold specified in Target Length.

path_id.description:
The index of the selected path.

A value greater than the number of paths in Paths results in a blank output.

shape_drop_shadow_color.description: Draw a shadow behind a shape.

shape_drop_shadow_grayscale.description: Draw a shadow behind a shape.

shape_extrude_2.description:
Extrude a 2D mask into a 2.5D shape.

v1.1

downscale.description: Downscale the final output to reduce aliasing.

shape_glow_color.description: Draw a color light behind or inside shape.

shape_glow_grayscale.description: Draw a color light behind or inside shape.

shape_mapper.description:
Projects an input image along a circle or polygon.

The projection deforms the image to follow the shape's outline, and make it fit exactly a specified amount of times without gaps.

input.description: The pattern which should be placed along the shape.

output.description: The result of the projection of the pattern along the shape, as a grayscale bitmap.

filtering_mode.description:
The method of filtering applied to the patterns placed along the shape:
  • Nearest: Applies the value from the closest projected pixel as-is, resulting in a crisper yet aliased look.
  • Bilinear: Applies a bilinear filter to interpolate the projected pixel with its neighbors, for a smoother yet blurrier look.

flip_1on_2.description: Flip one every other shape vertically.

link_segments_pattern_amount.description:
Use the Pattern amount as the number of Segments.

This prevents patterns from wrapping around corners, ensuring a straight and consistent aspect.

non_square_expansion.description: In non-square images, keeps the generated shape square and expands the image generation to the image's bounds.

pattern_amount.description: The amount of patterns placed along the selected shape.

radius.description: A multiplier for the radius of the shape, where 1.0 is half the length of the image's shortest side.

rotation.description: The amount of rotation applied to the shape, in number of turns clockwise from the horizontal right.

segments.description:
The amount of segments for the polygon along which patterns are placed.

Segments are evenly sized, and all vertices are equidistant from the center, such that increasing the amount of segments makes the polygon converge towards a circle.

shape.description:
Sets the type of shape along which patterns should be placed:
  • Circle
  • Polygon

width.description: A multiplier for the width of the patterns along the shape, where 1.0 is half the length of the image's shortest side.

splatter_data_1.description:
R: Pattern ID, UV.x
G: Pattern Index.x, UV.y
B: Pattern Rotation, Shape Rotation
A: Height

splatter_data_2.description:
Data are in texture space. They can be used by Flood Fill companion filters as well.
R: Pattern UV.x
G: Pattern UV.y
B: Pattern bBox Width
A: Pattern bBox Height

height_conform_to_background.description: At 0, the shape height remains intact, at 1 the shape height will be deformed by the underlying height background.

height_cutout_black_pixels.description: Ignores the black value from the pattern inputs.

height_offset_map_multiplier.description: Multiplies the shape height offset in function of the input image grayscale value.

height_scale_auto_adjust.description: Automatically adjust the height in function of the pattern scale to keep the shape height proportionnal to the background height.

height_scale_map_multiplier.description: Multiplies the shape height scale in function of the input image grayscale value.

height_skew.description: Deforms the shape height in function of the local background height slope: a linear gradient corresponding to the background slope is added to the shape height.

out_of_bounds_option.description:
Due to the technical nature of the splatter, shapes can't be drawn more than 2 cells size away from their original position.
If a shape becomes too large or is moved too far you have two options:
- Scale Shape will reduce the shape size when it hit a bound
- Constrain Position will move the shape back to its original position

pattern_distribution_map_multiplier.description: Select shape pattern in function of the input image grayscale value.

position_random_distribution.description:
Switches from a Guaussian distribution to a Uniform Distribution for the random position.

The Gaussian distribution will produce a more organic result compared to the Uniform distribution.

shape_rotation_from_background_slope.description:
Modifies the shape rotation in function of the background height slope.
Usually used in combination with the "Size Ratio from Bg Slope" parameter

shape_rotation_map_multiplier.description: Multiplies the shaperotation in function of the input image grayscale value.

shape_rotation_vector_map_multiplier.description: Set the shape rotation in function of the vector image input.

shape_scale_map_multiplier.description: Multiplies the shape scale in function of the input image grayscale value.

shape_scale_no_overlap.description: Reduces the shape scale so that they don't overlap.

shape_size_ratio_from_background_slope.description: Modifies the shape size ratio in function on the background height slope.

shape_splatter_blend.description: Takes the Splatter Data as input to assign specific maps to each pattern.

color_input.description: Use this image input along with the "Color Input Opacity" to colorize patterns.

shape_splatter_blend_color.description:
Takes the Splatter Data as input to assign specific maps to each pattern.
This Filter can also handle normal maps.

shape_splatter_data_extract.description:
Extract useful information out of the Splatter Data (1 and 2) maps.
Data you can extract:
- Position
- UVs (in texture space)
- UVs (in shape space)
- Bounding Box Size (X, Y)
- Pattern Index (integer)
- Pattern ID (integer)
- Shape Angle

bounding_box_size.description: Returns the Shape bounding box size.

pattern_id.description: Returns the pattern ID as integer.

position.description: Returns the shape final position (center of the shape).

shape_angle.description: Angle of the shape in turns.

shape_index.description: Outputs the shape original index, represents the initial integer coordinates in the grid.

uv_shape_space.description: Returns the shape UVs aligned to the image coordinate system.

uv_texture_space.description: Returns the shape UVs aligned to the shape coordinate system.

shape_splatter_mapper_color.description:
Maps color images on shapes generated and scattered using the Shape splatter v2 node, using the additional data provided by the node.

Images are provided as separate pattern inputs or packed into a grid atlas, and can be applied to the shapes using UV mapping, triplanar projection or custom mapping.

Shapes can be tinted and their colors adjusted uniformly or randomly per shape.

background_input.description: The color image used as background for the mapped shapes.

color_input.description:
The color image used to tint the mapped shapes according to their pivot position.

Use the Color input opacity parameter to adjust the intensity of these colors' contribution to the shapes color.

grid_atlas_input.description:
A color image of patterns packed into a grid layout.

The grid size should match that used by the Shape splatter v2 node.

Use the Grid atlas color node to pack separate patterns into a grid atlas.

normal.description:
The normals computed for the scattered shapes, masked according to the blending with the background height.

If the Shape type is 'Grid atlas', the normals provided to the Grid atlas normal input are used directly.

pattern_input_1.description:
The color image for the #1 pattern that is mapped to the shapes.

Tip: Use a resolution that is close to the maximum size the pattern may have when scattered.

pattern_input_2.description:
The color image for the #2 pattern that is mapped to the shapes.

Tip: Use a resolution that is close to the maximum size the pattern may have when scattered.

pattern_input_3.description:
The color image for the #3 pattern that is mapped to the shapes.

Tip: Use a resolution that is close to the maximum size the pattern may have when scattered.

pattern_input_4.description:
The color image for the #4 pattern that is mapped to the shapes.

Tip: Use a resolution that is close to the maximum size the pattern may have when scattered.

pattern_input_5.description:
The color image for the #5 pattern that is mapped to the shapes.

Tip: Use a resolution that is close to the maximum size the pattern may have when scattered.

pattern_input_6.description:
The color image for the #6 pattern that is mapped to the shapes.

Tip: Use a resolution that is close to the maximum size the pattern may have when scattered.

pattern_input_7.description:
The color image for the #7 pattern that is mapped to the shapes.

Tip: Use a resolution that is close to the maximum size the pattern may have when scattered.

pattern_input_8.description:
The color image for the #8 pattern that is mapped to the shapes.

Tip: Use a resolution that is close to the maximum size the pattern may have when scattered.

shape_splatter_data_1.description:
R - X component of the position on the shape surface, in object space.
G - Y component of the position on the shape surface, in object space.
B - Z component of the position on the shape surface, in object space.
A - Packed data:
      - Integer part: U component of the UV coordinates for the shapes' data in the Data 2/3 outputs.
      - Fractional part: V component of the UV coordinates for the shapes' data in the Data 2/3 outputs.
      - Sign: Binary mask for the blending of the shapes with the background height.

shape_splatter_data_2.description:
R - X component of the shapes' 3D rotation.
G - Y component of the shapes' 3D rotation.
B - Z component of the shapes' 3D rotation.
A - The shapes' rotation around their normal.

All rotations are defined in number of turns.

shape_splatter_data_3.description:
R - X component of the shapes' position.
G - Y component of the shapes' position.
B - The shapes' offset along their normal.
A - Packed data:
      - Integer part: The shape's ID.
      - Fractional part:The index of the shapes' pattern in its source atlas. (If using a grid atlas pattern type)

shape_splatter_data_4.description:
Pixel 1
R - X size of the Data 2/3 output images.
G - Y size of the Data 2/3 output images.
B - X size of the Data 4 output image.
A - Y size of the Data 4 output image.

Pixel 2
R - The shape type. (E.g. Cube, cylinder, ...)
G - Packed data:
      - Absolute value: The pattern input number.
      - Sign: Normal format of the output normal map. (Positive: DirectX / Negative: OpenGL)
B - X size of the grid atlas. (I.e. the amount of columns)
A - Y size of the grid atlas. (I.e. the amount of rows)

shape_splatter_uvw.description:
R - U component of the shapes' UVs.
G - V component of the shapes' UVs.
B - The shapes' height. (W)
A - Packed data:
      - Integer part: The shapes' unique identifier. (ID)
      - Fractional part: Depends on the Shape type: Material ID if SDF/primitive, pattern ID* if pattern input/grid atlas.

*: The pattern ID is the index of the shape in the list/atlas.

output.description: The colored shapes.

color_input_blending_mode.description:
The color blending operation used to combine the foreground and background images.

These operations are identical to their counterparts in the Blend node.

Available modes:
- Copy
- Add (linear dodge)
- Subtract
- Multiply
- Overlay

color_input_opacity.description: The intensity of the Color input's contribution to the shapes' colors, according to the selected Color input blending mode.

custom_function.description:
Specifies the RGBA per-pixel color of the shapes as a Float4.

The following variables are available:
- shape.position.os (Float3) The position of the shape surface in object space.
- shape.position.ws (Float3) The position of the shape surface in world space*.
- shape.normal.os (Float3) The normals of the shape surface in object space.
- shape.normal.ws (Float3) The normals of the shape surface in world space*.
- shape.id (Float) The shape's unique identifier.
- material.id (Float) The material ID of the shape surface, defined by the 'Shape splatter v2' node.

*: The shape's world space is centered on its pivot and does not account for the shape's height. This means the only difference with object space is orientation.

If sampling the 'Shape splatter v2 mapper color' node's inputs is needed, these Sample color node input slots may be used:
- 0: Grid atlas
- 1-8: Pattern input 1-8

hsl_adjustement.description: An offset uniformly applied to the hue, saturation and luminance (HSL) of all shapes.

hsl_random.description: A random positive or negative offset applied to the hue, saturation and luminance (HSL) of the shapes, up to the specified values.

image_index.description: The index of the input pattern from the Pattern input # or Grid atlas input that should be mapped onto the shapes.

input_normal_format.description:
The format of the normal maps provided to the Grid atlas input or the Pattern input #.

Effectively inverts the green channel.

- DirectX: The Y axis points up.
- OpenGL: The Y axis points down.

input_selection_mode.description:
The method of selecting which of the input images should be mapped to the shapes.

The Shape type selected in the source 'Shape splatter v2' node changes the way of assigning images to shapes:

- Grid atlas means the images are fetched in the 'Grid atlas input' by matching grid indexes (Both atlases should use the same grid size)
- Pattern input means the images are fetched in the 'Pattern input #' inputs by matching indexes.
- Other shape types: images are assigned by matching the indexes to the shape's material IDs.

The available methods of selecting the indexes are:
- From splatter data: Match the indexes of the 'Pattern input #' or 'Grid atlas input' images to the indexes of the shapes assigned by the 'Shape splatter v2' node.
- Manual: Use the index specified by the 'Image index' parameter.
- Random: Use a random index in the range specified by the 'Random range' parameter.

is_normal.description:
Specifies whether the images provided to the Grid atlas input or the Pattern input # are normal maps.

This is necessary to enable the processing required for correctly handling the normal vectors and applying them onto the shapes.

normal_angle_random.description:
A direction vector is generated from the normal vector's origin to a random point on the base of a cone around the normal vector, then the normal vector is blended with that random direction vector.

This parameter adjusts the angle of the cone, where 1 is a hemisphere and 0 means the direction vector is equal to the normal vector.

pattern_input_number.description: The amount of Pattern input # input images that should be mapped onto the shapes.

projection_mode.description:
The method of projecting the input images onto the shapes:

- From splatter UVs: Use the UVs provided by the 'Shape splatter v2' node.
- Triplanar: Use triplanar projection to map the images on the shapes' local XYZ axes.
- Custom function: Author a function graph to define the mapping of the images onto the shapes.

random_offset.description:
Applies a random amount of positive or negative offset per shape to to the mapping of the images across the shapes, up to the specified value.

This offset is added to the UV offset, if any.

random_range.description: The range of indexes from the Pattern input # or Grid atlas input that pattern should be randomly selected in to be mapped onto the shapes.

tiling_mode.description:
The axes along which the texture should be repeated:
- No tiling
- Horizontal tiling
- Vertical tiling
- H and V tiling: Combined horizontal and vertical tiling.

triplanar_blending_contrast.description: The sharpness of the transitions between planar projections, where 1 means no fade gradient.

triplanar_image_projection.description:
The amount of Pattern input # images distributed across the planar projections contributing to the triplanar mapping.

In order to cover all sides of a shape, a front (+) and back (-) planar projection is performed on each axis, totalling 6 projections.

- 1 image: The Pattern input 1 is used for all planar projections.
- 3 images: A separate Pattern input is used for the +/- projection of each axis.
- 6 images: Each projection uses a separate Pattern input.
- 1 image per material ID: Use a separate Pattern input per material ID, where each image is used for all planar projections.

triplanar_projection_center.description:
Offsets the triplanar projection per axis, in object space.

The offset is applied to the entire projection space, so an offset on one axis will affect the positioning of the textures projected on the other two axes.

triplanar_projection_scale.description: Adjusts the scale of the projected textures on all axes, by the specified factor.

uv_offset.description:
Applies an offset to the mapping of the images across the shapes, which enables fine adjustment of the positioning of the images on the shapes.

This offset is added to the Random offset, if any.

uv_scale.description: Adjusts the tiling of the images mapped onto the shapes by the specified factor, with separate controls for U and V scaling. Higher values result in more repetitions.

uv_tiling.description:
Adjusts the global tiling of the images mapped onto the shapes

Higher values result in more repetitions.

shape_splatter_mapper_grayscale.description:
Maps grayscale images on shapes generated and scattered using the Shape splatter v2 node, using the additional data provided by the node.

Images are provided as separate pattern inputs or packed into a grid atlas, and can be applied to the shapes using UV mapping, triplanar projection or custom mapping.

Shapes can be tinted and their luminance adjusted uniformly or randomly per shape.

background_input.description: The grayscale image used as background for the mapped shapes.

color_input.description:
The grayscale image used to tint the mapped shapes according to their pivot position.

Use the Color input opacity parameter to adjust the intensity of these colors' contribution to the shapes color.

grid_atlas_input.description:
A grayscale image of patterns packed into a grid layout.

The grid size should match that used by the Shape splatter v2 node.

Use the Grid atlas grayscale node to pack separate patterns into a grid atlas.

normal.description:
The normals computed for the scattered shapes, masked according to the blending with the background height.

If the Shape type is 'Grid atlas', the normals provided to the Grid atlas normal input are used directly.

pattern_input_1.description:
The grayscale image for the #1 pattern that is mapped to the shapes.

Tip: Use a resolution that is close to the maximum size the pattern may have when scattered.

pattern_input_2.description:
The grayscale image for the #2 pattern that is mapped to the shapes.

Tip: Use a resolution that is close to the maximum size the pattern may have when scattered.

pattern_input_3.description:
The grayscale image for the #3 pattern that is mapped to the shapes.

Tip: Use a resolution that is close to the maximum size the pattern may have when scattered.

pattern_input_4.description:
The grayscale image for the #4 pattern that is mapped to the shapes.

Tip: Use a resolution that is close to the maximum size the pattern may have when scattered.

pattern_input_5.description:
The grayscale image for the #5 pattern that is mapped to the shapes.

Tip: Use a resolution that is close to the maximum size the pattern may have when scattered.

pattern_input_6.description:
The grayscale image for the #6 pattern that is mapped to the shapes.

Tip: Use a resolution that is close to the maximum size the pattern may have when scattered.

pattern_input_7.description:
The grayscale image for the #7 pattern that is mapped to the shapes.

Tip: Use a resolution that is close to the maximum size the pattern may have when scattered.

pattern_input_8.description:
The grayscale image for the #8 pattern that is mapped to the shapes.

Tip: Use a resolution that is close to the maximum size the pattern may have when scattered.

shape_splatter_data_1.description:
R - X component of the position on the shape surface, in object space.
G - Y component of the position on the shape surface, in object space.
B - Z component of the position on the shape surface, in object space.
A - Packed data:
      - Integer part: U component of the UV coordinates for the shapes' data in the Data 2/3 outputs.
      - Fractional part: V component of the UV coordinates for the shapes' data in the Data 2/3 outputs.
      - Sign: Binary mask for the blending of the shapes with the background height.

shape_splatter_data_2.description:
R - X component of the shapes' 3D rotation.
G - Y component of the shapes' 3D rotation.
B - Z component of the shapes' 3D rotation.
A - The shapes' rotation around their normal.

All rotations are defined in number of turns.

shape_splatter_data_3.description:
R - X component of the shapes' position.
G - Y component of the shapes' position.
B - The shapes' offset along their normal.
A - Packed data:
      - Integer part: The shape's ID.
      - Fractional part:The index of the shapes' pattern in its source atlas. (If using a grid atlas pattern type)

shape_splatter_data_4.description:
Pixel 1
R - X size of the Data 2/3 output images.
G - Y size of the Data 2/3 output images.
B - X size of the Data 4 output image.
A - Y size of the Data 4 output image.

Pixel 2
R - The shape type. (E.g. Cube, cylinder, ...)
G - Packed data:
      - Absolute value: The pattern input number.
      - Sign: Normal format of the output normal map. (Positive: DirectX / Negative: OpenGL)
B - X size of the grid atlas. (I.e. the amount of columns)
A - Y size of the grid atlas. (I.e. the amount of rows)

shape_splatter_uvw.description:
R - U component of the shapes' UVs.
G - V component of the shapes' UVs.
B - The shapes' height. (W)
A - Packed data:
      - Integer part: The shapes' unique identifier. (ID)
      - Fractional part: Depends on the Shape type: Material ID if SDF/primitive, pattern ID* if pattern input/grid atlas.

*: The pattern ID is the index of the shape in the list/atlas.

output.description: The colored shapes.

color_input_blending_mode.description:
The color blending operation used to combine the foreground and background images.

These operations are identical to their counterparts in the Blend node.

Available modes:
- Copy
- Add (linear dodge)
- Subtract
- Multiply
- Overlay

color_input_opacity.description: The intensity of the Color input's contribution to the shapes' colors, according to the selected Color input blending mode.

custom_function.description:
Specifies the per-pixel luminance of the shapes as a Float.

The following variables are available:
- shape.position.os (Float3) The position of the shape surface in object space.
- shape.position.ws (Float3) The position of the shape surface in world space*.
- shape.normal.os (Float3) The normals of the shape surface in object space.
- shape.normal.ws (Float3) The normals of the shape surface in world space*.
- shape.id (Float) The shape's unique identifier.
- material.id (Float) The material ID of the shape surface, defined by the 'Shape splatter v2' node.

*: The shape's world space is centered on its pivot and does not account for the shape's height. This means the only difference with object space is orientation.

If sampling the 'Shape splatter v2 mapper grayscale' node's inputs is needed, these Sample grayscale node input slots may be used:
- 0: Grid atlas
- 1-8: Pattern input 1-8

image_index.description: The index of the input pattern from the Pattern input # or Grid atlas input that should be mapped onto the shapes.

input_selection_mode.description:
The method of selecting which of the input images should be mapped to the shapes.

The Shape type selected in the source 'Shape splatter v2' node changes the way of assigning images to shapes:

- Grid atlas means the images are fetched in the 'Grid atlas input' by matching grid indexes (Both atlases should use the same grid size)
- Pattern input means the images are fetched in the 'Pattern input #' inputs by matching indexes.
- Other shape types: images are assigned by matching the indexes to the shape's material IDs.

The available methods of selecting the indexes are:
- From splatter data: Match the indexes of the 'Pattern input #' or 'Grid atlas input' images to the indexes of the shapes assigned by the 'Shape splatter v2' node.
- Manual: Use the index specified by the 'Image index' parameter.
- Random: Use a random index in the range specified by the 'Random range' parameter.

luminance_adjustement.description: An offset uniformly applied to the luminance of all shapes.

luminance_random.description: A random positive or negative offset applied to the luminance of the shapes, up to the specified values.

pattern_input_number.description: The amount of Pattern input # input images that should be mapped onto the shapes.

projection_mode.description:
The method of projecting the input images onto the shapes:

- From splatter UVs: Use the UVs provided by the 'Shape splatter v2' node.
- Triplanar: Use triplanar projection to map the images on the shapes' local XYZ axes.
- Custom function: Author a function graph to define the mapping of the images onto the shapes.

random_offset.description:
Applies a random amount of positive or negative offset per shape to to the mapping of the images across the shapes, up to the specified value.

This offset is added to the UV offset, if any.

random_range.description: The range of indexes from the Pattern input # or Grid atlas input that pattern should be randomly selected in to be mapped onto the shapes.

tiling_mode.description:
The axes along which the texture should be repeated:
- No tiling
- Horizontal tiling
- Vertical tiling
- H and V tiling: Combined horizontal and vertical tiling.

triplanar_blending_contrast.description: The sharpness of the transitions between planar projections, where 1 means no fade gradient.

triplanar_image_projection.description:
The amount of Pattern input # images distributed across the planar projections contributing to the triplanar mapping.

In order to cover all sides of a shape, a front (+) and back (-) planar projection is performed on each axis, totalling 6 projections.

- 1 image: The Pattern input 1 is used for all planar projections.
- 3 images: A separate Pattern input is used for the +/- projection of each axis.
- 6 images: Each projection uses a separate Pattern input.
- 1 image per material ID: Use a separate Pattern input per material ID, where each image is used for all planar projections.

triplanar_projection_center.description:
Offsets the triplanar projection per axis, in object space.

The offset is applied to the entire projection space, so an offset on one axis will affect the positioning of the textures projected on the other two axes.

triplanar_projection_scale.description: Adjusts the scale of the projected textures on all axes, by the specified factor.

uv_offset.description:
Applies an offset to the mapping of the images across the shapes, which enables fine adjustment of the positioning of the images on the shapes.

This offset is added to the Random offset, if any.

uv_scale.description: Adjusts the tiling of the images mapped onto the shapes by the specified factor, with separate controls for U and V scaling. Higher values result in more repetitions.

uv_tiling.description:
Adjusts the global tiling of the images mapped onto the shapes

Higher values result in more repetitions.

position.description: Returns the shape final position (center of the shape).

shape_splatter_to_mask_2.description:
Computes a mask out of a selection of shapes generated by the Shape splatter v2 node.

Available options include random selection as well as selecting ranges of shapes by unique identifier and/or material ID/pattern ID*.

Shapes are pre-masked by the Shape splatter v2 node from their height blend with the background height.
Both the background and the unselected shapes are pure black. (I.e a value of 0)

*: One of the values retrieved from the Shape splatter UVW input is either the material ID or pattern ID, depending on the Shape type used in the Shape splatter v2 node:
- SDF/primitive: Material ID
- Pattern input/Grid atlas: Pattern ID, I.e. the index of the pattern in the list/atlas.

shape_splatter_uvw.description:
R - U component of the shapes' UVs.
G - V component of the shapes' UVs.
B - The shapes' height. (W)
A - Packed data:
      - Integer part: The shapes' unique identifier. (ID)
      - Fractional part: Depends on the Shape type: Material ID if SDF/primitive, pattern ID* if pattern input/grid atlas.

*: The pattern ID is the index of the shape in the list/atlas.

position.description: The computed mask of the selected shapes.

material_id_mask_combination.description:
Specified the logical operator used to combine the selection by unique identifier (ID) with the selection by material ID/pattern ID.

- None: Ignore the material ID/pattern ID entirely for the selection.
- AND: Selected shapes have to be included in both the ID and material ID/pattern ID ranges. (Includes less shapes)
- OR: Selected shapes have to be included in either the ID or material ID/pattern ID ranges. (Includes more shapes)

material_id_range_end.description:
The material ID or pattern ID* used as the end of the selection range. (Included)

*: See node description for details.

material_id_range_start.description:
The material ID or pattern ID* used as the start of the selection range. (Included)

*: See node description for details.

output.description:
The values used for the selected shapes in the output mask.

- Binary mask: Selected shapes all use a value of 1.
- Shape ID (integer): Selected shapes use their unique identifier. (ID)
- Material ID (integer): Selected shapes use their material ID.
- Shape ID (normalized): Selected shapes use their unique identifier (ID) mapped to the [0, 1] range from the lowest selected ID to the highest.
- Material ID (normalized): Selected shapes use their material ID mapped to the [0, 1] range from the lowest selected material ID to the highest.

shape_id_offset.description:
Offsets the shapes' unique identifiers by the specified value, in the context of the selection range.

This makes it easy to offset the current selection by the specified value without having to adjust the start and end bounds manually.

shape_id_range_end.description: The shape unique identifier (ID) used as the end of the selection range. (Included)

shape_id_range_start.description: The shape unique identifier (ID) used as the start of the selection range. (Included)

shape_random_mask.description: A factor for the random masking of shapes, where 1 means all shapes are masked.

shape_splatter_v2.description:
Scatters shapes on a background height surface with advanced capabilities for scattering in a virtual 3D space, with controls for position, rotation, scaling and randomness.

The node offers basic primitive 3D shapes and supports custom ones provided either as a pattern image, an atlas, or as a signed distance field (SDF) function for complex custom 3D shapes.

Several methods of distributing shapes are available, including authoring a custom function for complete control.

Shapes can be pulled towards specific areas using a custom density map.

Note: This node is not meant to be used with the CPU versions of the Substance engine, I.e. SSE2 (Windows, Linux) and NEON (macOS).

background_height.description:
The base height map in which shapes are scattered. The heights of each are combined using a 'Max blend', where the higher of the two is used.

The contribution of the background height to the output height is controlled by the Background input opacity parameter.

density_map.description:
A grayscale map driving the displacement of shapes according to its luminance, in that shapes gather in its brighter areas.

The intensity of the displacement of shapes is controlled by the Density map multiplier parameter.

grid_atlas_height.description:
The image describing the height of patterns packed into an atlas.

Use the Grid atlas size parameter to specify the grid size of the atlas.

grid_atlas_normal.description:
The image describing the normals of patterns packed into an atlas.

Use the Grid atlas size parameter to specify the grid size of the atlas.

height_offset_map.description:
A grayscale map which values are added to the shapes uniformly according to the shapes' pivot location.

The contribution of the map is controlled by the Height offset map multiplier parameter.

height_scale_map.description:
A grayscale map which values are used as a factor for the height of the shapes.

The contribution of the map is controlled by the Height scale map multiplier parameter.

mask_map.description:
The image used to mask shapes according to the Mask map threshold.

I.e. shapes located in areas of the map where the luminance is below that threshold will be masked.

pattern_input_1.description:
The height map for the #1 pattern that is scattered when Pattern type is set to 'Pattern input'.

Tip: Use a resolution that is close to the maximum size the pattern may have when scattered.

pattern_input_2.description:
The height map for the #2 pattern that is scattered when Pattern type is set to 'Pattern input'.

Tip: Use a resolution that is close to the maximum size the pattern may have when scattered.

pattern_input_3.description:
The height map for the #3 pattern that is scattered when Pattern type is set to 'Pattern input'.

Tip: Use a resolution that is close to the maximum size the pattern may have when scattered.

pattern_input_4.description:
The height map for the #4 pattern that is scattered when Pattern type is set to 'Pattern input'.

Tip: Use a resolution that is close to the maximum size the pattern may have when scattered.

pattern_input_5.description:
The height map for the #5 pattern that is scattered when Pattern type is set to 'Pattern input'.

Tip: Use a resolution that is close to the maximum size the pattern may have when scattered.

pattern_input_6.description:
The height map for the #6 pattern that is scattered when Pattern type is set to 'Pattern input'.

Tip: Use a resolution that is close to the maximum size the pattern may have when scattered.

pattern_input_7.description:
The height map for the #7 pattern that is scattered when Pattern type is set to 'Pattern input'.

Tip: Use a resolution that is close to the maximum size the pattern may have when scattered.

pattern_input_8.description:
The height map for the #8 pattern that is scattered when Pattern type is set to 'Pattern input'.

Tip: Use a resolution that is close to the maximum size the pattern may have when scattered.

shape_rotation_map.description: A grayscale map which values are added to the 3D rotation of shapes, adjusted by the per-axis factors provided by the 3D rotation map multiplier parameter.

shape_scale_map.description:
A grayscale map which values are used as a factor for the scale of the shapes.

The contribution of the map is controlled by the Scale map multiplier parameter.

vector_map.description:
A map describing direction vectors that can be used to drive the rotation and/or position of shapes, using the following parameters:

- Vector map displacement adjusts the effect of the map for moving the shapes.
- Slope rotation input can be set to 'Vector map' to use this map to rotate the shapes using the related parameters.

height.description: The computed height map for the scattered shapes, including the background height if used and visible.

normal.description:
The normals computed for the scattered shapes, masked according to the blending with the background height.

If the Shape type is 'Grid atlas', the normals provided to the Grid atlas normal input are used directly.

sdf_color.description:
The colors of the shape produced by the SDF function.

Use the Set color node in the SDF function graph to define a color per component of the shape.

sdf_metalness.description:
The colors of the shape produced by the SDF function.

Use the Set metalness node in the SDF function graph to define a metalness value per component of the shape.

sdf_roughness.description:
The colors of the shape produced by the SDF function.

Use the Set roughness node in the SDF function graph to define a roughness value per component of the shape.

shape_splatter_data_1.description:
R - X component of the position on the shape surface, in object space.
G - Y component of the position on the shape surface, in object space.
B - Z component of the position on the shape surface, in object space.
A - Packed data:
      - Integer part: U component of the UV coordinates for the shapes' data in the Data 2/3 outputs.
      - Fractional part: V component of the UV coordinates for the shapes' data in the Data 2/3 outputs.
      - Sign: Binary mask for the blending of the shapes with the background height.

shape_splatter_data_2.description:
R - X component of the shapes' 3D rotation.
G - Y component of the shapes' 3D rotation.
B - Z component of the shapes' 3D rotation.
A - The shapes' rotation around their normal.

All rotations are defined in number of turns.

shape_splatter_data_3.description:
R - X component of the shapes' position.
G - Y component of the shapes' position.
B - The shapes' offset along their normal.
A - Packed data:
      - Integer part: The shape's unique identifier.
      - Fractional part:The index of the shapes' pattern in its source atlas. (If using a grid atlas pattern type)

shape_splatter_data_4.description:
Pixel 1
R - X size of the Data 2/3 output images.
G - Y size of the Data 2/3 output images.
B - X size of the Data 4 output image.
A - Y size of the Data 4 output image.

Pixel 2
R - The shape type. (E.g. Cube, cylinder, ...)
G - Packed data:
      - Absolute value: The pattern input number.
      - Sign: Normal format of the output normal map. (Positive: DirectX / Negative: OpenGL)
B - X size of the grid atlas. (I.e. the amount of columns)
A - Y size of the grid atlas. (I.e. the amount of rows)

shape_splatter_uvw.description:
R - U component of the shapes' UVs.
G - V component of the shapes' UVs.
B - The shapes' height. (W)
A - Packed data:
      - Integer part: The shapes' unique identifier. (ID)
      - Fractional part: Depends on the Shape type: Material ID if SDF/primitive, pattern ID* if pattern input/grid atlas.

*: The pattern ID is the index of the shape in the list/atlas.

2d_shape_size.description: Separate scale factors per axis for adjusting the size of the shapes.

2d_shape_size_random.description: Scales down the shapes by a random factor per axis up to the specified value, where 1 may result in some shapes being scaled all the way down to zero size.

3d_rotation.description: Applies a per-axis rotation uniformly to all shapes, in number of turns.

3d_rotation_map_multiplier.description: The intensity by which the values in the Shape rotation map are added to the per-axis rotation of each shape, where 1 means the full amount of rotation is added.

3d_rotation_random.description: A factor for the random amount of rotation applied to the shapes up to the specified value, clockwise or counterclockwise, in number of turns.

3d_rotation_random_multiplier.description: Adjusts the amount of random rotation applied by 3D rotation random by a separate factor per axis.

3d_shape_size.description: Separate scale factors per axis for adjusting the size of the shapes.

3d_shape_size_random.description: Scales down the shapes by a random factor per axis up to the specified value, where 1 may result in some shapes being scaled all the way down to zero size.

background_input_opacity.description:
The intensity of the provided Background height input in the final height map.

The heights of the shapes and the background are combined using a 'Max blend', where the higher of the two is used.

conform_to_background.description:
The intensity of the deformation applied to the shapes' height to match the background height per pixel, where 1 means an exact match.

Note: This parameter has no effect when Height offset from background = 0.

cylinder_cap_uv_mode.description:
The method of computing the UVs for the cylinder's caps.

- Polar: Use polar coordinates where U increases around the cylinder's Z-axis and V increases as its gets farther away from it.
- Planar: Use a planar projection where the UVs are mapped using the caps' bounding box (I.e. a rectangle adjusted to the caps' size)

cylinder_cap_uv_scale.description: A per-axis multiplier for the UVs of the cylinder's caps, where tiling increases with the values.

cylinder_radius.description: The radius of the scattered cylinder SDFs. The radius is expressed as a factor defined by the Size mode.

density_map_multiplier.description: A factor for the offset applied to the shapes to have them gather up in the brightest areas of the Density map.

enable_shape_extrude.description: Toggles the extrusion of input patterns by interpreting them as height maps, which results in complex procedural 3D shapes.

grid_atlas_normal_format.description:
The format of the normal map provided to the Grid atlas normal image input.

Effectively inverts the green channel.

- DirectX: The Y axis points up.
- OpenGL: The Y axis points down.

grid_atlas_size.description:
The amount of rows and columns of the atlas provided to the Grid atlas image inputs.

Note: Empty cells in the atlas will result in gaps in the shape distribution.

height_offset.description:
A value added to the shapes' height, resulting in a straight offset.

The value is expressed in the image's full height range.

height_offset_by_id.description:
The offset applied to the shapes' height according to their index in the distribution, where the offset increases linearly from one shape to the next up to the specified value.

The value can be manually set beyond the [0, 1] range.

height_offset_from_background.description:
The ratio of the background height that should be added to the shapes' height, where 1 means the full background height is added.

This may be used to have the shapes 'rest' upon the background height.

height_offset_map_multiplier.description:
Adjusts the intensity of the offset applied by the Height offset map using the specified factor, where 1 means the intensity values from the map are applied as is.

The entire shape's height is offset by adding the value in the offset map at its XY pivot location.

The multiplier value can be manually set beyond the [0, 1] range.

height_offset_random.description:
Applies a random offset to the shapes' height, up to the specified value.

The value is expressed in the image's full height range.

height_scale.description: A multiplier for the shape's height, where 1 is the shape's full height expressed in the image's full height range of the shape's normalized height range. (See Normalized workflow)

height_scale_map_multiplier.description: The intensity of the provided Height scale map, where 1 means the full map value is multiplied against the shape's height.

height_scale_random.description: Randomly scales down each shape's height up to the specified ratio, where 1 means a shape's height may be scaled entirely down to 0.

keep_size_ratio.description: Adjusts the size of the shapes to preserve their original proportions in non-square grids and image sizes.

line_position_offset.description: An offset applied to lines of shapes on the grid according to the Line position offset mode.

line_position_offset_mode.description:
The method of applying the Line position offset to the shapes.

The All methods apply the offset as a factor of the image's longest side (I.e. in texture space).
- All - Horizontal: Gradually adds the offset value horizontally row by row, by a factor of the row index.
- All - Vertical: Gradually adds the offset value vertically column by column, by a factor of the column index.

The Quincunx methods apply the offset as a factor of the shapes' cell size.
- Quincunx - Horizontal: Adds the offset value uniformly every other row.
- Quincunx - Vertical: Adds the offset value uniformly every other column.

mask_map_threshold.description:
The grayscale value in the Mask map below which shapes are hidden.

The map is combined with the Mask random parameter.

mask_random.description:
Hides the specified ratio of the total amount of shapes in random sequence, where 1 means all shapes are hidden.

This parameter is combined with the Mask map. (If used)

non_square_expansion.description: In non-square images, preserves the shapes ratio and expands their generation to the image's bounds.

normalized_workflow.description:
When on, enables the automatic adjustment of the shapes' height so they preserve their original proportions as they are scaled up or down.

When off, the shapes' height is expressed in the image's full height range regardless of their original proportions.

The height of the shapes can still be manually adjusted by using the Height scale parameters.

offset_along_normal.description: Displaces the shapes along their normal – I.e. their local Z-axis.

offset_along_normal_random.description:
Adds a random amount of displacement to the shapes along their normal.

The random amount may be positive or negative up to the specified value, or down to its negative.

output_normal_format.description:
The format of the output normal map.

Effectively inverts the green channel.

- DirectX: The Y axis points up.
- OpenGL: The Y axis points down.

pattern_custom_function.description:
The authored Substance function graph used to compute the pattern mapped to a 3D plane SDF.

These patterns can also be extruded by using Enable shape extrude.

pattern_cutout_threshold.description: The grayscale value below which the values in the patterns are trimmed. I.e. the value used as the border of the silhouette for the patterns.

pattern_enable_cutout.description: Toggles the trimming of patterns, which ignores all values below the Cutout threshold. This ensures only the desired silhouette of the patterns is used.

pattern_input_number.description:
The amount of patterns provided as input images.

Adds as many Pattern input # input pins to the node.

pattern_sdf_function.description:
The Substance function graph authoring the signed distance field (SDF) which describes the surface of a 3D object in space.

Browse the built-in collection of SDF functions in the Library to author a complex object by combining multiple SDF primitives using the available operators and transformations.

An SDF shape is entirely procedural and can be adjusted dynamically, which can enable each scattered shape to be unique.

Use the 3D viewer node to visualize the result of an SDF function.

Note: To apply randomness in SDF functions, use the 'Hash' nodes rather than 'Random'.

Available variables:
  - shape.id (Float) The shape's unique identifier.
  - shape.amount (Float) The amount of shapes specified by the Amount parameter.

pivot_position.description:
Adjusts the position of the pivot in the shape's local space, which impacts the origin of transformations. (I.e. position offset, rotation and scaling)

For instance, adjust the Z pivot position to have shapes pivot around their base.

position_distribution_mode.description:
The method of distributing the shapes in space:

- 2D grid: A simple uniform grid.
- Poisson disc: A simulation aiming at randomly offsetting the cells of a grid to prevent overlaps while making use of the available space.
- Uniform: An even distribution of a specified number of shapes. Requires more intensive computations.
- Custom function: Author a function graph to define the distribution of shapes. Available variables are listed in the node description.

position_distribution_sequence.description:
The algorithm used to distribute the shapes uniformly in space.

- R2: Based on the golden ratio. It is fast and offers more even and seemingly random distributions regardless of the amount of shapes.
- Halton: Based on prime numbers. It provides great results for sparse distributions, but gets slower and may result in visible lines as the amount of shapes increases.

These algorithms are known as quasirandom and low-discrepancy, in that they follow a deterministic sequence (quasirandom) aimed at covering a space evenly (low-discrepancy).

position_function.description:
The function graph used to define the distribution of shapes.

The graph outputs a Float2 value for the XY normalized position of the shapes in the image.

Available variables:
  - shape.id (Float) The shape's unique identifier.
  - shape.amount (Float) The amount of shapes specified by the Amount parameter.

position_global_offset.description:
An offset applied to the position of each shape after any height offset, random offsets and other displacements are applied.

This means moving the shapes using this parameter will not modify their position, orientation and scale.

position_random.description: Applies a random offset on the XY axes up to the specified value, where 1 is the length of the image's longest side.

position_random_multiplier.description: Separate factors per axis for the random offset applied to the shapes on the XY axes.

position_vector_displacement_multiplier.description: Adjusts the Vector map displacement by a separate factor per axis, where 0.0 means no displacement is applied on that axis.

position_vector_map_displacement.description:
A factor for the displacement applied to the shapes by adding the RGB values in the Vector map to the shape's XYZ coordinates respectively.

The displacement is expressed as a factor of the image's longest side.
E.g. an RGB value of (0.5, 0.5, 0) displaces the shapes by half its size along the X and Y axes.

A parameter value of 1.0 means the full value is added.

recompute_grid_atlas_normal.description:
When True, the normal map provided to the Grid atlas normal image input is ignored and the normals for the patterns provided to the Grid atlas height are computed from scratch.

When False, the normal map provided to the Grid atlas normal is used as is.

Note: The intensity of the normals is adjusted according to the Shape extrude height.

rotation_around_normal.description: The amount of rotation applied uniformly to all shapes around their normal – I.e their local Z-axis – in number of turns.

rotation_around_normal_random.description: Applies a random rotation to each shape around its normal – I.e. its local Z-axis – clockwise or counterclockwise, up to one full turn.

sdf_bounding_frame_size.description:
Defines the maximum bounding box (Bbox) size of the SDF shape, which in turn is used to compute its 2D Bbox.

Shapes are only drawn within the bounds of their 2D Bbox and the rest is trimmed.

shape_extrude_enable_symmetry.description:
Enables forward/backward symmetrical extrusion of the input patterns.

The symmetry axis is the extrusion's halfway point, which means its location may change according to the shapes' pivot position.

shape_extrude_height.description:
The maximum distance of extrusion in image space, where 1 is the image's longest side.

This distance is scaled against the Shape scale value.

shape_extrude_samples.description:
The amount of samples performed to draw the extrusion of the input patterns.

A greater amount results in smoother, more defined extrusions at the cost of some performance.

shape_rotation_vector_map_multiplier.description:
Rotates the shapes around the axis specified by Vector map rotation axis to match the direction of the vectors described by the Vector map texture.
I.e., applies a rotation equal to that of the global X-right vector to the vectors in the texture.

This parameter is a factor for this rotation, where 1 means the full rotation is applied.

This rotation is added to other rotations that may be applied to the shapes.

shape_scale.description:
The size of the shape as a factor defined by the Size mode.

Note: When using the Poisson disc distribution, adjusting the size of the shapes results in them being moved to leverage the available space. Use the Shape scale post Poisson parameter to scale shapes in place.

shape_scale_affects_height_scale.description:
When True, a shape's height scale is adjusted as its scale changes to preserve its proportions.

When False, the height scale is independent from the shape scale, which results in deformation.

shape_scale_map_multiplier.description: The intensity of multiplying the values in the Shape scale map against the size of the shapes.

shape_scale_post_poisson.description: A scale factor applied after the Poisson disc simulation.

shape_scale_random.description: Scales down the shapes by a random factor up to the specified value, where 1 may result in some shapes being scaled all the way down to zero size.

shape_type.description:
There are several types of shape(s) available to be scattered, each offering specific features.

The SDF function is a function graph generating a signed distance field (SDF) describing the surface of a 3D shape. This enables 3D scattering of complex procedural shapes that can vary dynamically.

Primitive shapes, computed using simple ray/surface intersection functions, are ready to use: cube, sphere, cylinder, plane, disc

Input patterns are images provided by the graph. These are mapped to planes and can be extruded into 3D shapes:
  - Image input: The pattern(s) connected to the Pattern input # input pins.
  - Grid atlas: The patterns packed into an atlas image connected to the Grid atlas inputs.

show_shape_2d_bbox.description: Overlays a visualisation of the shape's bounding rectangle in the image. This is the area in which shapes are drawn.

show_shape_3d_bbox.description:
Overlays a visualisation of the shape's bounding volume in 3D space. This is the area in which the SDF shapes and extruded planes are drawn.

For SDF shapes, this areas matches the SDF bounding frame size.

This visualisation helps assess the span and orientation of the shape.

show_shape_pivot.description:
Overlays a visualisation of the shapes pivot, as a combination of its local XYZ axis vectors.

This visualisation helps assess the orientation of the shape as well as the origin of its transformations. (I.e. offset, rotation, scaling)

size_mode.description:
The method of defining the size of the scattered shapes:

- Auto: Size is expressed as a factor of the shape cell size.
- Absolute (texture space): Size is expressed as a factor of the image's longest side.

slope_rotation.description:
Rotates the shapes to match the slope of the background at their location.
I.e., applies a rotation equal to that of the global Z-up vector to the background height's normal.

This parameter is a factor for this rotation, where 1 means the full rotation is applied.

This rotation is added to other rotations that may be applied to the shapes.

slope_rotation_input.description:
The source of the slope used to drive the Slope rotation.

- Background: The Background height texture is used, the normal computed out of that height map is the target direction for the rotation.
- Vector map: The vectors specified by the Vector map texture are used as is for the target direction of the rotation.

smooth_background_slope.description:
The intensity of the smoothing applied to the background height used for the Height offset from background and Conform to background adjustments.

This softens the frequencies of deformation and height offset, which may be harsher than desired.

uv_scale.description: A per-axis multiplier for the UVs of the shapes, where tiling increases with the values.

vector_map_rotation_axis.description:
The axis around which the rotation specified by the Vector map should be performed.

- Normal: Rotates the shapes around their normal, similarly to using the 'Rotation around normal' parameter.
- Z axis: Rotates the shapes around the global Z axis, similarly to using the Z component of the '3D rotation' parameter.

x_amount.description:
The amount of columns in the distribution grid.

I.e. the amount of shapes generated on the X axis.

y_amount.description:
The amount of rows in the distribution grid.

I.e. the amount of shapes generated on the Y axis.

shape_stroke_color.description: Draw a line around or inside a shape.

shape_stroke_grayscale.description: Draw a line around or inside a shape.

slope_blur.description: Directional blur with direction driven by effect map slope

slope_blur_grayscale_2.description:
Directional blur with direction driven by effect map slope grayscale version.

v1.0.1

snow_cover.description: Adds a realistic snow layer on top of a material. The snow has a tweakable thickness and will addapt to the underlying Height-map.

buildup.description: Adds snow buildup in creases and holes.

flakes_intensity.description: Adds flakes in the snow normal map.

fresh_snow.description: Adds snow based on the slope (normal map).

melted_snow.description: Melts the fresh snow but preserves the occluded parts (requires an AO input).

spline_2dtransform.description: Applies a global transformation to all input splines, including inverting their direction.

preview.description: The preview of the input splines as a grayscale image.

spline_coords.description:
The coordinates of the input splines’ points encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - X position
G - Y position
B - Height
A - Packed data:

  • Sign: Spline is closed (negative) or open (positive).
  • Absolute value: Thickness + 1

spline_data.description:
Additional data of the input splines encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - Tangents X
G - Tangents Y
B - Unused
A - Unused

matrix22.description:
The transformation matrix applied to the splines.

Three modes of editing the matrix parameters are available:

  • Transformation gizmo: tweak the handles of the gizmo displayed in the 2D View when the Spline 2D Transform node is selected;
  • Rotation/Stretch: Individually control the rotation and stretching of the splines. Note that values are always applied relatively to the current transformation. E.g., applying 50% width twice results in a 25% width;
  • Matrix values: Click the Edit Matrix Values button to input the raw numerical values of the matrix directly.

offset.description: Applies a position offset to the splines in X (horizontal) and Y (vertical).

segments_amount.description:
Adjusts the number of segments used to draw the spline visualization in the Preview output.

A higher value results in a smoother line.

show_direction_helper.description: Displays a dot at the start of the spline and an arrowhead at its end in the Preview output.

show_thickness_envelope.description: Displays additional lines at the edges of the spline’s thickness.

thickness_px.description: Adjusts the thickness in pixels of the spline visualization in the Preview output.

spline_append.description:
Splines are packaged as a list. This node appends a list of input splines (set #2) onto an existing list (set #1).

The order of the lists is preserved, meaning appending a list D-E-F onto a list A-B-C results in a list A-B-C-D-E-F.

Be mindful of the order in which you append splines, as this order is taken into account in other nodes, such as Scatter on Splines, the Spline Bridge nodes, etc.

preview_2.description: The preview of the second set of input splines as a grayscale image.

spline_amount_2.description: The number of input splines in the second set.
