flip_spline_1_direction.description: Inverts the direction of the splines in the first set.

flip_spline_2_direction.description: Inverts the direction of the splines in the second set.

preview_segment_amount.description:
Adjusts the number of segments used to draw the spline visualization in the Preview output.

A higher value results in a smoother line.

preview_show_direction_helper.description: Displays a dot at the start of the spline and an arrowhead at its end in the Preview output.

show_thickness_envelope.description: Displays additional lines at the edges of the spline’s thickness.

spline_bridge_2_splines.description:
Generates splines from Spline #1 to Spline #2 along these splines. The generated splines can be Linear (straight) or Cubic Bezier (curved).

Note: If the data supplied to the Spline #1 and Spline #2 inputs contain more than one spline, only the last spline in each list is used.

end_tangent_length_curve.description:
The image describing a curve using the values of its first row of pixels.

This input is used to control the length of the ‘in’ tangents for the end point of each generated spline along Spline #2.

You may use a Curve node to author the curve.

end_tangent_rotation_curve.description:
The image describing a curve using the values of its first row of pixels.

This input is used to control the rotation of the ‘in’ tangents for the end point of each generated spline along Spline #2.

The grayscale value of the image represents a number of turns.

You may use a Curve node to author the curve.

preview_1.description: The preview of the input splines #1 as a grayscale image.

preview_2.description: The preview of the input splines #2 as a grayscale image.

G - Y position
B - Height
A - Packed data:

  • Absolute value: Thickness + 1

The image describing a curve using the values of its first row of pixels.

This input is used to control the length of the ‘out’ tangents for the start point of each generated spline along Spline #1.

You may use a Curve node to author the curve.

start_tangent_rotation_curve.description:
The image describing a curve using the values of its first row of pixels.

This input is used to control the rotation of the ‘out’ tangents for the start point of each generated spline along Spline #1.

The grayscale value of the image represents a number of turns.

You may use a Curve node to author the curve.

bridge_splines_amount.description: The number of splines generated along Spline #1 to Spline #2.

bridge_splines_type.description:
The type of spline that is generated:

  • Linear: a straight spline from Start to End;
  • Cubic Bezier: a curved spline from Start to End, the curve being controlled by the length and angle of the Start and End points.

end_random_offset.description:
Applies a random offset to the ending point of each spline along Spline #2. The value is the normalized distance between splines on Spline #2.

When left at 0, the splines are evenly spaced between the Start Spline #2 and End Spline #2 points.

end_tangent_length.description: The length of the ‘in’ tangent for the end point on Spline #2 of all generated splines.

end_tangent_rotation.description:
The rotation of the 'in' tangent for the end point on Spline #2 of all generated splines.

The value is a number of turns.

preview_segment_amount.description:
Adjusts the number of segments used to draw the spline visualization in the Preview output.

A higher value results in a smoother line.

preview_show_direction_helper.description: Displays a dot at the start of the spline and an arrowhead at its end in the Preview output.

show_thickness_envelope.description: Displays additional lines at the edges of the spline’s thickness.

spline_1_end.description:
Offsets the location along Spline #1 up to where splines are generated. The value is the normalized length of Spline #1.

A lower value results in the same number of splines being packed more tightly together.

spline_1_offset.description:
Applies an offset to the starting point of all splines along Spline #1. The value is the normalized length of Spline #1.

Splines that meet the start or end of the spline are left there.

spline_1_start.description:
Offsets the location along Spline #1 from where splines are generated. The value is the normalized length of Spline #1.

A higher value results in the same number of splines being packed more tightly together.

spline_2_end.description:
Offsets the location along Spline #2 up to where splines are generated. The value is the normalized length of Spline #2.

A lower value results in the same number of splines being packed more tightly together.

spline_2_offset.description:
Applies an offset to the starting point of all splines along Spline #2. The value is the normalized length of Spline #2.

Splines that meet the start or end of the spline are left there.

spline_2_start.description:
Offsets the location along Spline #2 from where splines are generated. The value is the normalized length of Spline #2.

A higher value results in the same number of splines being packed more tightly together.

start_random_offset.description:
Applies a random offset to the starting point of each spline along Spline #1. The value is the normalized distance between splines on Spline #1.

When left at 0, the splines are evenly spaced between the Start Spline #1 and End Spline #1 points.

start_tangent_length.description: The length of the ‘out’ tangent for the start point on Spline #1 of all generated splines.

start_tangent_rotation.description:
The rotation of the ‘out’ tangent for the start point on Spline #1 of all generated splines.

The value is a number of turns.

thickness_px.description: Adjusts the thickness in pixels of the spline visualization in the Preview output.

spline_bridge_list_v2.description:
Generates splines traversing all input Splines along them. The generated splines can be Linear (straight) or Quadratic Bezier (curved).

Note: The generated splines go from the first spline in the list to the last and traverse the intermediary splines by strictly following the order of these splines in the list.
Therefore, you should be mindful about the order in which you append splines together beforehand.

preview.description: The preview of the input splines as a grayscale image.

R - X position
G - Y position
B - Height
A - Packed data:

  • Sign: Spline is closed (negative) or open (positive).
  • Absolute value: Thickness + 1

R - Tangents X
G - Tangents Y
B - Unused
A - Unused

bridge_spline_amount.description: The number of splines generated across the input splines.

bridge_splines_type.description:
The type of spline that is generated:

  • Linear: a sharp spline connecting intermediary splines with straight trajectories from Start to End;
  • Quadratic Bezier: a curved spline connecting intermediary splines with smooth trajectories from Start to End.

Note: At least 3 input splines are requires to compute a Quadratic Bezier spline.

close_bridge_spline.description: Extends the traversing splines to connect back to the first spline in the input list.

end_random_offset.description: A multiplier for the random offset applied on the end position of bridge splines, where the maximum distance is specified by the Random offset range parameter.

first_bridge_spline_offset.description:
Applies an offset to the start of all traversed splines. The value is the normalized length of the input splines.

Generated splines that meet the start or end of the traversed splines are left there.

global_random_offset.description: A multiplier for the equal amount of random offset applied on the both the start and end position of bridge splines, where the maximum distance is specified by the Random offset range parameter.

height.description: The absolute height value applied to the bridge splines.

height_mode.description:
The method of acquiring the height value for the bridge splines.

  • Inherit from parent splines: The height of the parent splines at the start and end positions of the bridge splines is used.
  • Override: The arbitrary value you specify in the Height parameter is used.

height_offset.description: The amount of offset applied to the height inherited from the parent splines, before that height is applied to the bridge splines.

height_random.description:
A random amount of adjustment to the height of the bridge splines, where that adjustment depends on the selected Height mode parameter:

  • Inherit from parent splines: The value is a multiplier for the inherited height.
  • Override: The value is an offset added to the height.

input_spline_are_closed.description:
Controls whether the first and last points of input splines should be processed as a single point.

This prevent duplicating the first and last traversing splines.

last_bridge_spline_offset.description:
Applies an offset to the end of all traversed splines. The value is the normalized length of the input splines.

Generated splines that meet the start or end of the traversed splines are left there.

non_square_correction.description:
Adjust the points’ positions and thickness to retain the spline shape in non-square resolutions.

This also impacts uniform distribution.

preview_segment_amount.description:
Adjusts the number of segments used to draw the spline visualization in the Preview output.

A higher value results in a smoother line.

preview_show_direction_helper.description: Displays a dot at the start of the spline and an arrowhead at its end in the Preview output.

random_offset_range.description:
The maximum distance used for the random offset applied on splines.

  • Parent spline: The full length of the parent splines is used. May cause overlaps.
  • Interval: The interval between bridge splines is used. This mitigates overlaps. This distance decreases as the amount of bridge splines increases.

show_thickness_envelope.description: Displays additional lines at the edges of the spline’s thickness.

start_random_offset.description: A multiplier for the random offset applied on the start position of bridge splines, where the maximum distance is specified by the Random offset range parameter.

thickness.description: The absolute thickness value applied to the bridge splines.

thickness_mode.description:
The method of acquiring the thickness value for the bridge splines.

  • Inherit from parent splines: The thickness of the parent splines at the start and end positions of the bridge splines is used.
  • Override: The arbitrary value you specify in the Thickness parameter is used.

thickness_px.description: Adjusts the thickness in pixels of the spline visualization in the Preview output.

thickness_random.description: A random multiplier for the thickness of the bridge splines, where the initial thickness that this multiplier is applied to is specified by the Thickness mode parameter.

uniform_distribution.description: When True, the points of the generated splines are evenly spaced from start to end.

spline_bridge_mapper_color.description:
Maps a color image across a list of input splines so the image traverses the splines in order.

Note: The mapping goes from the first spline in the list to the last and traverses the intermediary splines by strictly following the order of these splines in the list.
Therefore, you should be mindful about the order in which you append splines together beforehand.

color_map.description: The input color image that should be mapped across the input splines.

spline_coords.description:
The coordinates of the input splines’ points encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - X position
G - Y position
B - Height
A - Packed data:

  • Sign: Spline is closed (negative) or open (positive).
  • Absolute value: Thickness + 1

spline_data.description:
Additional data of the input splines encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - Tangents X
G - Tangents Y
B - Unused
A - Unused

color.description: The result of mapping the input Color image across the splines, as a color image.

height.description: The height of the splines mapped across the splines, as a grayscale image.

mask.description: A mask of the mapping across the splines.

uv.description: The UVs (I.e., coordinates) of the mapped image, encoded in the red (U) and green (V) channels of a color image.

background_color.description: The color of the background in the output Color image.

manual_uv_scale.description: Adjusts the scale of the image coordinates. Higher values result in more a densely tiled image.

reduce_uv_stretching.description: Adjusts the method used to interpolate the image coordinates from one spline to the next to minimize stretching when the distance between splines is uneven.

segments_amount.description:
Splines are simplified into segments before image coordinates traverse them.

A higher amount of segments results in a smoother mapping along curves.

uv_rotation.description: Rotates the image coordinates around their center.

spline_bridge_mapper_grayscale.description:
Maps a grayscale image across a list of input splines so the image traverses the splines in order.

Note: The mapping goes from the first spline in the list to the last and traverses the intermediary splines by strictly following the order of these splines in the list.
Therefore, you should be mindful about the order in which you append splines together beforehand.

color_map.description: The input grayscale image that should be mapped across the input splines.

spline_coords.description:
The coordinates of the input splines’ points encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - X position
G - Y position
B - Height
A - Packed data:

  • Sign: Spline is closed (negative) or open (positive).
  • Absolute value: Thickness + 1

A - Unused

height.description: The height of the splines mapped across the splines, as a grayscale image.

mask.description: A mask of the mapping across the splines.

uv.description: The UVs (I.e., coordinates) of the mapped image, encoded in the red (U) and green (V) channels of a color image.

spline_circle.description: Generates a single spline in the shape of a circle.

append_input_spline.description: Adds the generated spline to the end of the list of splines connected to the Spline inputs.

circle_position.description: Sets the position of the centre of the circle in texture space.

circle_post_rotation.description: Applies a rotation to the base circle after Size is applied.

circle_pre_rotation.description: Applies a rotation to the base circle before Size is applied.

circle_radius.description: Adjusts the radius of the circle in texture space.

circle_size.description: Adjusts the horizontal size (X) and vertical size (Y) of the circle.

end_height.description:
Adjusts the height of the circle’s end point where a lower value means a lower or deeper location.

This height is interpolated along the spline from the Start Height.

end_thickness.description:
Adjusts the thickness of the circle’s end point.

This thickness is interpolated along the spline to the Start Thickness.

Note: Thickness is used by specific Spline nodes.

non_square_correction.description:
Adjust the points’ positions and thickness to retain the spline shape in non-square resolutions.

This also impacts uniform distribution.

preview_segment_amount.description:
Adjusts the number of segments used to draw the spline visualization in the Preview output.

A higher value results in a smoother line.

preview_show_direction_helper.description: Displays a dot at the start of the spline and an arrowhead at its end in the Preview output.

show_thickness_envelope.description: Displays additional lines at the edges of the spline’s thickness.

spiral.description:
Displaces the start point of the circle from its radius to its centre.

The distance from the centre is then interpolated along the spline to the end of the spline.

This value is normalised.

spiral_power.description:
Applies a power curve to the distance from the centre used to draw the spiral.

A value higher than one means a greater portion of the spiral remains close to the centre.

spiral_turns.description: Defines the number of turns made by the spiral around its centre.

start_height.description:
Adjusts the height of the circle’s start point where a lower value means a lower or deeper location.

This height is interpolated along the spline to the End Height.

start_thickness.description:
Adjusts the thickness of the circle’s start point.

This thickness is interpolated along the spline to the End Thickness.

Note: Thickness is used by specific Spline nodes.

thickness_px.description: Adjusts the thickness in pixels of the spline visualization in the Preview output.

trim.description:
Offsets the start and end points of the spline along the circle.

These values are normalised.

uniform_distribution.description: When True, the points of the spline are evenly spaced from start to end.

spline_cubic.description:
Generates a single spline between two points p1 and p2 at arbitrary locations.

The trajectory of the spline is controlled by the ‘out’ tangent of p1 and the ‘in’ tangent of p2.

preview.description: The preview of the input splines as a grayscale image.

preview.description: The preview of the output splines as a grayscale image.

spline_coords.description:
The coordinates of the output splines’ points encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - X position
G - Y position
B - Height
A - Packed data:

  • Sign: Spline is closed (negative) or open (positive).
  • Absolute value: Thickness + 1

spline_data.description:
Additional data of the output splines encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - Tangents X
G - Tangents Y
B - Unused
A - Unused

append_input_spline.description: Adds the generated spline to the end of the list of splines connected to the Spline inputs.

auto_tangent_height.description: Automatically sets the height of the spline tangents to interpolate linearly from the Start Height to the End Height.

auto_tangent_thickness.description:
Automatically sets the thickness of the spline tangents to interpolate linearly from the Start Thickness to the End Thickness.

Note: Thickness is used by specific Spline nodes.

end_height.description:
Adjusts the height of the p2 point where a lower value means a lower or deeper location.

This impacts the thickness of the spline at p2.

end_thickness.description:
Adjusts the thickness of the p2 point.

This impacts the thickness of the spline at p2.

Note: Thickness is used by specific Spline nodes.

non_square_correction.description:
Adjust the points’ positions and thickness to retain the spline shape in non-square resolutions.

This also impacts uniform distribution.

p1.description: Sets the position of the p1 point in texture space.

p1_height.description:
Adjusts the height of the p1 point ‘out’ tangent where a lower value means a lower or deeper location.

This impacts the height along the spline as it draws away from p1.

p1_tangent.description: Sets the position of the p1 point ‘out’ tangent handle in texture space.

p1_thickness.description:
Adjusts the thickness of the p1 point ‘out’ tangent.

This impacts the thickness along the spline as it draws away from p1.

Note: Thickness is used by specific Spline nodes.

p2.description: Sets the position of the p2 point in texture space.

p2_height.description:
Adjusts the height of the p2 point ‘in’ tangent where a lower value means a lower or deeper location.

This impacts the height along the spline as it draws away from p2.

p2_tangent.description: Sets the position of the p2 point ‘in’ tangent handle in texture space.

p2_thickness.description:
Adjusts the thickness of the p2 point ‘in’ tangent.

This impacts the thickness along the spline as it draws away from p2.

Note: Thickness is used by specific Spline nodes.

segments_amount.description:
Adjusts the number of segments used to draw the spline visualization in the Preview output.

A higher value results in a smoother line.

show_direction_helper.description: Displays a dot at the start of the spline and an arrowhead at its end in the Preview output.

show_tangents.description: Displays the p1 point ‘out’ tangent and p2 point ‘in’ tangent in the Preview output.

show_thickness_envelope.description: Displays additional lines at the edges of the spline’s thickness.

start_height.description:
Adjusts the height of the p1 point where a lower value means a lower or deeper location.

This impacts the height of the spline at p1.

start_thickness.description:
Adjusts the thickness of the p1 point.

This impacts the thickness of the spline at p1.

Note: Thickness is used by specific Spline nodes.

thickness_px.description: Adjusts the thickness in pixels of the spline visualization in the Preview output.

uniform_distribution.description: When True, the points of the spline are evenly spaced from start to end.

spline_fill.description:
Fills the interior of input Splines with solid white. The exterior is filled with solid black.

Open splines are closed with a straight line from start to end. Intersections where the spline crosses itself are resolved by inverting the interior and exterior sides of the lines at those junctions.

Note: It is not recommended to use this node on splines that are out of the [0,1] tile. The filling process is unreliable in that case.

preview.description: The preview of the input splines as a grayscale image.

spline_coords.description:
The coordinates of the input splines’ points encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - X position
G - Y position
B - Height
A - Packed data:

  • Sign: Spline is closed (negative) or open (positive).
  • Absolute value: Thickness + 1

spline_data.description:
Additional data of the input splines encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - Tangents X
G - Tangents Y
B - Unused
A - Unused

output.description: The result image of filling the input Splines with flat white against a flat black background.

spline_flow_mapper.description:
Draws a flow map where flow vector data is drawn along the input splines.

This lets you use splines to control the direction, trajectory, intensity and thickness of flow, as well as the gradient ramp used to fade the drawn data into the neutral background.

attenuation_profile_curve.description:
The image describing a curve using the values of its first row of pixels.

When the Attenuation Profile parameter is set to Input Profile Curve, this input is used to control the gradient ramp for the attenuation of the flow vector data drawn along the spline.

You may use a Curve node to author the curve.

spline_coords.description:
The coordinates of the input splines’ points encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - X position
G - Y position
B - Height
A - Packed data:

  • Sign: Spline is closed (negative) or open (positive).
  • Absolute value: Thickness + 1

spline_data.description:
Additional data of the input splines encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - Tangents X
G - Tangents Y
B - Unused
A - Unused

output.description:
The output flow vectors encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image.

R - X coordinate
G - Y coordinate
B - Unused
A - Unused

attenuation_profile.description:
The gradient ramp used to draw the attenuation of the flow vector data drawn along the spline:

  • Linear: Use a linear gradient ramp;
  • Gaussian: Use a gaussian gradient ramp;
  • Input Profile Curve: Use the curve provided to the Attenuation Profile Curve input as a gradient ramp.

direction.description:
The direction of the vector flow in relation to the spline:

  • Tangent: Use the spline’s tangent vector;
  • Normal: Use the spline’s normal vector;
  • Normal Mirrored: Use the mirrored version of the spline’s normal vector.

draw_spline_index.description: The index of the spline along which vector flow data should be drawn.

draw_spline_range.description: The range of indexes for the splines along which vector flow data should be drawn.

end_attenuation.description: Adds a half-circle at the end of the spline. The half-circle uses the same attenuation as the spline.

flip_direction.description: Inverts the direction of the splines, which also impacts the direction of the flow vector.

mode.description:
The method of selecting the splines along which vector flow data should be drawn:

  • Draw Spline List: All the splines in the input list are used;
  • Draw Single Spline: Only the spline with the specified index is used;
  • Draw Spline Range: Only the splines which index is included in the specified range are used.

non_square_correction.description:
Adjust the points’ positions and thickness to retain the spline shape in non-square resolutions.

This also impacts uniform distribution.

segments_amount.description:
Splines are simplified into segments before vector flow data traverses them.

A higher amount of segments results in a smoother flow mapping along curves.

spline_height_attenuation.description: The intensity of the flow vector data drawn along the spline is multiplied against the spline’s height, where the drawn data fades to the background’s neutral (0.5, 0.5, 0) color as the height gets closer to 0.

start_attenuation.description: Adds a half-circle at the start of the spline. The half-circle uses the same attenuation as the spline.

thickness.description: The arbitrary value for the thickness of the vector flow data drawn along the splines.

thickness_mode.description:
The method of setting the thickness of the drawn vector flow data:

  • Manual: Set the thickness explicitly with an arbitrary value;
  • From Spline: Use the thickness of the spline.

thickness_multiplier.description: A global multiplier for the thickness of the vector flow data drawn along the splines, when that thickness it driven by that of the splines.

spline_mapper_color.description:
Maps an input color image on a primitive shape stretched along the input splines.

The primitive shape can be a plane, a half-cylinder or cylinder. The cylinders can be twisted along the spline to deform the mapped image accordingly.

The node outputs the mapped image as a color image, as well as other information such as height, UVs (I.e., image coordinates) and an ID mask for selecting each mapped spline independently.

color_map.description: The input color image that should be mapped along the input splines.

height_map.description: The input grayscale height map that should be mapped along the input splines.

spline_coords.description:
The coordinates of the input splines’ points encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - X position
G - Y position
B - Height
A - Packed data:

  • Sign: Spline is closed (negative) or open (positive).
  • Absolute value: Thickness + 1

spline_data.description:
Additional data of the input splines encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - Tangents X
G - Tangents Y
B - Unused
A - Unused

twist_curve.description:
The image describing a curve using the values of its first row of pixels.

When the Shape parameter is set to Half-Cylinder or Cylinder, this input is used to control the twisting of the UVs around the shape. Its impact is controlled using the Twist UVs Curve Multiplier parameter.

The curve provides a profile for the amount of rotation along the spline, where the first pixel in the row is the rotation at the start of the spline, and the last is the rotation at the end. The grayscale value represents a number of turns.

You may use a Curve node to author the curve.

color.description: The result of mapping the Color input image across the input splines, as a color image.

height.description: The result of mapping the Height input image across the input splines, as a grayscale image.

id.description: A mask of the images mapped along the input splines, where the white values are incremented by 1 from one spline to the next so that each shape can be selected independently.

uv.description: The UVs (I.e., coordinates) of the mapping across the input splines, encoded in a color image.

auto_scale_uvs.description: Adjusts the scale of the coordinates automatically to retain a square image when mapping it along the splines.

auto_uv_scale.description:
Adjusts the scale of the mapped coordinates in X (horizontally) and Y (vertically).

Higher values result in a more densely tiled image.

background_color.description: The color of the background in the Color output.

cylinder_height_multiplier.description:
A multiplier for the intensity of the cylinder’s height contribution in the Height output.

Height adjustments are cumulative.

cylinder_height_offset.description: Offsets the center of the Cylinder or Half-Cylinder shape profile from the spline's surface to one diameter beneath the surface.

draw_spline_index.description: The index of the spline along which the image should be mapped.

draw_spline_range.description: The range of indexes for the splines along which the image should be mapped.

end.description:
Offsets the end of the portion of the spline which should be mapped.

The value represents the normalized length of the spline.

input_map_height_multplier.description:
Adjusts the intensity of the Height Map input’s contribution to the Height output.

Height adjustments are cumulative.

manual_uv_scale.description:
Adjusts the scale of the mapped coordinates in X (horizontally) and Y (vertically).

Higher values result in a more densely tiled image.

mode.description:
The method of selecting the splines along which the image should be mapped:

  • Draw Spline List: All the splines in the input list are used;
  • Draw Single Spline: Only the spline with the specified index is used;
  • Draw Spline Range: Only the splines which index is included in the specified range are used.

non_square_correction.description:
Adjust the points’ positions and thickness to retain the spline shape in non-square resolutions.

This also impacts uniform distribution.

segments_amount.description:
Splines are simplified into segments before image coordinates traverse them.

A higher amount of segments results in a smoother mapping along curves.

shape.description:
The primitive shape used to map image coordinates along the splines:

  • Plane: Coordinates are mapped to a flat plane;
  • Half Cylinder: Coordinates are mapped to a half-cylinder which base circle’s axis follows the direction of the spline;
  • Cylinder: Coordinates are mapped to a cylinder which base circle’s axis follows the direction of the spline.

spline_height_multiplier.description:
Adjusts the intensity of the Spline Height input’s contribution to the Height output.

Height adjustments are cumulative.

start.description:
Offsets the start of the portion of the spline which should be mapped.

The value represents the normalized length of the spline.

thickness.description: The arbitrary value for the thickness of the mapped image along the splines.

thickness_mode.description:
The method of setting the thickness of the mapped image:

  • Manual: Set the thickness explicitly with an arbitrary value;
  • From Spline: Use the thickness of the spline.

thickness_multiplier.description: A global multiplier for the thickness of the mapped image along the splines, when that thickness it driven by that of the splines.

twist_uvs_curve_multiplier.description:
A multiplier for the intensity of the Twist Curve input’s contribution to the twisting of the cylinder.

The curve provides a profile for the amount of rotation along the spline, where the first pixel in the row is the rotation at the start of the spline, and the last is the rotation at the end. The grayscale value represents a number of turns.

twist_uvs_curve_offset.description: Applies a global offset to the rotation values supplied by the Twist Curve input, in number of turns.

twist_uvs_intensity.description:
The twisting of the image coordinates around the cylinder, in number of turns.

Twisting involves rotating the cylinder at the end of the spline only. The rotation is then interpolated along the spline.

spline_mapper_grayscale.description:
Maps an input grayscale image on a primitive shape stretched along the input splines.

The primitive shape can be a plane, a half-cylinder or cylinder. The cylinders can be twisted along the spline to deform the mapped image accordingly.

The node outputs the mapped image as a grayscale image, as well as other information such as height, UVs (I.e., image coordinates) and an ID mask for selecting each mapped spline independently.

  • From Spline: Use the thickness of the spline.

thickness_multiplier.description: A global multiplier for the thickness of the mapped image along the splines, when that thickness it driven by that of the splines.

twist_uvs_curve_multiplier.description:
A multiplier for the intensity of the Twist Curve input’s contribution to the twisting of the cylinder.

The curve provides a profile for the amount of rotation along the spline, where the first pixel in the row is the rotation at the start of the spline, and the last is the rotation at the end. The grayscale value represents a number of turns.

spline_merge_list.description:
Merges all splines in the input list into a single spline by connecting their extremities in order.

Note: Splines are connected by strictly following the order of these splines in the list.
Therefore, you should be mindful about the order in which you append splines together beforehand.

closed_spline_distance_threshold.description:
The distance in texture space below which two extremities of a same spline are processed as a single point closing that spline.

This prevents overlaps when scattering shapes or mapping images along the splines.

preview_segment_amount.description:
Adjusts the number of segments used to draw the spline visualization in the Preview output.

A higher value results in a smoother line.

preview_show_direction_helper.description: Displays a dot at the start of the spline and an arrowhead at its end in the Preview output.

show_thickness_envelope.description: Displays additional lines at the edges of the spline’s thickness.

thickness_px.description: Adjusts the thickness in pixels of the spline visualization in the Preview output.

spline_poly_quadratic.description:
Generates a spline along several points. The amount and locations of these points may be arbitrary or gathered from a Point List node.

The trajectory of the spline can be smoothed away from its intermediary points, in that each intermediary point is the meeting point of the ‘out’ and ‘in’ tangents of its neighbours.

point_list_input.description:
An input list of points encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image.

R - X position
G - Y position
B - Height
A - Packed data:

  • Integer part: Smoothness;
  • Fractional part: Thickness.

points_preview.description: The preview of points as a grayscale image.

append_input_spline.description: Adds the generated spline to the end of the list of splines connected to the Spline inputs.

close_spline.description:
Controls whether the end point of the spline should be connected to its start point.

The smoothing applied to the spline at the start and end points is specified by the Smoothness values of those points.

connect_end_to_input_spline.description: When True, the end of the generated spline is connected to the first point of the first spline in the input splines.

connect_start_to_input_spline.description: When True, the start of the generated spline is connected to the last point of the last spline in the input splines.

end_connection_position.description:
The position on the selected input spline where the connection to the end the generated spline should land.

This value is the normalized length of the selected input spline.

end_connection_spline_index.description: The index of the input spline which should be connected to the end of the generated spline.

global_smoothness_adjustment.description:
Applies a uniform offset to the smoothness value of all points.

The resulting smoothness value is clamped to the [0;1] range.

input_spline_connection_mode.description:
The method used to connect the input Splines:

  • - Auto: The end of the last input spline is connected to the start of the generated spline, and the end to the generated spline is connected to the start of the first input spline;
  • - Manual: You can specify which of the input splines should be connected to the extremities of the generated spline, and where on the input splines these connections should land.

non_square_correction.description:
Adjust the points’ positions and thickness to retain the spline shape in non-square resolutions.

This also impacts uniform distribution.

p1.description: Sets the position of the p# point in texture space.

p10.description: Sets the position of the p# point in texture space.

p10_properties.description:
Sets the properties of the p# point:

  • Height: Adjusts the height of the point where a lower value means a lower or deeper location;
  • Smoothness: Offsets the start of the smoothing of the spline at p#, where a value of 0 results in a hard trajectory and 1 in an entirely smooth one;
  • Thickness: Adjusts the thickness of the spline at p#. Thickness is used by specific Spline nodes.

  • Thickness: Adjusts the thickness of the spline at p#. Thickness is used by specific Spline nodes.

p9.description: Sets the position of the p# point in texture space.

p9_properties.description:
Sets the properties of the p# point:

  • Height: Adjusts the height of the point where a lower value means a lower or deeper location;
  • Smoothness: Offsets the start of the smoothing of the spline at p#, where a value of 0 results in a hard trajectory and 1 in an entirely smooth one;
  • Thickness: Adjusts the thickness of the spline at p#. Thickness is used by specific Spline nodes.

point_amount.description: The arbitrary number of points used to build the spline.

points_label_size.description: The size of the label for each point in texture space, where 0.1 is a tenth of the texture's width.

points_size.description: The radius of the points in texture space, where 0.1 is a tenth of the texture's width.

preview_segment_amount.description:
Adjusts the number of segments used to draw the spline visualization in the Preview output.

A higher value results in a smoother line.

preview_show_direction_helper.description: Displays a dot at the start of the spline and an arrowhead at its end in the Preview output.

show_points.description: Displays the control points for the spline in the Preview output.

show_points_label.description: For each point, displays the point's name next to it in the Preview output.

show_tangents.description: Displays the points' tangents in the Preview output.

show_thickness_envelope.description: Displays additional lines at the edges of the spline’s thickness.

start_connection_position.description:
The position on the selected input spline where the connection to the start of the generated spline should land.

This value is the normalized length of the selected input spline.

start_connection_spline_index.description: The index of the input spline which should be connected to the start of the generated spline.

thickness_px.description: Adjusts the thickness in pixels of the spline visualization in the Preview output.

uniform_distribution.description: When True, the points of the spline are evenly spaced from start to end.

use_input_point_list.description: Use the list of points supplied to the Input Point List and Point Number input connectors instead of an arbitrary list of points.

spline_quadratic.description:
Generates a single spline between two points p1 and p3 at arbitrary locations.

The trajectory of the spline is controlled by the ‘out’ tangent of p1 and the ‘in’ tangent of p3, both controlled by a single point p3.

The span of the arc formed by the spline is adjustable, so that some of its trajectory from its extremities can remain straight.

preview.description: The preview of the input splines as a grayscale image.

spline_coords.description:
The coordinates of the output splines’ points encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - X position
G - Y position
B - Height
A - Packed data:

  • Sign: Spline is closed (negative) or open (positive).
  • Absolute value: Thickness + 1

spline_data.description:
Additional data of the input splines encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - Tangents X
G - Tangents Y
B - Tangents Z
A - Unused

preview.description: The preview of the output splines as a grayscale image.

The coordinates of the output splines’ points encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - X position
G - Y position
B - Height

R - Tangents X

append_input_spline.description: Adds the generated spline to the end of the list of splines connected to the Spline inputs.

auto_tangent_height.description: Automatically sets the height of the spline tangents to interpolate linearly from the Start Height to the End Height.

auto_tangent_thickness.description:
Automatically sets the thickness of the spline tangents to interpolate linearly from the Start Thickness to the End Thickness.

Note: Thickness is used by specific Spline nodes.

end_height.description:
Adjusts the height of the p3 point where a lower value means a lower or deeper location.

This impacts the thickness of the spline at p3.

end_thickness.description:
Adjusts the thickness of the p3 point.

This impacts the thickness of the spline at p3.

Note: Thickness is used by specific Spline nodes.

non_square_correction.description:
Adjust the points’ positions and thickness to retain the spline shape in non-square resolutions.

This also impacts uniform distribution.

p1.description: Sets the position of the p1 point in texture space.

p2.description:
Sets the position of the p2 point in texture space.

The p2 point controls the tangents of both p1 and p3 points.

p3.description: Sets the position of the p3 point in texture space.

segments_amount.description:
Adjusts the number of segments used to draw the spline visualization in the Preview output.

A higher value results in a smoother line.

show_direction_helper.description: Displays a dot at the start of the spline and an arrowhead at its end in the Preview output.

show_tangents.description: Displays the p1 point ‘out’ tangent and p3 point ‘in’ tangent in the Preview output.

show_thickness_envelope.description: Displays additional lines at the edges of the spline’s thickness.

smoothness.description:
Adjusts the span of the arc formed by the spline, where 1 means the full length of the spline is arched and 0 means the spline is entirely straight.

The arc progresses from point p3 along the spline up to its extremities.

start_height.description:
Adjusts the height of the p1 point where a lower value means a lower or deeper location.

This impacts the height of the spline at p1.

start_thickness.description:
Adjusts the thickness of the p1 point.

This impacts the thickness of the spline at p1.

Note: Thickness is used by specific Spline nodes.

tangent_height.description:
Adjusts the height driven by the tangents controlled by the p2 point.

This impacts the height along the spline as it draws away from p1 and goes into p3.

tangent_thickness.description:
Adjusts the thickness driven by the tangents controlled by the p2 point.

This impacts the thickness along the spline as it draws away from p1 and goes into p3.

Note: Thickness is used by specific Spline nodes.

thickness_px.description: Adjusts the thickness in pixels of the spline visualization in the Preview output.

uniform_distribution.description: When True, the points of the spline are evenly spaced from start to end.

spline_render.description: Draws strings of segments along the input Splines over the input Background.

background.description: The grayscale image over which splines should be drawn.

spline_coords.description:
The coordinates of the input splines’ points encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - X position
G - Y position
B - Height
A - Packed data:

  • Sign: Spline is closed (negative) or open (positive).
  • Absolute value: Thickness + 1

spline_data.description:
Additional data of the input splines encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - Tangents X
G - Tangents Y
B - Unused
A - Unused

output.description: The result image of drawing the input Splines on top of the Background.

background_intensity.description: The value multiplied against the Background input image.

blend_mode.description:
The method of blending the colors of the Background and overlapping segments drawn along the splines:

  • Max: The brightest value is used;
  • Add: The values are added together.

draw_spline_index.description: The index of the spline that should be drawn.

draw_spline_range.description: The range of indexes for the splines that should be drawn.

enable_joints.description: Fills the gaps between the individual segments drawn along the splines, using discs.

end.description:
Offsets the end of the portion of the spline which should be drawn.

The value represents the normalized length of the spline.

envelope_spline_amount.description: The number of duplicate segments that should be drawn along each spline’s thickness.

mode.description:
The method of selecting which splines should be drawn:

  • Draw Spline List: Draw all splines in the input list;
  • Draw Single Spline: Draw only the specified spline from the input list;
  • Draw Spline Range: Draw only the splines in the specified range from the input list.

non_square_correction.description:
Adjust the points’ positions and thickness to retain the spline shape in non-square resolutions.

This also impacts uniform distribution.

random_luminance.description: For each string of uncut segments in a spline, applies a random offset in the specified range to the grayscale value used to draw that string.

random_segments_end.description: Adjusts the probability that the string of segments closer to the end of the spline is cut.

random_segments_random_offset.description:
Sets the maximum amount of displacement applied to each cut segment along its normal.

This parameter has no effect when Random Segments Start and Random Segments End are both set to 0.

random_segments_random_offset_center.description: Offsets the center of the random displacement applied to each cut segment along its normal.

random_segments_start.description: Adjusts the probability that the string of segments closer to the start of the spline is cut.

segments_amount.description:
Adjusts the number of segments drawn along the splines.

A higher value results in smoother lines.

show_direction_helper.description: For each spline, draws a dot at the start of the spline and an arrowhead at its end.

spline_color.description:
The uniform grayscale value used to draw the segments.

When a Spline Style other than Solid is selected, this color is multiplied against the styled color.

spline_style.description:
The method used to color the splines:

  • Solid: The segments are drawn using a uniform grayscale value;
  • Gradient: A gradient from black to white is applied along each string of segments from start to end;
  • Height: The height of the splines is used as the grayscale value for drawing the segments.

start.description:
Offsets the start of the portion of the spline which should be drawn.

The value represents the normalized length of the spline.

thickness_image.description: The thickness of the drawn segments normalized in texture space, where 1 is the full width of the image.

thickness_px.description: The thickness of the drawn segments as an absolute number of pixels in the texture, where 1 is a full pixel.

thickness_size_mode.description:
The method of computing the thickness of the drawn segments:

  • Image: the value is normalized in texture space, where 1 is the full width of the image. Thickness is relative to the texture resolution;
  • Pixel: the value is an absolute number of pixels in the texture, where 1 is a full pixel. Thickness is separate from the texture resolution.

spline_sample_height.description:
Modifies the height of the input splines by mapping an input Height Map onto them.

The effect of the mapped height map can be adjusted by changing its blending mode and the opacity of that effect.

height_map.description: The input grayscale image used to change the input Splines’ height.

preview.description: The preview of the input splines as a grayscale image.

spline_coords.description:
The coordinates of the input splines’ points encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - X position
G - Y position
B - Height
A - Packed data:

  • Sign: Spline is closed (negative) or open (positive).
  • Absolute value: Thickness + 1

G - Tangents Y

blending_mode.description:
The method of blending the Height Map’s data with the input spline’s height:

  • Copy: Override the spline’s height with the Height Map values;
  • Add: Add the Height Map values to the spline’s height;
  • Subtract: Subtract the Height Map values to the spline’s height;
  • Multiply: Multiply the Height Map values against the spline’s height.

opacity.description: A multiplier for the intensity of the Height Map input’s contribution to the spline’s height.

preview_segment_amount.description:
Adjusts the number of segments used to draw the spline visualization in the Preview output.

A higher value results in a smoother line.

preview_show_direction_helper.description: Displays a dot at the start of the spline and an arrowhead at its end in the Preview output.

sampling_mode.description:
The method of mapping the values in the Height Map input to the splines:

  • Texture space: The values are applied to the splines where they would be if placed in a texture using the texture’s UV coordinates. This effectively applies the value to the splines ‘in place’;
  • Horizontal along spline: The values are applied to the encoded splines’ coordinates directly (see Spline Coords input), where each row is applied to a different spline from top to bottom;
  • Hor. along spline (rand. offset X): The values are applied to the encoded splines’ coordinates directly (see Spline Coords input), with a random horizontal offset in the Height Map for each spline (I.e., each row in Spline Coords);
  • Hor. along spline (rand. offset Y): The values are applied to the encoded splines’ coordinates directly (see Spline Coords input), with a random vertical offset in the Height Map for each spline (I.e., each row in Spline Coords).

show_thickness_envelope.description: Displays additional lines at the edges of the spline’s thickness.

thickness_px.description: Adjusts the thickness in pixels of the spline visualization in the Preview output.

spline_sample_thickness.description:
Modifies the thickness of the input splines by mapping an input Thickness Map onto them.

The effect of the mapped thickness map can be adjusted by changing its blending mode and the opacity of that effect.

The method of mapping the values in the Thickness Map input to the splines:

  • Texture space: The values are applied to the splines where they would be if placed in a texture using the texture’s UV coordinates. This effectively applies the value to the splines ‘in place’;

Selects splines in the input list according to the specified criteria, and outputs a new list including the selected splines only.

Selected splines can also be trimmed.

range_start.description: The lowest index in the range of selected splines.

selection_mode.description:

  • First: Selects the first spline in the list;
  • Last: Selects the last spline in the list;
  • Index: Selects the spline with specified index;
  • Range: Selects the splines which indexes are included in the specified range.

show_thickness_envelope.description: Displays additional lines at the edges of the spline’s thickness.

spline_index.description: The index of the spline which should be selected.

start.description:
Offsets the start of the portion of the spline which should be selected. This effectively trims the spline.

The value represents the normalized length of the spline.

thickness_px.description: Adjusts the thickness in pixels of the spline visualization in the Preview output.

spline_warp.description:
Displaces the input splines based on the input Intensity Map or Vector Map.

The intensity of the warping effect can be adjusted along the spline using attenuation controls.

attenuation_curve.description:
The grayscale image describing a curve using the values of its first row of pixels.

When the Use Attenuation Curve parameter is set to True, this input is used to control the attenuation of the warping effect near the start and end of the spline.

The curve provides a profile for the attenuation, where the first pixel in the row is the intensity of the warping effect at the start of the spline, and the last is the intensity at the end. The grayscale value is the intensity.

You may use a Curve node to author the curve.

intensity_map.description:
The input grayscale image used to control the direction and intensity of the warping effect on the input splines.

The color of each pixel in the image specifies a multiplier for displacing the spline’s points along their normal (I.e., the direction perpendicular to the spline), up to the full span of the image.

The [0; 1] values in the image are remapped to the [-1; 1] range when read as a multiplier: 0 and 1 displace the spline by the same distance but in opposite directions. 0.5 leaves the spline in place.

preview.description: The preview of the input splines as a grayscale image.

spline_coords.description:
The coordinates of the input splines’ points encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - X position
G - Y position
B - Height
A - Packed data:

  • Sign: Spline is closed (negative) or open (positive).
  • Absolute value: Thickness + 1

spline_data.description:
Additional data of the input splines encoded in the RGBA channels of a color image:

R - Tangents X
G - Tangents Y
B - Unused
A - Unused

vector_map.description:
The input color image used to control the direction and intensity of the warping effect on the input splines.

The color of each pixel in the image specifies vector (X, Y) which coordinates are encoded in the red (X) and green (Y) channels. +X is right and +Y is down.

The [0; 1] values in the image are remapped to the [-1; 1] range when read as vector coordinates: 0 red displaces points left and 0 green displaces points up. 0.5 red and green leaves the spline in place.

background_intensity.description: The value multiplied against the Preview background input image.

end_attenuation.description:
A multiplier for the attenuation of the warping effect near the end of the spline.

A value of 1 means no warping is applied to the end of the spline.

intensity_map_tiling.description: Adjusts the tiling of the Intensity Map when mapped to the spline coordinates directly (see Spline Coords input).

preview_segment_amount.description:
Adjusts the number of segments used to draw the spline visualization in the Preview output.

A higher value results in a smoother line.

preview_show_direction_helper.description: Displays a dot at the start of the spline and an arrowhead at its end in the Preview output.

recompute_tangents.description:
When True, a spline’s tangents are recomputed after the warping effect is applied.

This ensures the spline’s tangents remain consistent with its trajectory when used in nodes such as Scatter on Spline or Spline Flow Mapper.

sampling_mode.description:
The method of mapping the values in the Intensity Map or Vector Map to the splines:

  • Texture space: The values are applied to the splines where they would be if placed in a texture using the texture’s UV coordinates. This effectively applies the value to the splines ‘in place’;
  • Horizontal along spline: The values are applied to the encoded splines’ coordinates directly (see Spline Coords input), where each row is applied to a different spline from top to bottom;
  • Hor. along spline (rand. offset X): The values are applied to the encoded splines’ coordinates directly (see Spline Coords input), with a random horizontal offset in the Intensity Map or Vector Map for each spline (I.e., each row in Spline Coords);
  • Hor. along spline (rand. offset Y): The values are applied to the encoded splines’ coordinates directly (see Spline Coords input), with a random vertical offset in the Intensity Map or Vector Map for each spline (I.e., each row in Spline Coords).

show_thickness_envelope.description: Displays additional lines at the edges of the spline’s thickness.

start_attenuation.description:
A multiplier for the attenuation of the warping effect near the start of the spline.

A value of 1 means no warping is applied to the start of the spline.

thickness_px.description: Adjusts the thickness in pixels of the spline visualization in the Preview output.

use_attenuation_curve.description:
Enables controlling the intensity of the warping effect along a spline using a curve encoded in the Attenuation Curve input image.

The curve provides a profile for the attenuation, where the first pixel in the row is the intensity of the warping effect at the start of the spline, and the last is the intensity at the end. The grayscale value is the intensity.

You may use a Curve node to author the curve.

use_vector_map.description:
Switches the method of displacing the splines to the use of a Vector Map input for specifying the direction of the displacement.

The color of each pixel in the image specifies vector (X, Y) which coordinates are encoded in the red (X) and green (Y) channels. +X is right and +Y is down.

The [0; 1] values in the image are remapped to the [-1; 1] range when read as vector coordinates: 0 red displaces points left and 0 green displaces points up. 0.5 red and green leaves the spline in place.

warp_center.description:
Specifies the Intensity Map value that corresponds to leaving the splines in place.

A value of 0 or 1 means the splines can only be displaced on one side.

warp_intensity.description: The intensity by which the splines are displaced.

straight_to_premult.description: Straight Alpha to Pre-multiplied Alpha conversion.

luminance_by_scale.description: Small patterns will be darker than large patterns.

tile_random_2.description:
Generates adjacent tiles of random sizes and height-to-width ratios.

The grid may be tweaked by randomly slanting the sides of the shapes to break up the angles.

Shapes may be adjusted with options for scaling, bevelling, rounding of the corners as well as disorted rotation.

These adjustments may be controlled by input maps.

A dedicated output lets you input the shape's UVs into Flood Fill to (...) nodes for applying additional variation.

bevel_distance_map.description:
The grayscale input image which controls the bevelling of the shapes.

Its impact is controlled by the Bevel Distance Input Map Mult. parameter.

mask_map.description:
The grayscale input image which controls the masking of the shapes.

Its impact is controlled by the Mask Map Input Start and Mask Map Input End parameters.

random_size_map.description:
The grayscale input image which controls the random scale of the shapes.

Its impact is controlled by the Random Size Input Map Multiplier parameter.

random_slant_map.description:
The grayscale input image which controls the random slanting of the shapes.

Its impact is controlled by the Random Slant Input Map Multiplier parameter.

round_corners_radius_map.description:
The grayscale input image which controls the radius of the shapes' rounded corners.

Its impact is controlled by the Round Corners Radius Input Map Mult. parameter.

flood_fill_data1.description:
The computed shapes' UV data.

This output should be connected to Flood Fill to (...) nodes.

output.description: The output image.

amount_x.description: The number of cells on the X axis.

amount_y.description: The number of cells on the Y axis.

bevel_distance_input_map_multiplier.description: Adjusts the distance of the bevelling using the Bevel Distance Map input map as a per-pixel multiplier.

bevel_distance_mode.description:
Sets the method of acquiring the distance by which shapes should be beveled:

- Relative to Grid Size: Shapes are beveled by the specified proportion of their grid size
- Relative to Shape Size: Shapes are beveled by the specified proportion of their size
- Relative to Image Size: Shapes are beveled by the specified proportion of the image.

bevel_distance_multiplier.description: Applies a global adjustment to the distance of the bevelling.

bevel_distance_x.description: Adjusts the absolute distance of the bevelling on the X axis only. This distance depends on the value of the Bevel Distance Mode parameter.

bevel_distance_y.description: Adjusts the absolute distance of the bevelling on the Y axis only. This distance depends on the value of the Bevel Distance Mode parameter.

bevel_rounded_curve.description: Adjusts the intensity of the rounding applied to the bevelling angle to make it more convex.

enable_per_axis_bevel_control.description:
When True, bevelling can be applied and adjusted separately on the X and Y axes.

Note

This cancels the Round Corners effect.

interstice_thickness.description: Adjusts the thickness of the gap between shapes. It is equal for all shapes.

mask_by_cell_area_end.description:
Adjusts the maximum cell's area threshold for masking shapes.

Note

This determines one limit of a value range for masking. The value may therefore be lower than the Mask by Cell Area Start value.

mask_by_cell_area_invert.description: Inverts the masking of shapes by the area of their cells.

mask_by_cell_area_start.description:
Adjusts the minimum cell's area threshold for masking shapes.

Note

This determines one limit of a value range for masking. The value may therefore be greater than the Mask by Cell Area End value.

mask_map_input_end.description:
Adjusts the maximum grayscale value threshold in the Mask Map input map for masking shapes.

Note

This determines one limit of a value range for masking. The value may therefore be lower than the Mask Map Input Start value.

mask_map_input_invert.description: Inverts the masking of shapes by the Mask Map input map.

mask_map_input_start.description:
Adjusts the minimum grayscale value threshold in the Mask Map input map for masking shapes.

Note

This determines one limit of a value range for masking. The value may therefore be greater than the Mask Map Input End value.

mask_random_end.description:
For a given Random Seed, pseudo-random masking is applied following a specific order from a start shape to an end shape. This parameter lets you offset the index of the end shape.

Note

This determines one limit of a value range for masking. The value may therefore be lower than the Mask Random Start value.

mask_random_invert.description: Inverts the random masking of shapes.

mask_random_start.description:
For a given Random Seed, pseudo-random masking is applied following a specific order from a start shape to an end shape. This parameter lets you offset the index of the start shape.

Note

This determines one limit of a value range for masking. The value may therefore be greater than the Mask Random End value.

random_position_multiplier.description: Applies a random position offset to the shape up to it meeting its cell's border.

random_size_distribution.description:
Controls the method of distributing random scaling values:

- Uniform: the random scale is applied the same way on all cells
- Blue Noise: the random scale is adjusted using a blue noise pattern

random_size_input_map_multiplier.description: Adjusts the intensity of the random scaling using the values sampled from the Random Size Map input.

random_size_multiplier.description: Applies a global adjustment to the intensity of the random scaling.

random_size_x.description: Adjusts the intensity of the random scaling on the X axis only.

random_size_y.description: Adjusts the intensity of the random scaling on the Y axis only.

random_slant_distribution.description:
Controls the method of distributing random slanting values:

- Uniform: the random slant is applied the same way on all cells
- Blue Noise: the random slant is adjusted using a blue noise pattern

random_slant_input_map_multiplier.description: Adjusts the intensity of the random slanting using the values sampled from the Random Slant Map input.

random_slant_multiplier.description: Applies a global adjustment to the intensity of the random slanting.

random_slant_x.description: Adjusts the intensity of the random slanting on the X axis only.

random_slant_y.description: Adjusts the intensity of the random slanting on the Y axis only.

round_corners_radius.description:
Adjusts the radius of the rounded corners of the shapes. A value of 0 means no rounding is applied.

Note

This effect cannot be applied when the Enable per Axis Bevel Control parameter is set to True.

round_corners_radius_input_map_multiplie.description:
Adjusts the intensity by which the Round Corners Radius Map input map impacts the radius of the rounded corners.
The map acts as a per-pixel multiplier for the Round Corners Radius parameter.

Note

This effect cannot be applied when the Enable per Axis Bevel Control parameter is set to True.
round_corners_radius_input_map_multiplie.display_name: Round corners radius input map mult.

shape_corners_position_random.description: Distorts the shapes by applying a random amount of offset to each of their corners along their cell's border.

shape_rotation.description:
Rotates shapes in their cells by moving each corner to its neighbour along the cell's border.

This method results in some amount of distortion and scaling being applied to the shape at is rotates.

shape_rotation_random.description:
Adjusts the intensity by which a random amount of rotation is applied to each shape.

The method of rotation is described in the Rotation parameter.

shape_scale_multiplier.description: Adjusts the size of each shape, as a proportion of the area of its cell.

shape_scale_random_multiplier.description: Adjusts the intensity by which a random scale is applied to each shape.

tile_sampler.description: This tile generator can sample in a texture to parametrize each pattern properties (size, position, rotation, color).

tile_sampler_color.description: This tile generator can sample in a texture to parametrize each pattern properties (size, position, rotation, color).

tileable_material_to_mesh_material.description: Add details to specular and diffuse map or atlas such as lighting, AO, sharp edges etc.

AO_Intensity.description: Set the AO multiplication intensity in diffuse map and specular maps.

Edges_Sharpness_Intensity.description: Darken inner edges and brighter sharp edges in diffuse map.

Pre_Lighting_Intensity.description: Set the prelighting intensity in diffuse map and specular maps.

contrast.description: Set the diffuse map contrast

normal_intensity.description: Set the normal map strength intensity. 0 = Null 1 = Maximum Intensity

contrast.description: Set the diffuse map contrast

normal_intensity.description: Set the normal map strength intensity. 0 = Null 1 = Maximum Intensity

contrast.description: Set the diffuse map contrast

normal_intensity.description: Set the normal map strength intensity. 0 = Null 1 = Maximum Intensity

contrast.description: Set the diffuse map contrast

normal_intensity.description: Set the normal map strength intensity. 0 = Null 1 = Maximum Intensity

background_color.description: Displayed background color in case the tiling is disabled.
Displayed background color in case the tiling is disabled.

triangle_grid_color.description:
Generates a color representation of a triangulated surface out of vertices in 3D space, using a Z-down orthographic projection.

The Color Output parameter lets you select the data used for the representation, resulting in various visual styles.

The positions of the vertices may be adjusted, which impacts the generated mesh.

color_input.description:
The color image input used to map the color of the vertices, segments or triangles.

This input is used when the Color Source parameter is set to Color Input.

height.description:
The grayscale image input used to map the height – i.e., Z position – of the vertices.

The influence of this input is controlled by the Height Input Multiplier parameter.

vector_map.description:
The color image input used to map the displacement of the vertices on the X and Y axes.

The X/Y offsets are mapped to the R/G channels of the image respectively.

The influence of this input is controlled by the Vector Map Displacement parameter.

output.description: The output image.

blend_mode.description: Sets the method of blending the values of overlapping triangles. The mode lets you effectively select which of the triangles should be visible.

color_output.description:
The method of representing the triangulated surface:

- Per Vertex: a color is assigned to each vertex and interpolated across the triangle's surface
- Per Triangle: a flat color is assigned to each triangle
- Thin Line: applies an outline to the segments between vertices
- Distance to Edge: renders the distance to the closest segment on each triangle
- Center: renders the normalized distance to each triangle's barycenter

color_source.description:
Sets the method of acquiring the color – i.e., luminance – which should be assigned to the vertex, triangle or segment, depending on the selected Color Output mode:

- Height: use the height of the vertex as luminance
- Random: use a random luminance value
- Color Input: use the value sampled from the Color Input input

color_source_opacity.description:
Controls the override of the Line Color value with the values resulting from the selected Color Source.

Note

When this value is set to 1, the Line Color parameter has no impact.

distance_blend_mode.description: Sets the method of blending the values of overlapping triangles. The mode lets you effectively select which of the triangles should be visible.

distance_to_edge_thickness.description: Sets the thickness of the distance gradient. A lower value results in a shorter gradient.

height_input_multiplier.description:
Adjusts the Z position of each vertex using the values sampled from the Height input.

This offset stacks with the Height Random parameter.

height_random.description:
Applies a random offset to the Z position of each vertex.

This offset stacks with the Height Input Multiplier parameter.

non_square_expansion.description: Enables compensation of squash and stretch with non-square ratios.

quincunx_offset_x.description:
Applies the specified amount of offset to every other row of vertices, relatively to the size of their cell in the grid.

This offset stacks with the Random Position and Vector Map Displacement parameters.

quincunx_offset_y.description:
Applies the specified amount of offset to every other column of vertices, relatively to the size of their cell in the grid.

This offset stacks with the Random Position and Vector Map Displacement parameters.

random_color_seed.description: The discrete seed value used in the pseudo-random color distribution.

random_color_seed_mode.description:
The method of acquiring the seed used in the pseudo-random color distribution:

- Global Random Seed: inherit the seed from the node's graph
- Manual Seed: use a custom discrete seed

random_position.description:
Adjusts the intensity of the random offset applied to the X and Y positions of each vertex, relatively to the size of their cell in the grid.

This offset stacks with the Quincux Offset and Vector Map Displacement parameters.

random_position_multiplier.description: Adjusts the global intensity of the random offset applied to the position of each vertex.

rotation.description:
Applies the specified amount of rotation to each vertex around its base position – i.e. its position before random offset and displacement is applied.

This rotation stacks with the Rotation Disorder parameter.

rotation_disorder.description:
Applies a random amount of rotation to each vertex around its base position – i.e. its position before random offset and displacement is applied.

This rotation stacks with the Rotation parameter.

thin_line_background.description:
The color value of the background visible between the segments.

Note

When the Blend Mode is set to Max, the background will override the segments where it is brighter, as expected.

thin_line_blend_mode.description: Sets the method of blending the values of overlapping triangles. The mode lets you effectively select which of the triangles should be visible.

thin_line_color.description:
The color value of the segments.

Note

When the Color Source Opacity value is set to 1, this parameter has no impact.

triangulation.description:
Sets the triangulation method for the surface,
i.e. which pair of opposing vertices in a quad
should be connected:

- Auto: automatically selects the pair of vertices
resulting in triangles facing the least away
from the camera
- 45°: connect opposing vertices resulting in a line
turned 45 degrees relative to the X-right axis
- -45°: connect opposing vertices resulting in a line
turned -45 degrees relative to the X-right axis
- Quincux horizontal: alternate the triangulation
orientation every other row of vertices
- Quincux vertical: alternate the triangulation
orientation every other column of vertices

vector_map_displacement.description:
Adjusts the global amount of displacement applied to each vertex using the values sampled from the Vector Map input.

This offset stacks with the Random Position and Quincux Offset parameters.

x_amount.description: The amount of vertices generated on the X axis.

y_amount.description: The amount of vertices generated on the Y axis.

triangle_grid_grayscale.description:
Generates a grayscale representation of a triangulated surface out of vertices in 3D space, using a Z-down orthographic projection.

The Color Output parameter lets you select the data used for the representation, resulting in various visual styles.

The positions of the vertices may be adjusted, which impacts the generated mesh.
