This offset stacks with the Random Position and Quincux Offset parameters.

x_amount.description: The amount of vertices generated on the X axis.

y_amount.description: The amount of vertices generated on the Y axis.

triplanar_3.description:
Project the input texture using 3 planar projections generated by the position and world space normal maps.

Position map should be baked with BSphere normalization in a 16bit compatible format.

v3.0

normalization_factor.description: Improves the projection blending by restoring the loss of contrast in the blending area.

triplanar_grayscale_3.description:
Project the input texture using 3 planar projections generated by the position and world space normal maps.

Position map should be baked with BSphere normalization in a 16bit compatible format.

v3.0

normalization_factor.description: Improves the projection blending by restoring the loss of contrast in the blending area.

uv_mapper_color.description: Maps the Input color image using the coordinates provided in the UV input.

input.description: The color image which should be mapped to the coordinates provided in the UV input.

uv.description: Image coordinates encoded in the red (U) and green (V) channels of a color image.

output.description: The result of mapping the Input image using the input UV coordinates, as a color image.

background_color.description:
The background color of the output image.

The background is visible in the areas of the image where UVs are not defined (I.e., the value is (0, 0, 0, 0)).

uv_mapper_grayscale.description: Maps the Input grayscale image using the coordinates provided in the UV input.

input.description: The grayscale image which should be mapped to the coordinates provided in the UV input.

uv.description: Image coordinates encoded in the red (U) and green (V) channels of a color image.

output.description: The result of mapping the Input image using the input UV coordinates, as a grayscale image.

vector_morph.description: Distort the input image using the secondary vector field (also called flow map).

vector_morph_grayscale.description: Distort the input image using the secondary vector field (also called flow map).

vector_warp.description: Distort the input image using the secondary vector map (also called flow map).

vector_warp_grayscale.description: Distort the input image using the secondary vector map (also called flow map).

vertex_processor.description:
Applies a transformation on the vertices position of the input Paths.

The node should be used as follows:

  1. Edit the Per Vertex Function parameter function;
  2. Use Get Float2 nodes to acquire the vertex.pos, prev.pos and/or next.pos variables;
  3. Do some operations on those values (E.g., multiply them to scale the paths);
  4. Set the result of your computation as the graph's output.

Be sure to set the appropriate Previous vertices accessed and Next vertices accessed values before querying prev.pos or next.pos.

You can also add input images and sample them from the function. You must first connect an input to able to sample it from the function (Be careful, first input is Image 1!).

You can also access the prev[2].pos (Float2), next[2].pos (Float2), vertex.corner (bool) and path.id (float) variables.

For advanced users, the Paths Format Specification page in Designer's documentation explains how the data of paths is encoded into color images, and provides tips for manipulating this data directly.

See also Paths Vertex Processor Simple.

input_1.description: Inputs for images that should be sampled in the Per Vertex Function parameter function.

input_2.description: Inputs for images that should be sampled in the Per Vertex Function parameter function.

input_3.description: Inputs for images that should be sampled in the Per Vertex Function parameter function.

input_4.description: Inputs for images that should be sampled in the Per Vertex Function parameter function.

input_5.description: Inputs for images that should be sampled in the Per Vertex Function parameter function.

input_6.description: Inputs for images that should be sampled in the Per Vertex Function parameter function.

input_7.description: Inputs for images that should be sampled in the Per Vertex Function parameter function.

input_8.description: Inputs for images that should be sampled in the Per Vertex Function parameter function.

paths.description:
Contains a list of Paths which describe lists of segments, encoded in a color image.

Connect this input to the result of a Mask to Paths or to another Path-processing node.

The transformed Paths. You can use the Preview Paths node to visualize the result of the transformation.

The result can be processed using another Paths-processing node, or sent to a Paths to Spline node to further process it as Splines.

fetchNextCount.description:
Adjusts the number of adjacent vertices that are available to be located: the next vertex along the path (next.pos) and the vertex after that (next[2].pos).

The position of these vertices is then acquired using Get Float2 nodes in the Per Vertex Function parameter function.

fetchPrevCount.description:
Adjusts the number of adjacent vertices that are available to be located: the previous vertex along the path (prev.pos) and the vertex before that (prev[2].pos).

The position of these vertices is then acquired using Get Float2 nodes in the Per Vertex Function parameter function.

imageInputCount.description:
The number of visible Input # input connectors to connect images that should be sampled in the Per Vertex Function parameter function.

Once you are done setting up all the desired samples, you can hide unused pins by reducing this parameter's value back to 0.

perVertex.description:
The function applied to each vertex in the Paths input. The function must return a Float2 value for the new position of the vertex.

See the node's Description for guidance.

vertex_processor_simple.description:
Applies a transformation on the vertices position of the input Paths.

The node should be used as follows:

  1. Edit the Per Vertex Function parameter function;
  2. Use Get Float2 nodes to acquire the vertex.pos, prev.pos and/or next.pos variables;
  3. Do some operations on those values (E.g., multiply them to scale the paths);
  4. Set the result of your computation as the graph's output.

Be sure to set the appropriate Previous vertices accessed and Next vertices accessed values before querying prev.pos or next.pos.

You can also add input images and sample them from the function. You must first connect an input to able to sample it from the function (Be careful, first input is Image 1!).

You can also access the vertex.corner (bool) and path.id (float) variables.

For advanced users, the Paths Format Specification page in Designer's documentation explains how the data of paths is encoded into color images, and provides tips for manipulating this data directly.

See also Paths Vertex Processor.

input_1.description: Inputs for images that should be sampled in the Per Vertex Function parameter function.

input_2.description: Inputs for images that should be sampled in the Per Vertex Function parameter function.

input_3.description: Inputs for images that should be sampled in the Per Vertex Function parameter function.

input_4.description: Inputs for images that should be sampled in the Per Vertex Function parameter function.

Contains a list of Paths which describe lists of segments, encoded in a color image.

Connect this input to the result of a Mask to Paths or to another Path-processing node.

The transformed Paths. You can use the Preview Paths node to visualize the result of the transformation.

The result can be processed using another Paths-processing node, or sent to a Paths to Spline node to further process it as Splines.

imageInputCount.description:
The number of visible Input # input connectors to connect images that should be sampled in the Per Vertex Function parameter function.

Once you are done setting up all the desired samples, you can hide unused pins by reducing this parameter's value back to 0.

If you need more inputs, use the Paths Vertex Processor instead.

perVertex.description:
The function applied to each vertex in the Paths input. The function must return a Float2 value for the new position of the vertex.

See the node's Description for guidance.

view_color_palette.description:
Packs a color palette into a square or rectangle to visualize it more easily in the Graph View or 2D View.
The packing aims at leaving as few empty slots as possible.

The order of colors in the palette is preserved, with the colors flowing from left to right and top to bottom similarly to text wrapping.

This node may be used to visualize the palettes produced by the following nodes: Quantize Color, Create Color Palette, Modify Color Palette.

input.description: This input is hidden and is used to specify the node's output size.

palette.description:
An ordered list of RGB colors encoded as a row of pixels. The palette can hold a maximum of 256 colors.

This is the palette that the node packs and renders.

palette_color_amount.description:
The amount of colors stored in the palette.

If that number does not match the actual amount of colors in the Palette image input, the visualization may be incomplete or have more blank slots than absolutely necessary.

output.description: The visualization of the packed palette.

voronoi.description:
Generates a 3D Voronoi noise mapped to a 2D image using a Z-down orthographic projection.

Note

This noise is meant to be used with the GPU engine only (i.e., Direct3D or OpenGL). Go to Tools > Switch engine... or press the F9 key to select the desired engine.

output.description: The output image.

disorder.description: The intensity of the random offset applied to each point of the noise in the X, Y and Z axes.

distance_mode.description: Sets the method to compute the distance gradient around each point of the noise.

distance_scale.description: Adjusts the distance of the gradient around each point of the noise.

distortion_intensity.description: Controls the intensity of a warping effect applied on the 3D Voronoi noise.

distortion_scale_multiplier.description: Controls the scale of the deforming pattern used in the warping effect controlled by the Distortion Intensity.

edge_thickness.description:
Adjusts the thickness of the edges detected between cells of the 3D Voronoi noise.

Edges are detected in the X, Y and Z axes, thus some thicknesses may increase quicker than other depending on the cells' depth.

invert.description: Inverts the output image.

minkowski_number.description:
The order p of the Minkowski distance.

If we divide the distance gradient into quadrants, this number impacts these quadrants as follows:

- p is exactly 1: Straight
- p is lower than 1: Concave
- p is greater than 1: Convex

Interesting values:

1.0 - Manhattan distance
2.0 - Euclidean distance
Infinity - Chebyshev distance

non_square_expansion.description: Enables compensation of squash and stretch with non-square ratios.

offset.description: Applies an offset to the position of the 3D Voronoi noise in the X, Y and Z axes.

random_color_seed.description: The discrete random seed which should be used for the color selection per cell.

random_color_seed_mode.description:
Sets the method of acquiring the random seed for the color selection per cell:

- Global Random Seed: Use the seed inherited by the node
- Manual Seed: Use a discrete seed

rounded_curve.description: Rounds the slope around each point of the noise to make it convex.

scale.description:
Controls the scale of the 3D Voronoi noise.

Note

When Tiling is enabled on any axis, the scale adjustement is stepped. This is expected.

size.description:
Controls the size of the 3D Voronoi noise in the X, Y and Z axes. Non-uniform values result in a stretching or squashing effect.

Note

When Tiling is enabled on any axis, the size adjustment is stepped. This is expected.

style.description: Sets the method rendering the data of the 3D Voronoi noise, considering the noise is based on a set of points in 3D space: - F1: the distance to the closest point in 3D space - F2: the distance to the second closest point in 3D space - F2-F1 - F1*F2 - F1/F2 - Edge: the edge between each cell of the noise in 3D space - Random color: assign a random flat color to each cell of the noise in 3D space

voronoi_fractal.description:
Generates a fractal 3D Voronoi noise mapped to a 2D image using a Z-down orthographic projection.

Note

This noise is meant to be used with the GPU engine only (i.e., Direct3D or OpenGL). Go to Tools > Switch engine... or press the F9 key to select the desired engine.

output.description: The output image.

blend_mode.description:
Sets the method of blending together the values of overlapping cells in 3D space:

- Add: Add the values
- Max: Retain the highest value
- Min: Retain the lowest value

disorder.description: The intensity of the random offset applied to each point of the noise in the X, Y and Z axes.

distance_mode.description: Sets the method to compute the distance gradient around each point of the noise.

distance_scale.description: Adjusts the distance of the gradient around each point of the noise.

distortion_intensity.description: Controls the intensity of a warping effect applied on the 3D Voronoi noise.

distortion_scale_multiplier.description: Controls the scale of the deforming pattern used in the warping effect controlled by the Distortion Intensity.

edge_thickness.description:
Adjusts the thickness of the edges detected between cells of the 3D Voronoi noise.

Edges are detected in the X, Y and Z axes, thus some thicknesses may increase quicker than other depending on the cells' depth.

global_opacity.description: Controls the range of the fractal 3D Perlin noise values from 0.

invert.description: Inverts the output image.

lacunarity.description:
Controls how the applied fractal pattern fills space.

A higher value results in less gaps in the pattern and a denser noise.

max_level.description:
The maximum level of of repetition used in the fractal pattern.

A wider minimum/maximum range results in a richer pattern with variation on more frequency ranges.

min_level.description:
The minimum level of of repetition used in the fractal pattern.

A wider minimum/maximum range results in a richer pattern with variation on more frequency ranges.

minkowski_number.description:
The order p of the Minkowski distance.

If we divide the distance gradient into quadrants, this number impacts these quadrants as follows:

- p is exactly 1: Straight
- p is lower than 1: Concave
- p is greater than 1: Convex

Interesting values:

1.0 - Manhattan distance
2.0 - Euclidean distance
Infinity - Chebyshev distance

non_square_expansion.description: Enables compensation of squash and stretch with non-square ratios.

offset.description: Applies an offset to the position of the fractal 3D Voronoi noise in the X, Y and Z axes.

random_color_seed.description: The discrete random seed which should be used for the color selection per cell.

random_color_seed_mode.description:
Sets the method of acquiring the random seed for the color selection per cell:

- Global Random Seed: Use the seed inherited by the node
- Manual Seed: Use a discrete seed

roughness.description:
Controls the balance between low and high levels of repetition in the fractal pattern.

Note

A value of 0 results in an output which is not in line with other low values following it. This is expected.

rounded_curve.description: Rounds the slope around each point of the noise to make it convex.

scale.description:
Controls the scale of the fractal 3D Voronoi noise.

Note

When Tiling is enabled on any axis, the scale adjustement is stepped. This is expected.

size.description:
Controls the size of the fractal 3D Voronoi noise in the X, Y and Z axes. Non-uniform values result in a stretching or squashing effect.

Note

When Tiling is enabled on any axis, the size adjustment is stepped. This is expected.

style.description: Sets the method rendering the data of the 3D Voronoi noise, considering the noise is based on a set of points in 3D space: - F1: the distance to the closest point in 3D space - F2: the distance to the second closest point in 3D space - F2-F1 - F1*F2 - F1/F2 - Edge: the edge between each cell of the noise in 3D space - Random color: assign a random flat color to each cell of the noise in 3D space

water_level.description: Adds a customizable water level to create puddles and wet surfaces.

mask.description: Allows to remove water from specific parts of the material. White areas will not receive water.

waveform_01_v2.description: A horizontal arrangement of user-selected patterns stacked into a shape akin to a waveform.

Waveform.description: The generated waveform as a grayscale bitmap.

amplitude_max.description: The maximum value (or thickness) of the waveform.

amplitude_min.description: The minimum value (or thickness) of the waveform.

custom_function.description:
Computes the vertical size of the pattern placed at each sample.

Available variables:
-pos (float) The position of the pattern on the X axis. This may be used to select patterns.

disorder.description:
Displaces the values of the waveform.

This can be used to animate it.

disorder_speed.description:
Adjusts the distance of displacement applied by the Disorder parameter.

This can be used to control the speed of displacement when animating the waveform.

function.description:
The function used to draw the waveform.

This controls the vertical size of the pattern placed at each sample:

- Value noise: A random distribution of values
- Cosine: Values follow the progression of a cosine function
- Custom function: Use a user-authored function to drive the values

noise.description: Applies noise to the waveform which randomly subtracts from its vertical span.

pattern.description: The pattern placed at each sample of the waveform.

pattern_variation.description: An additional adjustment available for some patterns.

position.description:
The position of the waveform in the image:

- Centered: The origin is at the vertical center of the image
- Bottom: The origin is a the bottom of the image

roughness.description:
Interpolates between a clean and smooth waveform with one that is more rough and evenly distributed.

This can be thought of as clean signal vs. white noise.

samples.description: The amount of patterns placed along the X axis to draw the waveform, where a lower value results in a more stepped look.

scale.description: The horizontal span of the waveform visible in the image.

white_noise_v2.description: Generates a white noise using one of three methods targeting different histogram shapes: uniform, Gaussian and triangle.

output.description: The generated noise as a grayscale bitmap.

disorder.description:
Displaces the ingredients of the noise.

This can be used to animate the noise.

disorder_speed.description:
Adjusts the distance of displacement applied by the Disorder parameter.

This can be used to control the speed of displacement when animating the noise.

noise_distribution.description:
The method of distributing the ingredients to target a histogram shape:

- Uniform: A flat histogram.
- Gaussian: A histogram representing a normal distribution, akin to a bell curve.
- Triangle: A triangular histogram.

normal_intensity.description: Set the normal map strength intensity. 0 = Null 1 = Maximum Intensity
